Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Genesis1:2 The earth was formless and void
Genesis1:3 Then God said
Genesis1:4 God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
Genesis1:5 God called the light day
Genesis1:6 Then God said
Genesis1:7 God made the expanse
Genesis1:8 God called the expanse heaven. And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:9 Then God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 The earth brought forth vegetation
Genesis1:13 There was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:14 Then God said
Genesis1:15 and let them be for lights in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth; and it was so.
Genesis1:16 God made the two great lights
Genesis1:17 God placed them in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth
Genesis1:18 and to govern the day and the night
Genesis1:19 There was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:20 Then God said
Genesis1:21 God created the great sea monsters and every living creature that moves
Genesis1:22 God blessed them
Genesis1:23 There was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:24 Then God said
Genesis1:25 God made the beasts of the earth after their kind
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 God created man in His own image
Genesis1:28 God blessed them; and God said to them
Genesis1:29 Then God said
Genesis1:30 and to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the sky and to every thing that moves on the earth which has life
Genesis1:31 God saw all that He had made
Genesis2:1 Thus the heavens and the earth were completed
Genesis2:2 By the seventh day God completed His work which He had done
Genesis2:3 Then God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it
Genesis2:4 This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created
Genesis2:5 Now no shrub of the field was yet in the earth
Genesis2:6 But a mist used to rise from the earth and water the whole surface of the ground.
Genesis2:7 Then the LORD God formed man of dust from the ground
Genesis2:8 The LORD God planted a garden toward the east
Genesis2:9 Out of the ground the LORD God caused to grow every tree that is pleasing to the sight and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden
Genesis2:10 Now a river flowed out of Eden to water the garden; and from there it divided and became four rivers.
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is Pishon; it flows around the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 The gold of that land is good; the bdellium and the onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is Gihon; it flows around the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The name of the third river is Tigris; it flows east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 Then the LORD God took the man and put him into the garden of Eden to cultivate it and keep it.
Genesis2:16 The LORD God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 but from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat
Genesis2:18 Then the LORD God said
Genesis2:19 Out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the sky
Genesis2:20 The man gave names to all the cattle
Genesis2:21 So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man
Genesis2:22 The LORD God fashioned into a woman the rib which He had taken from the man
Genesis2:23 The man said
Genesis2:24 For this reason a man shall leave his father and his mother
Genesis2:25 And the man and his wife were both naked and were not ashamed.

Genesis5:8 So all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years

Genesis5:11 So all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years

Genesis5:28 Lamech lived one hundred and eighty-two years
Genesis5:29 Now he called his name Noah
Genesis5:30 Then Lamech lived five hundred and ninety-five years after he became the father of Noah
Genesis5:31 So all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years
Genesis5:32 Noah was five hundred years old

Exodus21:13 But if he did not lie in wait for him
Exodus21:14 If

Exodus21:16 He who kidnaps a man
Exodus21:17 He who curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.

Exodus21:29 If

Exodus22:1 If a man steals an ox or a sheep and slaughters it or sells it
Exodus22:2 If the thief is caught while breaking in and is struck so that he dies
Exodus22:3 But if the sun has risen on him
Exodus22:4 If what he stole is actually found alive in his possession
Exodus22:5 If a man lets a field or vineyard be grazed bare and lets his animal loose so that it grazes in another man's field
Exodus22:6 If a fire breaks out and spreads to thorn bushes

Exodus22:28 You shall not curse God
Exodus22:29 You shall not delay the offering from your harvest and your vintage. The firstborn of your sons you shall give to Me.

Exodus23:1 You shall not bear a false report; do not join your hand with a wicked man to be a malicious witness.
Exodus23:2 You shall not follow the masses in doing evil
Exodus23:3 nor shall you be partial to a poor man in his dispute.
Exodus23:4 If you meet your enemy's ox or his donkey wandering away
Exodus23:5 If you see the donkey of one who hates you lying helpless under its load
Exodus23:6 You shall not pervert the justice due to your needy brother in his dispute.
Exodus23:7 Keep far from a false charge
Exodus23:8 You shall not take a bribe
Exodus23:9 You shall not oppress a stranger
Exodus23:10 You shall sow your land for six years and gather in its yield
Exodus23:11 but on the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow
Exodus23:12 Six days you are to do your work
Exodus23:13 Now concerning everything which I have said to you
Exodus23:14 Three times a year you shall celebrate a feast to Me.
Exodus23:15 You shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread; for seven days you are to eat unleavened bread
Exodus23:16 Also you shall observe the Feast of the Harvest of the first fruits of your labors from what you sow in the field; also the Feast of the Ingathering at the end of the year when you gather in the fruit of your labors from the field.
Exodus23:17 Three times a year all your males shall appear before the Lord GOD.
Exodus23:18 You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leavened bread; nor is the fat of My feast to remain overnight until morning.
Exodus23:19 You shall bring the choice first fruits of your soil into the house of the LORD your God. You are not to boil a young goat in the milk of its mother.
Exodus23:20 Behold
Exodus23:21 Be on your guard before him and obey his voice; do not be rebellious toward him
Exodus23:22 But if you truly obey his voice and do all that I say
Exodus23:23 For My angel will go before you and bring you in to the land of the Amorites
Exodus23:24 You shall not worship their gods
Exodus23:25 But you shall serve the LORD your God
Exodus23:26 There shall be no one miscarrying or barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
Exodus23:27 I will send My terror ahead of you
Exodus23:28 I will send hornets ahead of you so that they will drive out the Hivites
Exodus23:29 I will not drive them out before you in a single year
Exodus23:30 I will drive them out before you little by little
Exodus23:31 I will fix your boundary from the Red Sea to the sea of the Philistines
Exodus23:32 You shall make no covenant with them or with their gods.
Exodus23:33 They shall not live in your land

Exodus26:1 Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet material; you shall make them with cherubim
Exodus26:2 The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits
Exodus26:3 Five curtains shall be joined to one another
Exodus26:4 You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set
Exodus26:5 You shall make fifty loops in the one curtain
Exodus26:6 You shall make fifty clasps of gold
Exodus26:7 Then you shall make curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle; you shall make eleven curtains in all.
Exodus26:8 The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits
Exodus26:9 You shall join five curtains by themselves and the other six curtains by themselves
Exodus26:10 You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the first set
Exodus26:11 You shall make fifty clasps of bronze
Exodus26:12 The overlapping part that is left over in the curtains of the tent
Exodus26:13 The cubit on one side and the cubit on the other
Exodus26:14 You shall make a covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red and a covering of porpoise skins above.
Exodus26:15 Then you shall make the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood
Exodus26:16 Ten cubits shall be the length of each board and one and a half cubits the width of each board.
Exodus26:17 There shall be two tenons for each board
Exodus26:18 You shall make the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side.
Exodus26:19 You shall make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards
Exodus26:20 and for the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:21 and their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board.
Exodus26:22 For the rear of the tabernacle
Exodus26:23 You shall make two boards for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear.
Exodus26:24 They shall be double beneath
Exodus26:25 There shall be eight boards with their sockets of silver
Exodus26:26 Then you shall make bars of acacia wood
Exodus26:27 and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:28 The middle bar in the center of the boards shall pass through from end to end.
Exodus26:29 You shall overlay the boards with gold and make their rings of gold as holders for the bars; and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
Exodus26:30 Then you shall erect the tabernacle according to its plan which you have been shown in the mountain.
Exodus26:31 You shall make a veil of blue and purple and scarlet material and fine twisted linen; it shall be made with cherubim
Exodus26:32 You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold
Exodus26:33 You shall hang up the veil under the clasps
Exodus26:34 You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the holy of holies.
Exodus26:35 You shall set the table outside the veil
Exodus26:36 You shall make a screen for the doorway of the tent of blue and purple and scarlet material and fine twisted linen
Exodus26:37 You shall make five pillars of acacia for the screen and overlay them with gold
Exodus27:1 And you shall make the altar of acacia wood
Exodus27:2 You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it
Exodus27:3 You shall make its pails for removing its ashes
Exodus27:4 You shall make for it a grating of network of bronze
Exodus27:5 You shall put it beneath
Exodus27:6 You shall make poles for the altar
Exodus27:7 Its poles shall be inserted into the rings
Exodus27:8 You shall make it hollow with planks; as it was shown to you in the mountain
Exodus27:9 You shall make the court of the tabernacle. On the south side there shall be hangings for the court of fine twisted linen one hundred cubits long for one side;
Exodus27:10 and its pillars shall be twenty
Exodus27:11 Likewise for the north side in length there shall be hangings one hundred cubits long
Exodus27:12 For the width of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits with their ten pillars and their ten sockets.
Exodus27:13 The width of the court on the east side shall be fifty cubits.
Exodus27:14 The hangings for the one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits with their three pillars and their three sockets.
Exodus27:15 And for the other side shall be hangings of fifteen cubits with their three pillars and their three sockets.
Exodus27:16 For the gate of the court there shall be a screen of twenty cubits
Exodus27:17 All the pillars around the court shall be furnished with silver bands with their hooks of silver and their sockets of bronze.
Exodus27:18 The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits
Exodus27:19 All the utensils of the tabernacle used in all its service
Exodus27:20 You shall charge the sons of Israel
Exodus27:21 In the tent of meeting
Exodus28:1 Then bring near to yourself Aaron your brother
Exodus28:2 You shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother
Exodus28:3 You shall speak to all the skillful persons whom I have endowed with the spirit of wisdom
Exodus28:4 These are the garments which they shall make: a breastpiece and an ephod and a robe and a tunic of checkered work
Exodus28:5 They shall take the gold and the blue and the purple and the scarlet material and the fine linen.
Exodus28:6 They shall also make the ephod of gold
Exodus28:7 It shall have two shoulder pieces joined to its two ends
Exodus28:8 The skillfully woven band
Exodus28:9 You shall take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel
Exodus28:10 six of their names on the one stone and the names of the remaining six on the other stone
Exodus28:11 As a jeweler engraves a signet
Exodus28:12 You shall put the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus28:13 You shall make filigree settings of gold
Exodus28:14 and two chains of pure gold; you shall make them of twisted cordage work
Exodus28:15 You shall make a breastpiece of judgment
Exodus28:16 It shall be square and folded double
Exodus28:17 You shall mount on it four rows of stones; the first row shall be a row of ruby
Exodus28:18 and the second row a turquoise
Exodus28:19 and the third row a jacinth
Exodus28:20 and the fourth row a beryl and an onyx and a jasper; they shall be set in gold filigree.
Exodus28:21 The stones shall be according to the names of the sons of Israel: twelve
Exodus28:22 You shall make on the breastpiece chains of twisted cordage work in pure gold.
Exodus28:23 You shall make on the breastpiece two rings of gold
Exodus28:24 You shall put the two cords of gold on the two rings at the ends of the breastpiece.
Exodus28:25 You shall put the other two ends of the two cords on the two filigree settings
Exodus28:26 You shall make two rings of gold and shall place them on the two ends of the breastpiece
Exodus28:27 You shall make two rings of gold and put them on the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus28:28 They shall bind the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord
Exodus28:29 Aaron shall carry the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment over his heart when he enters the holy place
Exodus28:30 You shall put in the breastpiece of judgment the Urim and the Thummim
Exodus28:31 You shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
Exodus28:32 There shall be an opening at its top in the middle of it; around its opening there shall be a binding of woven work
Exodus28:33 You shall make on its hem pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet material
Exodus28:34 a golden bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 It shall be on Aaron when he ministers; and its tinkling shall be heard when he enters and leaves the holy place before the LORD
Exodus28:36 You shall also make a plate of pure gold and shall engrave on it
Exodus28:37 You shall fasten it on a blue cord
Exodus28:38 It shall be on Aaron's forehead
Exodus28:39 You shall weave the tunic of checkered work of fine linen
Exodus28:40 For Aaron's sons you shall make tunics; you shall also make sashes for them
Exodus28:41 You shall put them on Aaron your brother and on his sons with him; and you shall anoint them and ordain them and consecrate them
Exodus28:42 You shall make for them linen breeches to cover their bare flesh; they shall reach from the loins even to the thighs.
Exodus28:43 They shall be on Aaron and on his sons when they enter the tent of meeting
Exodus29:1 Now this is what you shall do to them to consecrate them to minister as priests to Me: take one young bull and two rams without blemish
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread and unleavened cakes mixed with oil
Exodus29:3 You shall put them in one basket
Exodus29:4 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the doorway of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus29:5 You shall take the garments
Exodus29:6 and you shall set the turban on his head and put the holy crown on the turban.
Exodus29:7 Then you shall take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him.
Exodus29:8 You shall bring his sons and put tunics on them.
Exodus29:9 You shall gird them with sashes
Exodus29:10 Then you shall bring the bull before the tent of meeting
Exodus29:11 You shall slaughter the bull before the LORD at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Exodus29:12 You shall take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger; and you shall pour out all the blood at the base of the altar.
Exodus29:13 You shall take all the fat that covers the entrails and the lobe of the liver
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the bull and its hide and its refuse
Exodus29:15 You shall also take the one ram
Exodus29:16 and you shall slaughter the ram and shall take its blood and sprinkle it around on the altar.
Exodus29:17 Then you shall cut the ram into its pieces
Exodus29:18 You shall offer up in smoke the whole ram on the altar; it is a burnt offering to the LORD: it is a soothing aroma
Exodus29:19 Then you shall take the other ram
Exodus29:20 You shall slaughter the ram
Exodus29:21 Then you shall take some of the blood that is on the altar and some of the anointing oil
Exodus29:22 You shall also take the fat from the ram and the fat tail
Exodus29:23 and one cake of bread and one cake of bread mixed with oil and one wafer from the basket of unleavened bread which is set before the LORD;
Exodus29:24 and you shall put all these in the hands of Aaron and in the hands of his sons
Exodus29:25 You shall take them from their hands
Exodus29:26 Then you shall take the breast of Aaron's ram of ordination
Exodus29:27 You shall consecrate the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering which was waved and which was offered from the ram of ordination
Exodus29:28 It shall be for Aaron and his sons as their portion forever from the sons of Israel
Exodus29:29 The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him
Exodus29:30 For seven days the one of his sons who is priest in his stead shall put them on when he enters the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.
Exodus29:31 You shall take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket
Exodus29:33 Thus they shall eat those things by which atonement was made at their ordination and consecration; but a layman shall not eat them
Exodus29:34 If any of the flesh of ordination or any of the bread remains until morning
Exodus29:35 Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons
Exodus29:36 Each day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement
Exodus29:37 For seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it; then the altar shall be most holy
Exodus29:38 Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two one year old lambs each day
Exodus29:39 The one lamb you shall offer in the morning and the other lamb you shall offer at twilight;
Exodus29:40 and there shall be one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil
Exodus29:41 The other lamb you shall offer at twilight
Exodus29:42 It shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the doorway of the tent of meeting before the LORD
Exodus29:43 I will meet there with the sons of Israel
Exodus29:44 I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; I will also consecrate Aaron and his sons to minister as priests to Me.
Exodus29:45 I will dwell among the sons of Israel and will be their God.
Exodus29:46 They shall know that I am the LORD their God who brought them out of the land of Egypt
Exodus30:1 Moreover
Exodus30:2 Its length shall be a cubit
Exodus30:3 You shall overlay it with pure gold
Exodus30:4 You shall make two gold rings for it under its molding; you shall make them on its two side walls--on opposite sides--and they shall be holders for poles with which to carry it.
Exodus30:5 You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus30:6 You shall put this altar in front of the veil that is near the ark of the testimony
Exodus30:7 Aaron shall burn fragrant incense on it; he shall burn it every morning when he trims the lamps.
Exodus30:8 When Aaron trims the lamps at twilight
Exodus30:9 You shall not offer any strange incense on this altar
Exodus30:10 Aaron shall make atonement on its horns once a year; he shall make atonement on it with the blood of the sin offering of atonement once a year throughout your generations. It is most holy to the LORD.
Exodus30:11 The LORD also spoke to Moses
Exodus30:12 When you take a census of the sons of Israel to number them

Exodus30:15 The rich shall not pay more and the poor shall not pay less than the half shekel

Exodus34:1 Now the LORD said to Moses
Exodus34:2 So be ready by morning
Exodus34:3 No man is to come up with you
Exodus34:4 So he cut out two stone tablets like the former ones
Exodus34:5 The LORD descended in the cloud and stood there with him as he called upon the name of the LORD.
Exodus34:6 Then the LORD passed by in front of him and proclaimed
Exodus34:7 who keeps lovingkindness for thousands
Exodus34:8 Moses made haste to bow low toward the earth and worship.
Exodus34:9 He said
Exodus34:10 Then God said
Exodus34:11 Be sure to observe what I am commanding you this day: behold
Exodus34:12 Watch yourself that you make no covenant with the inhabitants of the land into which you are going
Exodus34:13 But rather
Exodus34:14 --for you shall not worship any other god
Exodus34:15 otherwise you might make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land and they would play the harlot with their gods and sacrifice to their gods
Exodus34:16 and you might take some of his daughters for your sons
Exodus34:17 You shall make for yourself no molten gods.
Exodus34:18 You shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you are to eat unleavened bread
Exodus34:19 The first offspring from every womb belongs to Me
Exodus34:20 You shall redeem with a lamb the first offspring from a donkey; and if you do not redeem it
Exodus34:21 You shall work six days
Exodus34:22 You shall celebrate the Feast of Weeks
Exodus34:23 Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord GOD
Exodus34:24 For I will drive out nations before you and enlarge your borders
Exodus34:25 You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leavened bread
Exodus34:26 You shall bring the very first of the first fruits of your soil into the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
Exodus34:27 Then the LORD said to Moses
Exodus34:28 So he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he did not eat bread or drink water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant

Exodus36:1 Now Bezalel and Oholiab

Exodus36:4 And all the skillful men who were performing all the work of the sanctuary came
Exodus36:5 and they said to Moses
Exodus36:6 So Moses issued a command
Exodus36:7 For the material they had was sufficient and more than enough for all the work
Exodus36:8 All the skillful men among those who were performing the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains; of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet material
Exodus36:9 The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains had the same measurements.
Exodus36:10 He joined five curtains to one another and the other five curtains he joined to one another.
Exodus36:11 He made loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set; he did likewise on the edge of the curtain that was outermost in the second set.
Exodus36:12 He made fifty loops in the one curtain and he made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that was in the second set; the loops were opposite each other.
Exodus36:13 He made fifty clasps of gold and joined the curtains to one another with the clasps
Exodus36:14 Then he made curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he made eleven curtains in all.
Exodus36:15 The length of each curtain was thirty cubits and four cubits the width of each curtain; the eleven curtains had the same measurements.
Exodus36:16 He joined five curtains by themselves and the other six curtains by themselves.
Exodus36:17 Moreover
Exodus36:18 He made fifty clasps of bronze to join the tent together so that it would be a unit.
Exodus36:19 He made a covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red
Exodus36:20 Then he made the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood
Exodus36:21 Ten cubits was the length of each board and one and a half cubits the width of each board.

Exodus36:23 He made the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side;

Exodus38:25 The silver of those of the congregation who were numbered was 100 talents and 1
Exodus38:26 a beka a head (that is
Exodus38:27 The hundred talents of silver were for casting the sockets of the sanctuary and the sockets of the veil; one hundred sockets for the hundred talents
Exodus38:28 Of the 1
Exodus38:29 The bronze of the wave offering was 70 talents and 2
Exodus38:30 With it he made the sockets to the doorway of the tent of meeting
Exodus38:31 and the sockets of the court all around and the sockets of the gate of the court
Exodus39:1 Moreover
Exodus39:2 He made the ephod of gold
Exodus39:3 Then they hammered out gold sheets and cut them into threads to be woven in with the blue and the purple and the scarlet material
Exodus39:4 They made attaching shoulder pieces for the ephod; it was attached at its two upper ends.
Exodus39:5 The skillfully woven band which was on it was like its workmanship
Exodus39:6 They made the onyx stones
Exodus39:7 And he placed them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus39:8 He made the breastpiece
Exodus39:9 It was square; they made the breastpiece folded double
Exodus39:10 And they mounted four rows of stones on it. The first row was a row of ruby
Exodus39:11 and the second row
Exodus39:12 and the third row
Exodus39:13 and the fourth row
Exodus39:14 The stones were corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel; they were twelve
Exodus39:15 They made on the breastpiece chains like cords
Exodus39:16 They made two gold filigree settings and two gold rings
Exodus39:17 Then they put the two gold cords in the two rings at the ends of the breastpiece.
Exodus39:18 They put the other two ends of the two cords on the two filigree settings
Exodus39:19 They made two gold rings and placed them on the two ends of the breastpiece
Exodus39:20 Furthermore
Exodus39:21 They bound the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord
Exodus39:22 Then he made the robe of the ephod of woven work
Exodus39:23 and the opening of the robe was at the top in the center
Exodus39:24 They made pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet material and twisted linen on the hem of the robe.
Exodus39:25 They also made bells of pure gold
Exodus39:26 alternating a bell and a pomegranate all around on the hem of the robe for the service
Exodus39:27 They made the tunics of finely woven linen for Aaron and his sons
Exodus39:28 and the turban of fine linen
Exodus39:29 and the sash of fine twisted linen
Exodus39:30 They made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold
Exodus39:31 They fastened a blue cord to it
Exodus39:32 Thus all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was completed; and the sons of Israel did according to all that the LORD had commanded Moses; so they did.
Exodus39:33 They brought the tabernacle to Moses
Exodus39:34 and the covering of rams' skins dyed red
Exodus39:35 the ark of the testimony and its poles and the mercy seat;
Exodus39:36 the table
Exodus39:37 the pure gold lampstand
Exodus39:38 and the gold altar
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar and its bronze grating
Exodus39:40 the hangings for the court
Exodus39:41 the woven garments for ministering in the holy place and the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments of his sons
Exodus39:42 So the sons of Israel did all the work according to all that the LORD had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:43 And Moses examined all the work and behold
Exodus40:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Exodus40:2 On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:3 You shall place the ark of the testimony there
Exodus40:4 You shall bring in the table and arrange what belongs on it; and you shall bring in the lampstand and mount its lamps.
Exodus40:5 Moreover
Exodus40:6 You shall set the altar of burnt offering in front of the doorway of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:7 You shall set the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it.
Exodus40:8 You shall set up the court all around and hang up the veil for the gateway of the court.
Exodus40:9 Then you shall take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it
Exodus40:10 You shall anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils
Exodus40:11 You shall anoint the laver and its stand
Exodus40:12 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the doorway of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus40:13 You shall put the holy garments on Aaron and anoint him and consecrate him
Exodus40:14 You shall bring his sons and put tunics on them;
Exodus40:15 and you shall anoint them even as you have anointed their father
Exodus40:16 Thus Moses did; according to all that the LORD had commanded him
Exodus40:17 Now in the first month of the second year
Exodus40:18 Moses erected the tabernacle and laid its sockets
Exodus40:19 He spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent on top of it
Exodus40:20 Then he took the testimony and put it into the ark
Exodus40:21 He brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 Then he put the table in the tent of meeting on the north side of the tabernacle
Exodus40:23 He set the arrangement of bread in order on it before the LORD
Exodus40:24 Then he placed the lampstand in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:25 He lighted the lamps before the LORD
Exodus40:26 Then he placed the gold altar in the tent of meeting in front of the veil;
Exodus40:27 and he burned fragrant incense on it
Exodus40:28 Then he set up the veil for the doorway of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 He set the altar of burnt offering before the doorway of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:30 He placed the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
Exodus40:31 From it Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
Exodus40:32 When they entered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:33 He erected the court all around the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud had settled on it
Exodus40:36 Throughout all their journeys whenever the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle
Exodus40:37 but if the cloud was not taken up
Exodus40:38 For throughout all their journeys
Leviticus1:1 Then the LORD called to Moses and spoke to him from the tent of meeting
Leviticus1:2 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus1:3 'If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd
Leviticus1:4 'He shall lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 'He shall slay the young bull before the LORD; and Aaron's sons the priests shall offer up the blood and sprinkle the blood around on the altar that is at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus1:6 'He shall then skin the burnt offering and cut it into its pieces.
Leviticus1:7 'The sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire.
Leviticus1:8 'Then Aaron's sons the priests shall arrange the pieces
Leviticus1:9 'Its entrails
Leviticus1:10 'But if his offering is from the flock
Leviticus1:11 'He shall slay it on the side of the altar northward before the LORD
Leviticus1:12 'He shall then cut it into its pieces with its head and its suet
Leviticus1:13 'The entrails
Leviticus1:14 'But if his offering to the LORD is a burnt offering of birds
Leviticus1:15 'The priest shall bring it to the altar
Leviticus1:16 'He shall also take away its crop with its feathers and cast it beside the altar eastward
Leviticus1:17 'Then he shall tear it by its wings
Leviticus2:1 'Now when anyone presents a grain offering as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus2:2 'He shall then bring it to Aaron's sons the priests; and shall take from it his handful of its fine flour and of its oil with all of its frankincense. And the priest shall offer it up in smoke as its memorial portion on the altar
Leviticus2:3 'The remainder of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons: a thing most holy
Leviticus2:4 'Now when you bring an offering of a grain offering baked in an oven
Leviticus2:5 'If your offering is a grain offering made on the griddle
Leviticus2:6 you shall break it into bits and pour oil on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 'Now if your offering is a grain offering made in a pan
Leviticus2:8 'When you bring in the grain offering which is made of these things to the LORD
Leviticus2:9 'The priest then shall take up from the grain offering its memorial portion
Leviticus2:10 'The remainder of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons: a thing most holy of the offerings to the LORD by fire.
Leviticus2:11 'No grain offering
Leviticus2:12 'As an offering of first fruits you shall bring them to the LORD
Leviticus2:13 'Every grain offering of yours
Leviticus2:14 'Also if you bring a grain offering of early ripened things to the LORD
Leviticus2:15 'You shall then put oil on it and lay incense on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 'The priest shall offer up in smoke its memorial portion
Leviticus3:1 'Now if his offering is a sacrifice of peace offerings
Leviticus3:2 'He shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and slay it at the doorway of the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:3 'From the sacrifice of the peace offerings he shall present an offering by fire to the LORD
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus3:5 'Then Aaron's sons shall offer it up in smoke on the altar on the burnt offering
Leviticus3:6 'But if his offering for a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD is from the flock
Leviticus3:7 'If he is going to offer a lamb for his offering
Leviticus3:8 and he shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and slay it before the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:9 'From the sacrifice of peace offerings he shall bring as an offering by fire to the LORD
Leviticus3:10 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus3:11 'Then the priest shall offer it up in smoke on the altar as food
Leviticus3:12 'Moreover
Leviticus3:13 and he shall lay his hand on its head and slay it before the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:14 'From it he shall present his offering as an offering by fire to the LORD
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus3:16 'The priest shall offer them up in smoke on the altar as food
Leviticus3:17 'It is a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings: you shall not eat any fat or any blood.'
Leviticus4:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus4:2 Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus4:3 if the anointed priest sins so as to bring guilt on the people
Leviticus4:4 'He shall bring the bull to the doorway of the tent of meeting before the LORD
Leviticus4:5 'Then the anointed priest is to take some of the blood of the bull and bring it to the tent of meeting
Leviticus4:6 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before the LORD
Leviticus4:7 'The priest shall also put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense which is before the LORD in the tent of meeting; and all the blood of the bull he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering which is at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:8 'He shall remove from it all the fat of the bull of the sin offering: the fat that covers the entrails
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus4:10 (just as it is removed from the ox of the sacrifice of peace offerings)
Leviticus4:11 'But the hide of the bull and all its flesh with its head and its legs and its entrails and its refuse
Leviticus4:12 that is
Leviticus4:13 'Now if the whole congregation of Israel commits error and the matter escapes the notice of the assembly
Leviticus4:14 when the sin which they have committed becomes known
Leviticus4:15 'Then the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the LORD
Leviticus4:16 'Then the anointed priest is to bring some of the blood of the bull to the tent of meeting;
Leviticus4:17 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD
Leviticus4:18 'He shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar which is before the LORD in the tent of meeting; and all the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering which is at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:19 'He shall remove all its fat from it and offer it up in smoke on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 'He shall also do with the bull just as he did with the bull of the sin offering; thus he shall do with it. So the priest shall make atonement for them
Leviticus4:21 'Then he is to bring out the bull to a place outside the camp and burn it as he burned the first bull; it is the sin offering for the assembly.
Leviticus4:22 'When a leader sins and unintentionally does any one of all the things which the LORD his God has commanded not to be done
Leviticus4:23 if his sin which he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:24 'He shall lay his hand on the head of the male goat and slay it in the place where they slay the burnt offering before the LORD; it is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 'Then the priest is to take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering; and the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:26 'All its fat he shall offer up in smoke on the altar as in the case of the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings. Thus the priest shall make atonement for him in regard to his sin
Leviticus4:27 'Now if anyone of the common people sins unintentionally in doing any of the things which the LORD has commanded not to be done
Leviticus4:28 if his sin which he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:29 'He shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slay the sin offering at the place of the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:30 'The priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering; and all the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:31 'Then he shall remove all its fat
Leviticus4:32 'But if he brings a lamb as his offering for a sin offering
Leviticus4:33 'He shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slay it for a sin offering in the place where they slay the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:34 'The priest is to take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:35 'Then he shall remove all its fat
Leviticus5:1 'Now if a person sins after he hears a public adjuration to testify when he is a witness
Leviticus5:2 'Or if a person touches any unclean thing
Leviticus5:3 'Or if he touches human uncleanness
Leviticus5:4 'Or if a person swears thoughtlessly with his lips to do evil or to do good
Leviticus5:5 'So it shall be when he becomes guilty in one of these
Leviticus5:6 'He shall also bring his guilt offering to the LORD for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:7 'But if he cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus5:8 'He shall bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 'He shall also sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 'The second he shall then prepare as a burnt offering according to the ordinance. So the priest shall make atonement on his behalf for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:11 'But if his means are insufficient for two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus5:12 'He shall bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 'So the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin which he has committed from one of these
Leviticus5:14 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus5:15 If a person acts unfaithfully and sins unintentionally against the LORD'S holy things
Leviticus5:16 He shall make restitution for that which he has sinned against the holy thing
Leviticus5:17 Now if a person sins and does any of the things which the LORD has commanded not to be done
Leviticus5:18 He is then to bring to the priest a ram without defect from the flock
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering; he was certainly guilty before the LORD.
Leviticus6:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:2 When a person sins and acts unfaithfully against the LORD
Leviticus6:3 or has found what was lost and lied about it and sworn falsely
Leviticus6:4 then it shall be
Leviticus6:5 or anything about which he swore falsely; he shall make restitution for it in full and add to it one-fifth more. He shall give it to the one to whom it belongs on the day he presents his guilt offering.
Leviticus6:6 Then he shall bring to the priest his guilt offering to the LORD
Leviticus6:7 and the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD
Leviticus6:8 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:9 Command Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:10 'The priest is to put on his linen robe
Leviticus6:11 'Then he shall take off his garments and put on other garments
Leviticus6:12 'The fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it. It shall not go out
Leviticus6:13 'Fire shall be kept burning continually on the altar; it is not to go out.
Leviticus6:14 'Now this is the law of the grain offering: the sons of Aaron shall present it before the LORD in front of the altar.
Leviticus6:15 'Then one of them shall lift up from it a handful of the fine flour of the grain offering
Leviticus6:16 'What is left of it Aaron and his sons are to eat. It shall be eaten as unleavened cakes in a holy place; they are to eat it in the court of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus6:17 'It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their share from My offerings by fire; it is most holy
Leviticus6:18 'Every male among the sons of Aaron may eat it; it is a permanent ordinance throughout your generations
Leviticus6:19 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:20 This is the offering which Aaron and his sons are to present to the LORD on the day when he is anointed; the tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering
Leviticus6:21 It shall be prepared with oil on a griddle. When it is well stirred
Leviticus6:22 The anointed priest who will be in his place among his sons shall offer it. By a permanent ordinance it shall be entirely offered up in smoke to the LORD.
Leviticus6:23 So every grain offering of the priest shall be burned entirely. It shall not be eaten.
Leviticus6:24 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:25 Speak to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus6:26 'The priest who offers it for sin shall eat it. It shall be eaten in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 'Anyone who touches its flesh will become consecrated; and when any of its blood splashes on a garment
Leviticus6:28 'Also the earthenware vessel in which it was boiled shall be broken; and if it was boiled in a bronze vessel
Leviticus6:29 'Every male among the priests may eat of it; it is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 'But no sin offering of which any of the blood is brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the holy place shall be eaten; it shall be burned with fire.
Leviticus7:1 'Now this is the law of the guilt offering; it is most holy.
Leviticus7:2 'In the place where they slay the burnt offering they are to slay the guilt offering
Leviticus7:3 'Then he shall offer from it all its fat: the fat tail and the fat that covers the entrails
Leviticus7:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus7:5 'The priest shall offer them up in smoke on the altar as an offering by fire to the LORD; it is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 'Every male among the priests may eat of it. It shall be eaten in a holy place; it is most holy.
Leviticus7:7 'The guilt offering is like the sin offering
Leviticus7:8 'Also the priest who presents any man's burnt offering
Leviticus7:9 'Likewise
Leviticus7:10 'Every grain offering
Leviticus7:11 'Now this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings which shall be presented to the LORD.
Leviticus7:12 'If he offers it by way of thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 'With the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thanksgiving
Leviticus7:14 'Of this he shall present one of every offering as a contribution to the LORD; it shall belong to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the peace offerings.
Leviticus7:15 'Now as for the flesh of the sacrifice of his thanksgiving peace offerings
Leviticus7:16 'But if the sacrifice of his offering is a votive or a freewill offering
Leviticus7:17 but what is left over from the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall be burned with fire.
Leviticus7:18 'So if any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings should ever be eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 'Also the flesh that touches anything unclean shall not be eaten; it shall be burned with fire. As for other flesh
Leviticus7:20 'But the person who eats the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings which belong to the LORD
Leviticus7:21 'When anyone touches anything unclean
Leviticus7:22 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:23 Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus7:24 'Also the fat of an animal which dies and the fat of an animal torn by beasts may be put to any other use
Leviticus7:25 'For whoever eats the fat of the animal from which an offering by fire is offered to the LORD
Leviticus7:26 'You are not to eat any blood
Leviticus7:27 'Any person who eats any blood
Leviticus7:28 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:29 Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus7:30 'His own hands are to bring offerings by fire to the LORD. He shall bring the fat with the breast
Leviticus7:31 'The priest shall offer up the fat in smoke on the altar
Leviticus7:32 'You shall give the right thigh to the priest as a contribution from the sacrifices of your peace offerings.
Leviticus7:33 'The one among the sons of Aaron who offers the blood of the peace offerings and the fat
Leviticus7:34 'For I have taken the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution from the sons of Israel from the sacrifices of their peace offerings
Leviticus7:35 'This is that which is consecrated to Aaron and that which is consecrated to his sons from the offerings by fire to the LORD
Leviticus7:36 'These the LORD had commanded to be given them from the sons of Israel in the day that He anointed them. It is their due forever throughout their generations.'
Leviticus7:37 This is the law of the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 which the LORD commanded Moses at Mount Sinai in the day that He commanded the sons of Israel to present their offerings to the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai.

Leviticus8:13 Next Moses had Aaron's sons come near and clothed them with tunics
Leviticus8:14 Then he brought the bull of the sin offering

Leviticus8:16 He also took all the fat that was on the entrails and the lobe of the liver
Leviticus8:17 But the bull and its hide and its flesh and its refuse he burned in the fire outside the camp
Leviticus8:18 Then he presented the ram of the burnt offering
Leviticus8:19 Moses slaughtered it and sprinkled the blood around on the altar.
Leviticus8:20 When he had cut the ram into its pieces
Leviticus8:21 After he had washed the entrails and the legs with water
Leviticus8:22 Then he presented the second ram
Leviticus8:23 Moses slaughtered it and took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron's right ear
Leviticus8:24 He also had Aaron's sons come near; and Moses put some of the blood on the lobe of their right ear
Leviticus8:25 He took the fat
Leviticus8:26 From the basket of unleavened bread that was before the LORD
Leviticus8:27 He then put all these on the hands of Aaron and on the hands of his sons and presented them as a wave offering before the LORD.
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took them from their hands and offered them up in smoke on the altar with the burnt offering. They were an ordination offering for a soothing aroma; it was an offering by fire to the LORD.
Leviticus8:29 Moses also took the breast and presented it for a wave offering before the LORD; it was Moses' portion of the ram of ordination
Leviticus8:30 So Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood which was on the altar and sprinkled it on Aaron
Leviticus8:31 Then Moses said to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus8:32 The remainder of the flesh and of the bread you shall burn in the fire.
Leviticus8:33 You shall not go outside the doorway of the tent of meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 The LORD has commanded to do as has been done this day
Leviticus8:35 At the doorway of the tent of meeting
Leviticus8:36 Thus Aaron and his sons did all the things which the LORD had commanded through Moses.

Leviticus11:1 The LORD spoke again to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus11:3 'Whatever divides a hoof
Leviticus11:4 'Nevertheless
Leviticus11:5 'Likewise
Leviticus11:6 the rabbit also
Leviticus11:7 and the pig
Leviticus11:8 'You shall not eat of their flesh nor touch their carcasses; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:9 'These you may eat
Leviticus11:10 'But whatever is in the seas and in the rivers that does not have fins and scales among all the teeming life of the water
Leviticus11:11 and they shall be abhorrent to you; you may not eat of their flesh
Leviticus11:12 'Whatever in the water does not have fins and scales is abhorrent to you.
Leviticus11:13 'These
Leviticus11:14 and the kite and the falcon in its kind
Leviticus11:15 every raven in its kind
Leviticus11:16 and the ostrich and the owl and the sea gull and the hawk in its kind
Leviticus11:17 and the little owl and the cormorant and the great owl
Leviticus11:18 and the white owl and the pelican and the carrion vulture
Leviticus11:19 and the stork
Leviticus11:20 'All the winged insects that walk on all fours are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:21 'Yet these you may eat among all the winged insects which walk on all fours: those which have above their feet jointed legs with which to jump on the earth.
Leviticus11:22 'These of them you may eat: the locust in its kinds
Leviticus11:23 'But all other winged insects which are four-footed are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 'By these
Leviticus11:25 and whoever picks up any of their carcasses shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:26 'Concerning all the animals which divide the hoof but do not make a split hoof
Leviticus11:27 'Also whatever walks on its paws
Leviticus11:28 and the one who picks up their carcasses shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:29 'Now these are to you the unclean among the swarming things which swarm on the earth: the mole
Leviticus11:30 and the gecko
Leviticus11:31 'These are to you the unclean among all the swarming things; whoever touches them when they are dead becomes unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:32 'Also anything on which one of them may fall when they are dead becomes unclean
Leviticus11:33 'As for any earthenware vessel into which one of them may fall
Leviticus11:34 'Any of the food which may be eaten
Leviticus11:35 'Everything
Leviticus11:36 'Nevertheless a spring or a cistern collecting water shall be clean
Leviticus11:37 'If a part of their carcass falls on any seed for sowing which is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 'Though if water is put on the seed and a part of their carcass falls on it
Leviticus11:39 'Also if one of the animals dies which you have for food
Leviticus11:40 'He too
Leviticus11:41 'Now every swarming thing that swarms on the earth is detestable
Leviticus11:42 'Whatever crawls on its belly
Leviticus11:43 'Do not render yourselves detestable through any of the swarming things that swarm; and you shall not make yourselves unclean with them so that you become unclean.
Leviticus11:44 'For I am the LORD your God. Consecrate yourselves therefore
Leviticus11:45 'For I am the LORD who brought you up from the land of Egypt to be your God; thus you shall be holy
Leviticus11:46 This is the law regarding the animal and the bird
Leviticus11:47 to make a distinction between the unclean and the clean

Leviticus13:6 The priest shall look at him again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 But if the scab spreads farther on the skin after he has shown himself to the priest for his cleansing

Leviticus13:13 then the priest shall look
Leviticus13:14 But whenever raw flesh appears on him

Leviticus13:19 and in the place of the boil there is a white swelling or a reddish-white

Leviticus13:22 and if it spreads farther on the skin

Leviticus13:36 then the priest shall look at him

Leviticus13:39 then the priest shall look
Leviticus13:40 Now if a man loses the hair of his head
Leviticus13:41 If his head becomes bald at the front and sides
Leviticus13:42 But if on the bald head or the bald forehead
Leviticus13:43 Then the priest shall look at him; and if the swelling of the infection is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead
Leviticus13:44 he is a leprous man
Leviticus13:45 As for the leper who has the infection
Leviticus13:46 He shall remain unclean all the days during which he has the infection; he is unclean. He shall live alone; his dwelling shall be outside the camp.

Leviticus14:29 Moreover
Leviticus14:30 He shall then offer one of the turtledoves or young pigeons
Leviticus14:31 He shall offer what he can afford
Leviticus14:32 This is the law for him in whom there is an infection of leprosy

Leviticus16:5 He shall take from the congregation of the sons of Israel two male goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 Then Aaron shall offer the bull for the sin offering which is for himself

Leviticus16:13 He shall put the incense on the fire before the LORD
Leviticus16:14 Moreover
Leviticus16:15 Then he shall slaughter the goat of the sin offering which is for the people
Leviticus16:16 He shall make atonement for the holy place

Leviticus16:18 Then he shall go out to the altar that is before the LORD and make atonement for it

Leviticus16:20 When he finishes atoning for the holy place and the tent of meeting and the altar
Leviticus16:21 Then Aaron shall lay both of his hands on the head of the live goat
Leviticus16:22 The goat shall bear on itself all their iniquities to a solitary land; and he shall release the goat in the wilderness.
Leviticus16:23 Then Aaron shall come into the tent of meeting and take off the linen garments which he put on when he went into the holy place
Leviticus16:24 He shall bathe his body with water in a holy place and put on his clothes
Leviticus16:25 Then he shall offer up in smoke the fat of the sin offering on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 The one who released the goat as the scapegoat shall wash his clothes and bathe his body with water; then afterward he shall come into the camp.
Leviticus16:27 But the bull of the sin offering and the goat of the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 Then the one who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body with water
Leviticus16:29 This shall be a permanent statute for you: in the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 for it is on this day that atonement shall be made for you to cleanse you; you will be clean from all your sins before the LORD.
Leviticus16:31 It is to be a sabbath of solemn rest for you
Leviticus16:32 So the priest who is anointed and ordained to serve as priest in his father's place shall make atonement: he shall thus put on the linen garments
Leviticus16:33 and make atonement for the holy sanctuary
Leviticus16:34 Now you shall have this as a permanent statute

Leviticus19:3 'Every one of you shall reverence his mother and his father
Leviticus19:4 'Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves molten gods; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:5 'Now when you offer a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD
Leviticus19:6 'It shall be eaten the same day you offer it
Leviticus19:7 'So if it is eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus19:8 'Everyone who eats it will bear his iniquity
Leviticus19:9 'Now when you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 'Nor shall you glean your vineyard
Leviticus19:11 'You shall not steal
Leviticus19:12 'You shall not swear falsely by My name
Leviticus19:13 'You shall not oppress your neighbor
Leviticus19:14 'You shall not curse a deaf man
Leviticus19:15 'You shall do no injustice in judgment; you shall not be partial to the poor nor defer to the great
Leviticus19:16 'You shall not go about as a slanderer among your people
Leviticus19:17 'You shall not hate your fellow countryman in your heart; you may surely reprove your neighbor
Leviticus19:18 'You shall not take vengeance
Leviticus19:19 'You are to keep My statutes. You shall not breed together two kinds of your cattle; you shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed
Leviticus19:20 'Now if a man lies carnally with a woman who is a slave acquired for another man
Leviticus19:21 'He shall bring his guilt offering to the LORD to the doorway of the tent of meeting
Leviticus19:22 'The priest shall also make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering before the LORD for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus19:23 'When you enter the land and plant all kinds of trees for food
Leviticus19:24 'But in the fourth year all its fruit shall be holy
Leviticus19:25 'In the fifth year you are to eat of its fruit
Leviticus19:26 'You shall not eat anything with the blood
Leviticus19:27 'You shall not round off the side-growth of your heads nor harm the edges of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 'You shall not make any cuts in your body for the dead nor make any tattoo marks on yourselves: I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:29 'Do not profane your daughter by making her a harlot
Leviticus19:30 'You shall keep My sabbaths and revere My sanctuary; I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:31 'Do not turn to mediums or spiritists; do not seek them out to be defiled by them. I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:32 'You shall rise up before the grayheaded and honor the aged
Leviticus19:33 'When a stranger resides with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 'The stranger who resides with you shall be to you as the native among you
Leviticus19:35 'You shall do no wrong in judgment
Leviticus19:36 'You shall have just balances
Leviticus19:37 'You shall thus observe all My statutes and all My ordinances and do them; I am the LORD.'

Leviticus21:22 'He may eat the food of his God

Leviticus21:24 So Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the sons of Israel.
Leviticus22:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:2 Tell Aaron and his sons to be careful with the holy gifts of the sons of Israel
Leviticus22:3 Say to them
Leviticus22:4 'No man of the descendants of Aaron
Leviticus22:5 or if a man touches any teeming things by which he is made unclean
Leviticus22:6 a person who touches any such shall be unclean until evening
Leviticus22:7 'But when the sun sets
Leviticus22:8 'He shall not eat an animal which dies or is torn by beasts
Leviticus22:9 'They shall therefore keep My charge
Leviticus22:10 'No layman
Leviticus22:11 'But if a priest buys a slave as his property with his money
Leviticus22:12 'If a priest's daughter is married to a layman
Leviticus22:13 'But if a priest's daughter becomes a widow or divorced
Leviticus22:14 'But if a man eats a holy gift unintentionally
Leviticus22:15 'They shall not profane the holy gifts of the sons of Israel which they offer to the LORD
Leviticus22:16 and so cause them to bear punishment for guilt by eating their holy gifts; for I am the LORD who sanctifies them.'
Leviticus22:17 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:18 Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus22:19 for you to be accepted--it must be a male without defect from the cattle
Leviticus22:20 'Whatever has a defect
Leviticus22:21 'When a man offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD to fulfill a special vow or for a freewill offering
Leviticus22:22 'Those that are blind or fractured or maimed or having a running sore or eczema or scabs
Leviticus22:23 'In respect to an ox or a lamb which has an overgrown or stunted member
Leviticus22:24 'Also anything with its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut
Leviticus22:25 nor shall you accept any such from the hand of a foreigner for offering as the food of your God; for their corruption is in them
Leviticus22:26 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:27 When an ox or a sheep or a goat is born
Leviticus22:28 But
Leviticus22:29 When you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the LORD
Leviticus22:30 It shall be eaten on the same day
Leviticus22:31 So you shall keep My commandments
Leviticus22:32 You shall not profane My holy name
Leviticus22:33 who brought you out from the land of Egypt
Leviticus23:1 The LORD spoke again to Moses
Leviticus23:2 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus23:3 'For six days work may be done
Leviticus23:4 'These are the appointed times of the LORD
Leviticus23:5 'In the first month
Leviticus23:6 'Then on the fifteenth day of the same month there is the Feast of Unleavened Bread to the LORD; for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 'On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not do any laborious work.
Leviticus23:8 'But for seven days you shall present an offering by fire to the LORD. On the seventh day is a holy convocation; you shall not do any laborious work.'
Leviticus23:9 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:10 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus23:11 'He shall wave the sheaf before the LORD for you to be accepted; on the day after the sabbath the priest shall wave it.
Leviticus23:12 'Now on the day when you wave the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 'Its grain offering shall then be two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 'Until this same day
Leviticus23:15 'You shall also count for yourselves from the day after the sabbath
Leviticus23:16 'You shall count fifty days to the day after the seventh sabbath; then you shall present a new grain offering to the LORD.
Leviticus23:17 'You shall bring in from your dwelling places two loaves of bread for a wave offering
Leviticus23:18 'Along with the bread you shall present seven one year old male lambs without defect
Leviticus23:19 'You shall also offer one male goat for a sin offering and two male lambs one year old for a sacrifice of peace offerings.
Leviticus23:20 'The priest shall then wave them with the bread of the first fruits for a wave offering with two lambs before the LORD; they are to be holy to the LORD for the priest.
Leviticus23:21 'On this same day you shall make a proclamation as well; you are to have a holy convocation. You shall do no laborious work. It is to be a perpetual statute in all your dwelling places throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 'When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 Again the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus23:25 'You shall not do any laborious work
Leviticus23:26 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:27 On exactly the tenth day of this seventh month is the day of atonement; it shall be a holy convocation for you
Leviticus23:28 You shall not do any work on this same day
Leviticus23:29 If there is any person who will not humble himself on this same day
Leviticus23:30 As for any person who does any work on this same day
Leviticus23:31 You shall do no work at all. It is to be a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your dwelling places.
Leviticus23:32 It is to be a sabbath of complete rest to you
Leviticus23:33 Again the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus23:35 'On the first day is a holy convocation; you shall do no laborious work of any kind.
Leviticus23:36 'For seven days you shall present an offering by fire to the LORD. On the eighth day you shall have a holy convocation and present an offering by fire to the LORD; it is an assembly. You shall do no laborious work.
Leviticus23:37 'These are the appointed times of the LORD which you shall proclaim as holy convocations
Leviticus23:38 besides those of the sabbaths of the LORD
Leviticus23:39 'On exactly the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 'Now on the first day you shall take for yourselves the foliage of beautiful trees
Leviticus23:41 'You shall thus celebrate it as a feast to the LORD for seven days in the year. It shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations; you shall celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 'You shall live in booths for seven days; all the native-born in Israel shall live in booths
Leviticus23:43 so that your generations may know that I had the sons of Israel live in booths when I brought them out from the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.'
Leviticus23:44 So Moses declared to the sons of Israel the appointed times of the LORD.
Leviticus24:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Command the sons of Israel that they bring to you clear oil from beaten olives for the light
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil of testimony in the tent of meeting
Leviticus24:4 He shall keep the lamps in order on the pure gold lampstand before the LORD continually.
Leviticus24:5 Then you shall take fine flour and bake twelve cakes with it; two-tenths of an ephah shall be in each cake.
Leviticus24:6 You shall set them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 You shall put pure frankincense on each row that it may be a memorial portion for the bread
Leviticus24:8 Every sabbath day he shall set it in order before the LORD continually; it is an everlasting covenant for the sons of Israel.
Leviticus24:9 It shall be for Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 Now the son of an Israelite woman
Leviticus24:11 The son of the Israelite woman blasphemed the Name and cursed. So they brought him to Moses. (Now his mother's name was Shelomith
Leviticus24:12 They put him in custody so that the command of the LORD might be made clear to them.
Leviticus24:13 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:14 Bring the one who has cursed outside the camp
Leviticus24:15 You shall speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus24:16 'Moreover
Leviticus24:17 'If a man takes the life of any human being
Leviticus24:18 'The one who takes the life of an animal shall make it good
Leviticus24:19 'If a man injures his neighbor
Leviticus24:20 fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 'Thus the one who kills an animal shall make it good
Leviticus24:22 'There shall be one standard for you; it shall be for the stranger as well as the native
Leviticus24:23 Then Moses spoke to the sons of Israel
Leviticus25:1 The LORD then spoke to Moses at Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus25:3 'Six years you shall sow your field
Leviticus25:4 but during the seventh year the land shall have a sabbath rest
Leviticus25:5 'Your harvest's aftergrowth you shall not reap
Leviticus25:6 'All of you shall have the sabbath products of the land for food; yourself
Leviticus25:7 'Even your cattle and the animals that are in your land shall have all its crops to eat.
Leviticus25:8 'You are also to count off seven sabbaths of years for yourself
Leviticus25:9 'You shall then sound a ram's horn abroad on the tenth day of the seventh month; on the day of atonement you shall sound a horn all through your land.
Leviticus25:10 'You shall thus consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim a release through the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you
Leviticus25:11 'You shall have the fiftieth year as a jubilee; you shall not sow
Leviticus25:12 'For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you. You shall eat its crops out of the field.
Leviticus25:13 'On this year of jubilee each of you shall return to his own property.
Leviticus25:14 'If you make a sale
Leviticus25:15 'Corresponding to the number of years after the jubilee
Leviticus25:16 'In proportion to the extent of the years you shall increase its price
Leviticus25:17 'So you shall not wrong one another
Leviticus25:18 'You shall thus observe My statutes and keep My judgments
Leviticus25:19 'Then the land will yield its produce
Leviticus25:20 'But if you say
Leviticus25:21 then I will so order My blessing for you in the sixth year that it will bring forth the crop for three years.
Leviticus25:22 'When you are sowing the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 'The land
Leviticus25:24 'Thus for every piece of your property
Leviticus25:25 'If a fellow countryman of yours becomes so poor he has to sell part of his property
Leviticus25:26 'Or in case a man has no kinsman
Leviticus25:27 then he shall calculate the years since its sale and refund the balance to the man to whom he sold it
Leviticus25:28 'But if he has not found sufficient means to get it back for himself
Leviticus25:29 'Likewise
Leviticus25:30 'But if it is not bought back for him within the space of a full year
Leviticus25:31 'The houses of the villages
Leviticus25:32 'As for cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 'What
Leviticus25:34 'But pasture fields of their cities shall not be sold
Leviticus25:35 'Now in case a countryman of yours becomes poor and his means with regard to you falter
Leviticus25:36 'Do not take usurious interest from him
Leviticus25:37 'You shall not give him your silver at interest
Leviticus25:38 'I am the LORD your God
Leviticus25:39 'If a countryman of yours becomes so poor with regard to you that he sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 'He shall be with you as a hired man
Leviticus25:41 'He shall then go out from you
Leviticus25:42 'For they are My servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt; they are not to be sold in a slave sale.
Leviticus25:43 'You shall not rule over him with severity
Leviticus25:44 'As for your male and female slaves whom you may have--you may acquire male and female slaves from the pagan nations that are around you.
Leviticus25:45 'Then
Leviticus25:46 'You may even bequeath them to your sons after you
Leviticus25:47 'Now if the means of a stranger or of a sojourner with you becomes sufficient
Leviticus25:48 then he shall have redemption right after he has been sold. One of his brothers may redeem him
Leviticus25:49 or his uncle
Leviticus25:50 'He then with his purchaser shall calculate from the year when he sold himself to him up to the year of jubilee; and the price of his sale shall correspond to the number of years. It is like the days of a hired man that he shall be with him.
Leviticus25:51 'If there are still many years
Leviticus25:52 and if few years remain until the year of jubilee
Leviticus25:53 'Like a man hired year by year he shall be with him; he shall not rule over him with severity in your sight.
Leviticus25:54 'Even if he is not redeemed by these means
Leviticus25:55 'For the sons of Israel are My servants; they are My servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus26:1 'You shall not make for yourselves idols
Leviticus26:2 'You shall keep My sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary; I am the LORD.
Leviticus26:3 'If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments so as to carry them out
Leviticus26:4 then I shall give you rains in their season
Leviticus26:5 'Indeed
Leviticus26:6 'I shall also grant peace in the land
Leviticus26:7 'But you will chase your enemies and they will fall before you by the sword;
Leviticus26:8 five of you will chase a hundred
Leviticus26:9 'So I will turn toward you and make you fruitful and multiply you
Leviticus26:10 'You will eat the old supply and clear out the old because of the new.
Leviticus26:11 'Moreover
Leviticus26:12 'I will also walk among you and be your God
Leviticus26:13 'I am the LORD your God
Leviticus26:14 'But if you do not obey Me and do not carry out all these commandments
Leviticus26:15 if
Leviticus26:16 I
Leviticus26:17 'I will set My face against you so that you will be struck down before your enemies; and those who hate you will rule over you
Leviticus26:18 'If also after these things you do not obey Me
Leviticus26:19 'I will also break down your pride of power; I will also make your sky like iron and your earth like bronze.
Leviticus26:20 'Your strength will be spent uselessly
Leviticus26:21 'If then
Leviticus26:22 'I will let loose among you the beasts of the field
Leviticus26:23 'And if by these things you are not turned to Me
Leviticus26:24 then I will act with hostility against you; and I
Leviticus26:25 'I will also bring upon you a sword which will execute vengeance for the covenant; and when you gather together into your cities
Leviticus26:26 'When I break your staff of bread
Leviticus26:27 'Yet if in spite of this you do not obey Me
Leviticus26:28 then I will act with wrathful hostility against you
Leviticus26:29 'Further
Leviticus26:30 'I then will destroy your high places
Leviticus26:31 'I will lay waste your cities as well and will make your sanctuaries desolate
Leviticus26:32 'I will make the land desolate so that your enemies who settle in it will be appalled over it.
Leviticus26:33 'You
Leviticus26:34 'Then the land will enjoy its sabbaths all the days of the desolation
Leviticus26:35 'All the days of its desolation it will observe the rest which it did not observe on your sabbaths
Leviticus26:36 'As for those of you who may be left
Leviticus26:37 'They will therefore stumble over each other as if running from the sword
Leviticus26:38 'But you will perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 'So those of you who may be left will rot away because of their iniquity in the lands of your enemies; and also because of the iniquities of their forefathers they will rot away with them.
Leviticus26:40 'If they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their forefathers
Leviticus26:41 I also was acting with hostility against them
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember My covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 'For the land will be abandoned by them
Leviticus26:44 'Yet in spite of this
Leviticus26:45 'But I will remember for them the covenant with their ancestors
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes and ordinances and laws which the LORD established between Himself and the sons of Israel through Moses at Mount Sinai.
Leviticus27:1 Again
Leviticus27:2 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus27:3 'If your valuation is of the male from twenty years even to sixty years old
Leviticus27:4 'Or if it is a female
Leviticus27:5 'If it be from five years even to twenty years old then your valuation for the male shall be twenty shekels and for the female ten shekels.
Leviticus27:6 'But if they are from a month even up to five years old
Leviticus27:7 'If they are from sixty years old and upward
Leviticus27:8 'But if he is poorer than your valuation
Leviticus27:9 'Now if it is an animal of the kind which men can present as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:10 'He shall not replace it or exchange it
Leviticus27:11 'If
Leviticus27:12 'The priest shall value it as either good or bad; as you
Leviticus27:13 'But if he should ever wish to redeem it
Leviticus27:14 'Now if a man consecrates his house as holy to the LORD
Leviticus27:15 'Yet if the one who consecrates it should wish to redeem his house
Leviticus27:16 'Again
Leviticus27:17 'If he consecrates his field as of the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:18 'If he consecrates his field after the jubilee
Leviticus27:19 'If the one who consecrates it should ever wish to redeem the field
Leviticus27:20 'Yet if he will not redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 and when it reverts in the jubilee
Leviticus27:22 'Or if he consecrates to the LORD a field which he has bought
Leviticus27:23 then the priest shall calculate for him the amount of your valuation up to the year of jubilee; and he shall on that day give your valuation as holy to the LORD.
Leviticus27:24 'In the year of jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom he bought it
Leviticus27:25 'Every valuation of yours
Leviticus27:26 'However
Leviticus27:27 'But if it is among the unclean animals
Leviticus27:28 'Nevertheless
Leviticus27:29 'No one who may have been set apart among men shall be ransomed; he shall surely be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 'Thus all the tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 'If
Leviticus27:32 'For every tenth part of herd or flock
Leviticus27:33 'He is not to be concerned whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments which the LORD commanded Moses for the sons of Israel at Mount Sinai.

Numbers1:2 Take a census of all the congregation of the sons of Israel
Numbers1:3 from twenty years old and upward
Numbers1:4 With you
Numbers1:5 These then are the names of the men who shall stand with you: of Reuben
Numbers1:6 of Simeon
Numbers1:7 of Judah
Numbers1:8 of Issachar
Numbers1:9 of Zebulun

Numbers1:11 of Benjamin
Numbers1:12 of Dan
Numbers1:13 of Asher
Numbers1:14 of Gad
Numbers1:15 of Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These are they who were called of the congregation
Numbers1:17 So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name
Numbers1:18 and they assembled all the congregation together on the first of the second month. Then they registered by ancestry in their families
Numbers1:19 just as the LORD had commanded Moses. So he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.

Numbers1:22 Of the sons of Simeon

Numbers1:24 Of the sons of Gad

Numbers1:26 Of the sons of Judah

Numbers1:28 Of the sons of Issachar

Numbers1:32 Of the sons of Joseph

Numbers1:34 Of the sons of Manasseh

Numbers1:36 Of the sons of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 their numbered men of the tribe of Benjamin were 35
Numbers1:38 Of the sons of Dan

Numbers1:40 Of the sons of Asher

Numbers1:43 their numbered men of the tribe of Naphtali were 53
Numbers1:44 These are the ones who were numbered

Numbers1:47 The Levites

Numbers1:53 But the Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:54 Thus the sons of Israel did; according to all which the LORD had commanded Moses
