Numbers3:1 Now these are the records of the generations of Aaron and Moses at the time when the LORD spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These then are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn
Numbers3:3 These are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died before the LORD when they offered strange fire before the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai; and they had no children. So Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests in the lifetime of their father Aaron.
Numbers3:5 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi near and set them before Aaron the priest
Numbers3:7 They shall perform the duties for him and for the whole congregation before the tent of meeting
Numbers3:8 They shall also keep all the furnishings of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:9 You shall thus give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the sons of Israel.
Numbers3:10 So you shall appoint Aaron and his sons that they may keep their priesthood
Numbers3:11 Again the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:12 Now
Numbers3:13 For all the firstborn are Mine; on the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 Then the LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 Number the sons of Levi by their fathers' households
Numbers3:16 So Moses numbered them according to the word of the LORD
Numbers3:17 These then are the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon and Kohath and Merari.
Numbers3:18 These are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei;
Numbers3:19 and the sons of Kohath by their families: Amram and Izhar
Numbers3:20 and the sons of Merari by their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their fathers' households.
Numbers3:21 Of Gershon was the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimeites; these were the families of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 Their numbered men
Numbers3:23 The families of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle westward
Numbers3:24 and the leader of the fathers' households of the Gershonites was Eliasaph the son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 Now the duties of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting involved the tabernacle and the tent
Numbers3:26 and the hangings of the court
Numbers3:27 Of Kohath was the family of the Amramites and the family of the Izharites and the family of the Hebronites and the family of the Uzzielites; these were the families of the Kohathites.
Numbers3:28 In the numbering of every male from a month old and upward
Numbers3:29 The families of the sons of Kohath were to camp on the southward side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 and the leader of the fathers' households of the Kohathite families was Elizaphan the son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Now their duties involved the ark
Numbers3:32 and Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest was the chief of the leaders of Levi
Numbers3:33 Of Merari was the family of the Mahlites and the family of the Mushites; these were the families of Merari.
Numbers3:34 Their numbered men in the numbering of every male from a month old and upward
Numbers3:35 The leader of the fathers' households of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail. They were to camp on the northward side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 Now the appointed duties of the sons of Merari involved the frames of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 and the pillars around the court with their sockets and their pegs and their cords.
Numbers3:38 Now those who were to camp before the tabernacle eastward
Numbers3:39 All the numbered men of the Levites
Numbers3:40 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers3:41 You shall take the Levites for Me
Numbers3:42 So Moses numbered all the firstborn among the sons of Israel
Numbers3:43 and all the firstborn males by the number of names from a month old and upward
Numbers3:44 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:45 Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the sons of Israel and the cattle of the Levites. And the Levites shall be Mine; I am the LORD.
Numbers3:46 For the ransom of the 273 of the firstborn of the sons of Israel who are in excess beyond the Levites
Numbers3:47 you shall take five shekels apiece
Numbers3:48 and give the money
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the ransom money from those who were in excess
Numbers3:50 from the firstborn of the sons of Israel he took the money in terms of the shekel of the sanctuary
Numbers3:51 Then Moses gave the ransom money to Aaron and to his sons
Numbers4:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers4:2 Take a census of the descendants of Kohath from among the sons of Levi
Numbers4:3 from thirty years and upward
Numbers4:4 This is the work of the descendants of Kohath in the tent of meeting
Numbers4:5 When the camp sets out
Numbers4:6 and they shall lay a covering of porpoise skin on it
Numbers4:7 Over the table of the bread of the Presence they shall also spread a cloth of blue and put on it the dishes and the pans and the sacrificial bowls and the jars for the drink offering
Numbers4:8 They shall spread over them a cloth of scarlet material
Numbers4:9 Then they shall take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand for the light
Numbers4:10 and they shall put it and all its utensils in a covering of porpoise skin
Numbers4:11 Over the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth and cover it with a covering of porpoise skin
Numbers4:12 and they shall take all the utensils of service
Numbers4:13 Then they shall take away the ashes from the altar
Numbers4:14 They shall also put on it all its utensils by which they serve in connection with it: the firepans
Numbers4:15 When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the holy objects and all the furnishings of the sanctuary
Numbers4:16 The responsibility of Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest is the oil for the light and the fragrant incense and the continual grain offering and the anointing oil--the responsibility of all the tabernacle and of all that is in it
Numbers4:17 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron

Numbers6:1 Again the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers6:2 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Numbers6:3 he shall abstain from wine and strong drink; he shall drink no vinegar
Numbers6:4 'All the days of his separation he shall not eat anything that is produced by the grape vine
Numbers6:5 'All the days of his vow of separation no razor shall pass over his head. He shall be holy until the days are fulfilled for which he separated himself to the LORD; he shall let the locks of hair on his head grow long.
Numbers6:6 'All the days of his separation to the LORD he shall not go near to a dead person.
Numbers6:7 'He shall not make himself unclean for his father or for his mother
Numbers6:8 'All the days of his separation he is holy to the LORD.
Numbers6:9 'But if a man dies very suddenly beside him and he defiles his dedicated head of hair
Numbers6:10 'Then on the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest
Numbers6:11 'The priest shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering
Numbers6:12 and shall dedicate to the LORD his days as a Nazirite
Numbers6:13 'Now this is the law of the Nazirite when the days of his separation are fulfilled
Numbers6:14 'He shall present his offering to the LORD: one male lamb a year old without defect for a burnt offering and one ewe-lamb a year old without defect for a sin offering and one ram without defect for a peace offering
Numbers6:15 and a basket of unleavened cakes of fine flour mixed with oil and unleavened wafers spread with oil
Numbers6:16 'Then the priest shall present them before the LORD and shall offer his sin offering and his burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 'He shall also offer the ram for a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD
Numbers6:18 'The Nazirite shall then shave his dedicated head of hair at the doorway of the tent of meeting
Numbers6:19 'The priest shall take the ram's shoulder when it has been boiled
Numbers6:20 'Then the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the LORD. It is holy for the priest
Numbers6:21 This is the law of the Nazirite who vows his offering to the LORD according to his separation

Numbers19:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 This is the statute of the law which the LORD has commanded
Numbers19:3 'You shall give it to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 'Next Eleazar the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and sprinkle some of its blood toward the front of the tent of meeting seven times.
Numbers19:5 'Then the heifer shall be burned in his sight; its hide and its flesh and its blood
Numbers19:6 'The priest shall take cedar wood and hyssop and scarlet material and cast it into the midst of the burning heifer.
Numbers19:7 'The priest shall then wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:8 'The one who burns it shall also wash his clothes in water and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:9 'Now a man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer and deposit them outside the camp in a clean place
Numbers19:10 'The one who gathers the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening; and it shall be a perpetual statute to the sons of Israel and to the alien who sojourns among them.
Numbers19:11 'The one who touches the corpse of any person shall be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 'That one shall purify himself from uncleanness with the water on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 'Anyone who touches a corpse
Numbers19:14 'This is the law when a man dies in a tent: everyone who comes into the tent and everyone who is in the tent shall be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:15 'Every open vessel
Numbers19:16 'Also
Numbers19:17 'Then for the unclean person they shall take some of the ashes of the burnt purification from sin and flowing water shall be added to them in a vessel.
Numbers19:18 'A clean person shall take hyssop and dip it in the water
Numbers19:19 'Then the clean person shall sprinkle on the unclean on the third day and on the seventh day; and on the seventh day he shall purify him from uncleanness
Numbers19:20 'But the man who is unclean and does not purify himself from uncleanness
Numbers19:21 'So it shall be a perpetual statute for them. And he who sprinkles the water for impurity shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 'Furthermore

Numbers35:1 Now the LORD spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho
Numbers35:2 Command the sons of Israel that they give to the Levites from the inheritance of their possession cities to live in; and you shall give to the Levites pasture lands around the cities.
Numbers35:3 The cities shall be theirs to live in; and their pasture lands shall be for their cattle and for their herds and for all their beasts.
Numbers35:4 The pasture lands of the cities which you shall give to the Levites shall extend from the wall of the city outward a thousand cubits around.
Numbers35:5 You shall also measure outside the city on the east side two thousand cubits
Numbers35:6 The cities which you shall give to the Levites shall be the six cities of refuge
Numbers35:7 All the cities which you shall give to the Levites shall be forty-eight cities

Numbers35:12 'The cities shall be to you as a refuge from the avenger

Numbers35:14 'You shall give three cities across the Jordan and three cities in the land of Canaan; they are to be cities of refuge.
Numbers35:15 'These six cities shall be for refuge for the sons of Israel
Numbers35:16 'But if he struck him down with an iron object
Numbers35:17 'If he struck him down with a stone in the hand
Numbers35:18 'Or if he struck him with a wooden object in the hand
Numbers35:19 'The blood avenger himself shall put the murderer to death; he shall put him to death when he meets him.
Numbers35:20 'If he pushed him of hatred
Numbers35:21 or if he struck him down with his hand in enmity
Numbers35:22 'But if he pushed him suddenly without enmity
Numbers35:23 or with any deadly object of stone
Numbers35:24 then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the blood avenger according to these ordinances.
Numbers35:25 'The congregation shall deliver the manslayer from the hand of the blood avenger
Numbers35:26 'But if the manslayer at any time goes beyond the border of his city of refuge to which he may flee
Numbers35:27 and the blood avenger finds him outside the border of his city of refuge
Numbers35:28 because he should have remained in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest. But after the death of the high priest the manslayer shall return to the land of his possession.
Numbers35:29 'These things shall be for a statutory ordinance to you throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Numbers35:30 'If anyone kills a person
Numbers35:31 'Moreover
Numbers35:32 'You shall not take ransom for him who has fled to his city of refuge
Numbers35:33 'So you shall not pollute the land in which you are; for blood pollutes the land and no expiation can be made for the land for the blood that is shed on it
Numbers35:34 'You shall not defile the land in which you live

Deuteronomy4:1 Now
Deuteronomy4:2 You shall not add to the word which I am commanding you
Deuteronomy4:3 Your eyes have seen what the LORD has done in the case of Baal-peor
Deuteronomy4:4 But you who held fast to the LORD your God are alive today
Deuteronomy4:5 See
Deuteronomy4:6 So keep and do them
Deuteronomy4:7 For what great nation is there that has a god so near to it as is the LORD our God whenever we call on Him?
Deuteronomy4:8 Or what great nation is there that has statutes and judgments as righteous as this whole law which I am setting before you today?
Deuteronomy4:9 Only give heed to yourself and keep your soul diligently
Deuteronomy4:10 Remember the day you stood before the LORD your God at Horeb
Deuteronomy4:11 You came near and stood at the foot of the mountain
Deuteronomy4:12 Then the LORD spoke to you from the midst of the fire; you heard the sound of words
Deuteronomy4:13 So He declared to you His covenant which He commanded you to perform
Deuteronomy4:14 The LORD commanded me at that time to teach you statutes and judgments
Deuteronomy4:15 So watch yourselves carefully
Deuteronomy4:16 so that you do not act corruptly and make a graven image for yourselves in the form of any figure
Deuteronomy4:17 the likeness of any animal that is on the earth
Deuteronomy4:18 the likeness of anything that creeps on the ground
Deuteronomy4:19 And beware not to lift up your eyes to heaven and see the sun and the moon and the stars
Deuteronomy4:20 But the LORD has taken you and brought you out of the iron furnace
Deuteronomy4:21 Now the LORD was angry with me on your account
Deuteronomy4:22 For I will die in this land
Deuteronomy4:23 So watch yourselves
Deuteronomy4:24 For the LORD your God is a consuming fire
Deuteronomy4:25 When you become the father of children and children's children and have remained long in the land
Deuteronomy4:26 I call heaven and earth to witness against you today
Deuteronomy4:27 The LORD will scatter you among the peoples
Deuteronomy4:28 There you will serve gods
Deuteronomy4:29 But from there you will seek the LORD your God
Deuteronomy4:30 When you are in distress and all these things have come upon you
Deuteronomy4:31 For the LORD your God is a compassionate God; He will not fail you nor destroy you nor forget the covenant with your fathers which He swore to them.
Deuteronomy4:32 Indeed
Deuteronomy4:33 Has any people heard the voice of God speaking from the midst of the fire
Deuteronomy4:34 Or has a god tried to go to take for himself a nation from within another nation by trials
Deuteronomy4:35 To you it was shown that you might know that the LORD
Deuteronomy4:36 Out of the heavens He let you hear His voice to discipline you; and on earth He let you see His great fire
Deuteronomy4:37 Because He loved your fathers

Deuteronomy19:6 otherwise the avenger of blood might pursue the manslayer in the heat of his anger
Deuteronomy19:7 Therefore
Deuteronomy19:8 If the LORD your God enlarges your territory
Deuteronomy19:9 if you carefully observe all this commandment which I command you today
Deuteronomy19:10 So innocent blood will not be shed in the midst of your land which the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance

Deuteronomy19:13 You shall not pity him
Deuteronomy19:14 You shall not move your neighbor's boundary mark
Deuteronomy19:15 A single witness shall not rise up against a man on account of any iniquity or any sin which he has committed; on the evidence of two or three witnesses a matter shall be confirmed.
Deuteronomy19:16 If a malicious witness rises up against a man to accuse him of wrongdoing
Deuteronomy19:17 then both the men who have the dispute shall stand before the LORD
Deuteronomy19:18 The judges shall investigate thoroughly
Deuteronomy19:19 then you shall do to him just as he had intended to do to his brother. Thus you shall purge the evil from among you.
Deuteronomy19:20 The rest will hear and be afraid
Deuteronomy19:21 Thus you shall not show pity: life for life

Joshua1:4 From the wilderness and this Lebanon

Joshua1:8 This book of the law shall not depart from your mouth
Joshua1:9 Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous! Do not tremble or be dismayed

Joshua2:6 But she had brought them up to the roof and hidden them in the stalks of flax which she had laid in order on the roof.

Joshua8:2 You shall do to Ai and its king just as you did to Jericho and its king; you shall take only its spoil and its cattle as plunder for yourselves. Set an ambush for the city behind it.
Joshua8:3 So Joshua rose with all the people of war to go up to Ai; and Joshua chose 30
Joshua8:4 He commanded them
Joshua8:5 Then I and all the people who are with me will approach the city. And when they come out to meet us as at the first
Joshua8:6 They will come out after us until we have drawn them away from the city
Joshua8:7 And you shall rise from your ambush and take possession of the city
Joshua8:8 Then it will be when you have seized the city
Joshua8:9 So Joshua sent them away

Joshua8:13 So they stationed the people
Joshua8:14 It came about when the king of Ai saw it

Joshua8:17 So not a man was left in Ai or Bethel who had not gone out after Israel

Joshua8:19 The men in ambush rose quickly from their place
Joshua8:20 When the men of Ai turned back and looked

Joshua8:22 The others came out from the city to encounter them
Joshua8:23 But they took alive the king of Ai and brought him to Joshua.

Joshua8:25 All who fell that day
Joshua8:26 For Joshua did not withdraw his hand with which he stretched out the javelin until he had utterly destroyed all the inhabitants of Ai.
Joshua8:27 Israel took only the cattle and the spoil of that city as plunder for themselves
Joshua8:28 So Joshua burned Ai and made it a heap forever

Joshua8:34 Then afterward he read all the words of the law
Joshua8:35 There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded which Joshua did not read before all the assembly of Israel with the women and the little ones and the strangers who were living among them.

2Samuel22:1 And David spoke the words of this song to the LORD in the day that the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
2Samuel22:2 He said
2Samuel22:3 My God
2Samuel22:4 I call upon the LORD
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death encompassed me; The torrents of destruction overwhelmed me;
2Samuel22:6 The cords of Sheol surrounded me; The snares of death confronted me.
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon the LORD
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth shook and quaked
2Samuel22:9 Smoke went up out of His nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He bowed the heavens also
2Samuel22:11 And He rode on a cherub and flew; And He appeared on the wings of the wind.
2Samuel22:12 And He made darkness canopies around Him
2Samuel22:13 From the brightness before Him Coals of fire were kindled.
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from heaven
2Samuel22:15 And He sent out arrows
2Samuel22:16 Then the channels of the sea appeared
2Samuel22:17 He sent from on high
2Samuel22:18 He delivered me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
2Samuel22:20 He also brought me forth into a broad place; He rescued me
2Samuel22:21 The LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD
2Samuel22:23 For all His ordinances were before me
2Samuel22:24 I was also blameless toward Him
2Samuel22:25 Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 With the kind You show Yourself kind
2Samuel22:27 With the pure You show Yourself pure
2Samuel22:28 And You save an afflicted people; But Your eyes are on the haughty whom You abase.
2Samuel22:29 For You are my lamp
2Samuel22:30 For by You I can run upon a troop; By my God I can leap over a wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 For who is God
2Samuel22:33 God is my strong fortress; And He sets the blameless in His way.
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like hinds' feet
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for battle
2Samuel22:36 You have also given me the shield of Your salvation
2Samuel22:37 You enlarge my steps under me
2Samuel22:38 I pursued my enemies and destroyed them
2Samuel22:39 And I have devoured them and shattered them
2Samuel22:40 For You have girded me with strength for battle; You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
2Samuel22:41 You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 Then I pulverized them as the dust of the earth; I crushed and stamped them as the mire of the streets.
2Samuel22:44 You have also delivered me from the contentions of my people; You have kept me as head of the nations; A people whom I have not known serve me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners pretend obedience to me; As soon as they hear
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners lose heart
2Samuel22:47 The LORD lives
2Samuel22:48 The God who executes vengeance for me
2Samuel22:49 Who also brings me out from my enemies; You even lift me above those who rise up against me; You rescue me from the violent man.
2Samuel22:50 Therefore I will give thanks to You
2Samuel22:51 He is a tower of deliverance to His king

1Kings6:1 Now it came about in the four hundred and eightieth year after the sons of Israel came out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 As for the house which King Solomon built for the LORD
1Kings6:3 The porch in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:4 Also for the house he made windows with artistic frames.
1Kings6:5 Against the wall of the house he built stories encompassing the walls of the house around both the nave and the inner sanctuary; thus he made side chambers all around.
1Kings6:6 The lowest story was five cubits wide
1Kings6:7 The house
1Kings6:8 The doorway for the lowest side chamber was on the right side of the house; and they would go up by winding stairs to the middle story
1Kings6:9 So he built the house and finished it; and he covered the house with beams and planks of cedar.
1Kings6:10 He also built the stories against the whole house
1Kings6:11 Now the word of the LORD came to Solomon saying
1Kings6:12 Concerning this house which you are building
1Kings6:13 I will dwell among the sons of Israel
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house and finished it.
1Kings6:15 Then he built the walls of the house on the inside with boards of cedar; from the floor of the house to the ceiling he overlaid the walls on the inside with wood
1Kings6:16 He built twenty cubits on the rear part of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the ceiling; he built them for it on the inside as an inner sanctuary
1Kings6:17 The house
1Kings6:18 There was cedar on the house within
1Kings6:19 Then he prepared an inner sanctuary within the house in order to place there the ark of the covenant of the LORD.
1Kings6:20 The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:21 So Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold. And he drew chains of gold across the front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:22 He overlaid the whole house with gold
1Kings6:23 Also in the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olive wood
1Kings6:24 Five cubits was the one wing of the cherub and five cubits the other wing of the cherub; from the end of one wing to the end of the other wing were ten cubits.
1Kings6:25 The other cherub was ten cubits; both the cherubim were of the same measure and the same form.
1Kings6:26 The height of the one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 He placed the cherubim in the midst of the inner house
1Kings6:28 He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 Then he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved engravings of cherubim
1Kings6:30 He overlaid the floor of the house with gold
1Kings6:31 For the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood
1Kings6:32 So he made two doors of olive wood
1Kings6:33 So also he made for the entrance of the nave four-sided doorposts of olive wood
1Kings6:34 and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door turned on pivots
1Kings6:35 He carved on it cherubim
1Kings6:36 He built the inner court with three rows of cut stone and a row of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid

1Kings7:1 Now Solomon was building his own house thirteen years
1Kings7:2 He built the house of the forest of Lebanon; its length was 100 cubits and its width 50 cubits and its height 30 cubits
1Kings7:3 It was paneled with cedar above the side chambers which were on the 45 pillars
1Kings7:4 There were artistic window frames in three rows
1Kings7:5 All the doorways and doorposts had squared artistic frames
1Kings7:6 Then he made the hall of pillars; its length was 50 cubits and its width 30 cubits
1Kings7:7 He made the hall of the throne where he was to judge
1Kings7:8 His house where he was to live
1Kings7:9 All these were of costly stones
1Kings7:10 The foundation was of costly stones
1Kings7:11 And above were costly stones
1Kings7:12 So the great court all around had three rows of cut stone and a row of cedar beams even as the inner court of the house of the LORD
1Kings7:13 Now King Solomon sent and brought Hiram from Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was a widow's son from the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 He fashioned the two pillars of bronze; eighteen cubits was the height of one pillar
1Kings7:16 He also made two capitals of molten bronze to set on the tops of the pillars; the height of the one capital was five cubits and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
1Kings7:17 There were nets of network and twisted threads of chainwork for the capitals which were on the top of the pillars; seven for the one capital and seven for the other capital.
1Kings7:18 So he made the pillars
1Kings7:19 The capitals which were on the top of the pillars in the porch were of lily design
1Kings7:20 There were capitals on the two pillars
1Kings7:21 Thus he set up the pillars at the porch of the nave; and he set up the right pillar and named it Jachin
1Kings7:22 On the top of the pillars was lily design. So the work of the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 Now he made the sea of cast metal ten cubits from brim to brim
1Kings7:24 Under its brim gourds went around encircling it ten to a cubit
1Kings7:25 It stood on twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 It was a handbreadth thick
1Kings7:27 Then he made the ten stands of bronze; the length of each stand was four cubits and its width four cubits and its height three cubits.
1Kings7:28 This was the design of the stands: they had borders
1Kings7:29 and on the borders which were between the frames were lions
1Kings7:30 Now each stand had four bronze wheels with bronze axles
1Kings7:31 Its opening inside the crown at the top was a cubit
1Kings7:32 The four wheels were underneath the borders
1Kings7:33 The workmanship of the wheels was like the workmanship of a chariot wheel. Their axles
1Kings7:34 Now there were four supports at the four corners of each stand; its supports were part of the stand itself.
1Kings7:35 On the top of the stand there was a circular form half a cubit high
1Kings7:36 He engraved on the plates of its stays and on its borders
1Kings7:37 He made the ten stands like this: all of them had one casting
1Kings7:38 He made ten basins of bronze
1Kings7:39 Then he set the stands
1Kings7:40 Now Hiram made the basins and the shovels and the bowls. So Hiram finished doing all the work which he performed for King Solomon in the house of the LORD:
1Kings7:41 the two pillars and the two bowls of the capitals which were on the top of the two pillars
1Kings7:42 and the four hundred pomegranates for the two networks
1Kings7:43 and the ten stands with the ten basins on the stands;
1Kings7:44 and the one sea and the twelve oxen under the sea;
1Kings7:45 and the pails and the shovels and the bowls; even all these utensils which Hiram made for King Solomon in the house of the LORD were of polished bronze.
1Kings7:46 In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them
1Kings7:47 Solomon left all the utensils unweighed
1Kings7:48 Solomon made all the furniture which was in the house of the LORD: the golden altar and the golden table on which was the bread of the Presence;
1Kings7:49 and the lampstands
1Kings7:50 and the cups and the snuffers and the bowls and the spoons and the firepans
1Kings7:51 Thus all the work that King Solomon performed in the house of the LORD was finished. And Solomon brought in the things dedicated by his father David

1Kings8:8 But the poles were so long that the ends of the poles could be seen from the holy place before the inner sanctuary
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets of stone which Moses put there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 It happened that when the priests came from the holy place
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud

1Kings8:19 'Nevertheless you shall not build the house

1Kings8:21 There I have set a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in the presence of all the assembly of Israel and spread out his hands toward heaven.
1Kings8:23 He said
1Kings8:24 who have kept with Your servant
1Kings8:25 Now therefore
1Kings8:26 Now therefore
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Behold
1Kings8:28 Yet have regard to the prayer of Your servant and to his supplication
1Kings8:29 that Your eyes may be open toward this house night and day
1Kings8:30 Listen to the supplication of Your servant and of Your people Israel
1Kings8:31 If a man sins against his neighbor and is made to take an oath
1Kings8:32 then hear in heaven and act and judge Your servants
1Kings8:33 When Your people Israel are defeated before an enemy
1Kings8:34 then hear in heaven
1Kings8:35 When the heavens are shut up and there is no rain
1Kings8:36 then hear in heaven and forgive the sin of Your servants and of Your people Israel
1Kings8:37 If there is famine in the land
1Kings8:38 whatever prayer or supplication is made by any man or by all Your people Israel
1Kings8:39 then hear in heaven Your dwelling place
1Kings8:40 that they may fear You all the days that they live in the land which You have given to our fathers.
1Kings8:41 Also concerning the foreigner who is not of Your people Israel
1Kings8:42 (for they will hear of Your great name and Your mighty hand
1Kings8:43 hear in heaven Your dwelling place
1Kings8:44 When Your people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 then hear in heaven their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:46 When they sin against You (for there is no man who does not sin) and You are angry with them and deliver them to an enemy
1Kings8:47 if they take thought in the land where they have been taken captive
1Kings8:48 if they return to You with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their enemies who have taken them captive
1Kings8:49 then hear their prayer and their supplication in heaven Your dwelling place
1Kings8:50 and forgive Your people who have sinned against You and all their transgressions which they have transgressed against You
1Kings8:51 (for they are Your people and Your inheritance which You have brought forth from Egypt
1Kings8:52 that Your eyes may be open to the supplication of Your servant and to the supplication of Your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For You have separated them from all the peoples of the earth as Your inheritance
1Kings8:54 When Solomon had finished praying this entire prayer and supplication to the LORD
1Kings8:55 And he stood and blessed all the assembly of Israel with a loud voice
1Kings8:56 Blessed be the LORD
1Kings8:57 May the LORD our God be with us
1Kings8:58 that He may incline our hearts to Himself
1Kings8:59 And may these words of mine
1Kings8:60 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the LORD is God; there is no one else.
1Kings8:61 Let your heart therefore be wholly devoted to the LORD our God

1Kings8:63 Solomon offered for the sacrifice of peace offerings
1Kings8:64 On the same day the king consecrated the middle of the court that was before the house of the LORD
1Kings8:65 So Solomon observed the feast at that time
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away and they blessed the king. Then they went to their tents joyful and glad of heart for all the goodness that the LORD had shown to David His servant and to Israel His people.

2Kings23:35 So Jehoiakim gave the silver and gold to Pharaoh
2Kings23:36 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king
2Kings23:37 He did evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:1 In his days Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up
2Kings24:2 The LORD sent against him bands of Chaldeans
2Kings24:3 Surely at the command of the LORD it came upon Judah
2Kings24:4 and also for the innocent blood which he shed
2Kings24:5 Now the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim and all that he did
2Kings24:6 So Jehoiakim slept with his fathers
2Kings24:7 The king of Egypt did not come out of his land again
2Kings24:8 Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he became king
2Kings24:9 He did evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:10 At that time the servants of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon went up to Jerusalem
2Kings24:11 And Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon came to the city
2Kings24:12 Jehoiachin the king of Judah went out to the king of Babylon
2Kings24:13 He carried out from there all the treasures of the house of the LORD
2Kings24:14 Then he led away into exile all Jerusalem and all the captains and all the mighty men of valor
2Kings24:15 So he led Jehoiachin away into exile to Babylon; also the king's mother and the king's wives and his officials and the leading men of the land
2Kings24:16 All the men of valor
2Kings24:17 Then the king of Babylon made his uncle Mattaniah king in his place
2Kings24:18 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
2Kings24:19 He did evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:20 For through the anger of the LORD this came about in Jerusalem and Judah until He cast them out from His presence. And Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
2Kings25:1 Now in the ninth year of his reign
2Kings25:2 So the city was under siege until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
2Kings25:3 On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
2Kings25:4 Then the city was broken into
2Kings25:5 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook him in the plains of Jericho and all his army was scattered from him.
2Kings25:6 Then they captured the king and brought him to the king of Babylon at Riblah
2Kings25:7 They slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
2Kings25:8 Now on the seventh day of the fifth month
2Kings25:9 He burned the house of the LORD
2Kings25:10 So all the army of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the guard broke down the walls around Jerusalem.
2Kings25:11 Then the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had deserted to the king of Babylon and the rest of the people
2Kings25:12 But the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and plowmen.
2Kings25:13 Now the bronze pillars which were in the house of the LORD
2Kings25:14 They took away the pots
2Kings25:15 The captain of the guard also took away the firepans and the basins
2Kings25:16 The two pillars
2Kings25:17 The height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits
2Kings25:18 Then the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest and Zephaniah the second priest
2Kings25:19 From the city he took one official who was overseer of the men of war
2Kings25:20 Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
2Kings25:21 Then the king of Babylon struck them down and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Judah was led away into exile from its land.
2Kings25:22 Now as for the people who were left in the land of Judah
2Kings25:23 When all the captains of the forces
2Kings25:24 Gedaliah swore to them and their men and said to them
2Kings25:25 But it came about in the seventh month
2Kings25:26 Then all the people
2Kings25:27 Now it came about in the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin king of Judah
2Kings25:28 and he spoke kindly to him and set his throne above the throne of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
2Kings25:29 Jehoiachin changed his prison clothes and had his meals in the king's presence regularly all the days of his life;
2Kings25:30 and for his allowance

2Chronicles3:2 He began to build on the second day in the second month of the fourth year of his reign.

2Chronicles3:4 The porch which was in front of the house was as long as the width of the house

2Chronicles3:9 The weight of the nails was fifty shekels of gold. He also overlaid the upper rooms with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 Then he made two sculptured cherubim in the room of the holy of holies and overlaid them with gold.

2Chronicles3:12 The wing of the other cherub
2Chronicles3:13 The wings of these cherubim extended twenty cubits

2Chronicles3:17 He erected the pillars in front of the temple

2Chronicles6:34 When Your people go out to battle against their enemies

2Chronicles32:4 So many people assembled and stopped up all the springs and the stream which flowed through the region
2Chronicles32:5 And he took courage and rebuilt all the wall that had been broken down and erected towers on it
2Chronicles32:6 He appointed military officers over the people and gathered them to him in the square at the city gate

2Chronicles32:8 With him is only an arm of flesh

2Chronicles32:15 'Now therefore

2Chronicles32:20 But King Hezekiah and Isaiah the prophet
2Chronicles32:21 And the LORD sent an angel who destroyed every mighty warrior

2Chronicles32:26 However

Ezra4:5 and hired counselors against them to frustrate their counsel all the days of Cyrus king of Persia
Ezra4:6 Now in the reign of Ahasuerus
Ezra4:7 And in the days of Artaxerxes
Ezra4:8 Rehum the commander and Shimshai the scribe wrote a letter against Jerusalem to King Artaxerxes
Ezra4:9 then wrote Rehum the commander and Shimshai the scribe and the rest of their colleagues
Ezra4:10 and the rest of the nations which the great and honorable Osnappar deported and settled in the city of Samaria
Ezra4:11 this is the copy of the letter which they sent to him: To King Artaxerxes: Your servants
Ezra4:12 let it be known to the king that the Jews who came up from you have come to us at Jerusalem; they are rebuilding the rebellious and evil city and are finishing the walls and repairing the foundations.
Ezra4:13 Now let it be known to the king
Ezra4:14 Now because we are in the service of the palace
Ezra4:15 so that a search may be made in the record books of your fathers. And you will discover in the record books and learn that that city is a rebellious city and damaging to kings and provinces
Ezra4:16 We inform the king that if that city is rebuilt and the walls finished
Ezra4:17 Then the king sent an answer to Rehum the commander
Ezra4:18 the document which you sent to us has been translated and read before me.
Ezra4:19 A decree has been issued by me
Ezra4:20 that mighty kings have ruled over Jerusalem
Ezra4:21 So
Ezra4:22 Beware of being negligent in carrying out this matter; why should damage increase to the detriment of the kings?
Ezra4:23 Then as soon as the copy of King Artaxerxes' document was read before Rehum and Shimshai the scribe and their colleagues
Ezra4:24 Then work on the house of God in Jerusalem ceased

Ezra6:1 Then King Darius issued a decree
Ezra6:2 In Ecbatana in the fortress
Ezra6:3 In the first year of King Cyrus
Ezra6:4 with three layers of huge stones and one layer of timbers. And let the cost be paid from the royal treasury.
Ezra6:5 'Also let the gold and silver utensils of the house of God
Ezra6:6 Now therefore
Ezra6:7 Leave this work on the house of God alone; let the governor of the Jews and the elders of the Jews rebuild this house of God on its site.
Ezra6:8 Moreover
Ezra6:9 Whatever is needed
Ezra6:10 that they may offer acceptable sacrifices to the God of heaven and pray for the life of the king and his sons.
Ezra6:11 And I issued a decree that any man who violates this edict
Ezra6:12 May the God who has caused His name to dwell there overthrow any king or people who attempts to change it
Ezra6:13 Then Tattenai
Ezra6:14 And the elders of the Jews were successful in building through the prophesying of Haggai the prophet and Zechariah the son of Iddo. And they finished building according to the command of the God of Israel and the decree of Cyrus
Ezra6:15 This temple was completed on the third day of the month Adar; it was the sixth year of the reign of King Darius.
Ezra6:16 And the sons of Israel
Ezra6:17 They offered for the dedication of this temple of God 100 bulls
Ezra6:18 Then they appointed the priests to their divisions and the Levites in their orders for the service of God in Jerusalem
Ezra6:19 The exiles observed the Passover on the fourteenth of the first month.
Ezra6:20 For the priests and the Levites had purified themselves together; all of them were pure. Then they slaughtered the Passover lamb for all the exiles
Ezra6:21 The sons of Israel who returned from exile and all those who had separated themselves from the impurity of the nations of the land to join them
Ezra6:22 And they observed the Feast of Unleavened Bread seven days with joy

Ezra8:20 and 220 of the temple servants

Ezra8:24 Then I set apart twelve of the leading priests

Ezra8:31 Then we journeyed from the river Ahava on the twelfth of the first month to go to Jerusalem; and the hand of our God was over us
Ezra8:32 Thus we came to Jerusalem and remained there three days.

Ezra8:34 Everything was numbered and weighed

Ezra8:36 Then they delivered the king's edicts to the king's satraps and to the governors in the provinces beyond the River

Nehemiah8:15 So they proclaimed and circulated a proclamation in all their cities and in Jerusalem
Nehemiah8:16 So the people went out and brought them and made booths for themselves
Nehemiah8:17 The entire assembly of those who had returned from the captivity made booths and lived in them. The sons of Israel had indeed not done so from the days of Joshua the son of Nun to that day. And there was great rejoicing.

Nehemiah9:25 They captured fortified cities and a fertile land. They took possession of houses full of every good thing
Nehemiah9:26 But they became disobedient and rebelled against You
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore You delivered them into the hand of their oppressors who oppressed them
Nehemiah9:28 But as soon as they had rest
Nehemiah9:29 And admonished them in order to turn them back to Your law. Yet they acted arrogantly and did not listen to Your commandments but sinned against Your ordinances

Esther1:1 Now it took place in the days of Ahasuerus
Esther1:2 in those days as King Ahasuerus sat on his royal throne which was at the citadel in Susa
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign he gave a banquet for all his princes and attendants
Esther1:4 And he displayed the riches of his royal glory and the splendor of his great majesty for many days
Esther1:5 When these days were completed
Esther1:6 There were hangings of fine white and violet linen held by cords of fine purple linen on silver rings and marble columns
Esther1:7 Drinks were served in golden vessels of various kinds
Esther1:8 The drinking was done according to the law
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also gave a banquet for the women in the palace which belonged to King Ahasuerus.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti before the king with her royal crown in order to display her beauty to the people and the princes
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's command delivered by the eunuchs. Then the king became very angry and his wrath burned within him.
Esther1:13 Then the king said to the wise men who understood the times--for it was the custom of the king so to speak before all who knew law and justice
Esther1:14 and were close to him: Carshena
Esther1:15 According to law
Esther1:16 In the presence of the king and the princes
Esther1:17 For the queen's conduct will become known to all the women causing them to look with contempt on their husbands by saying
Esther1:18 This day the ladies of Persia and Media who have heard of the queen's conduct will speak in the same way to all the king's princes
Esther1:19 If it pleases the king
Esther1:20 When the king's edict which he will make is heard throughout all his kingdom
Esther1:21 This word pleased the king and the princes
Esther1:22 So he sent letters to all the king's provinces
Esther2:1 After these things when the anger of King Ahasuerus had subsided
Esther2:2 Then the king's attendants
Esther2:3 Let the king appoint overseers in all the provinces of his kingdom that they may gather every beautiful young virgin to the citadel of Susa
Esther2:4 Then let the young lady who pleases the king be queen in place of Vashti. And the matter pleased the king
Esther2:5 Now there was at the citadel in Susa a Jew whose name was Mordecai
Esther2:6 who had been taken into exile from Jerusalem with the captives who had been exiled with Jeconiah king of Judah
Esther2:7 He was bringing up Hadassah
Esther2:8 So it came about when the command and decree of the king were heard and many young ladies were gathered to the citadel of Susa into the custody of Hegai
Esther2:9 Now the young lady pleased him and found favor with him. So he quickly provided her with her cosmetics and food
Esther2:10 Esther did not make known her people or her kindred
Esther2:11 Every day Mordecai walked back and forth in front of the court of the harem to learn how Esther was and how she fared.
Esther2:12 Now when the turn of each young lady came to go in to King Ahasuerus
Esther2:13 the young lady would go in to the king in this way: anything that she desired was given her to take with her from the harem to the king's palace.
Esther2:14 In the evening she would go in and in the morning she would return to the second harem
Esther2:15 Now when the turn of Esther
Esther2:16 So Esther was taken to King Ahasuerus to his royal palace in the tenth month which is the month Tebeth
Esther2:17 The king loved Esther more than all the women
Esther2:18 Then the king gave a great banquet
Esther2:19 When the virgins were gathered together the second time
Esther2:20 Esther had not yet made known her kindred or her people
Esther2:21 In those days
Esther2:22 But the plot became known to Mordecai and he told Queen Esther
Esther2:23 Now when the plot was investigated and found to be so

Job1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job; and that man was blameless
Job1:2 Seven sons and three daughters were born to him.
Job1:3 His possessions also were 7
Job1:4 His sons used to go and hold a feast in the house of each one on his day
Job1:5 When the days of feasting had completed their cycle
Job1:6 Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD
Job1:7 The LORD said to Satan
Job1:8 The LORD said to Satan
Job1:9 Then Satan answered the LORD
Job1:10 Have You not made a hedge about him and his house and all that he has
Job1:11 But put forth Your hand now and touch all that he has; he will surely curse You to Your face.
Job1:12 Then the LORD said to Satan
Job1:13 Now on the day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother's house
Job1:14 a messenger came to Job and said
Job1:15 and the Sabeans attacked and took them. They also slew the servants with the edge of the sword
Job1:16 While he was still speaking
Job1:17 While he was still speaking
Job1:18 While he was still speaking
Job1:19 and behold
Job1:20 Then Job arose and tore his robe and shaved his head
Job1:21 He said
Job1:22 Through all this Job did not sin nor did he blame God.
Job2:1 Again there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD
Job2:2 The LORD said to Satan
Job2:3 The LORD said to Satan
Job2:4 Satan answered the LORD and said
Job2:5 However
Job2:6 So the LORD said to Satan
Job2:7 Then Satan went out from the presence of the LORD and smote Job with sore boils from the sole of his foot to the crown of his head.
Job2:8 And he took a potsherd to scrape himself while he was sitting among the ashes.
Job2:9 Then his wife said to him
Job2:10 But he said to her
Job2:11 Now when Job's three friends heard of all this adversity that had come upon him
Job2:12 When they lifted up their eyes at a distance and did not recognize him
Job2:13 Then they sat down on the ground with him for seven days and seven nights with no one speaking a word to him
Job3:1 Afterward Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.
Job3:2 And Job said
Job3:3 Let the day perish on which I was to be born
Job3:4 May that day be darkness; Let not God above care for it
Job3:5 Let darkness and black gloom claim it; Let a cloud settle on it; Let the blackness of the day terrify it.
Job3:6 As for that night
Job3:7 Behold
Job3:8 Let those curse it who curse the day
Job3:9 Let the stars of its twilight be darkened; Let it wait for light but have none
Job3:10 Because it did not shut the opening of my mother's womb
Job3:11 Why did I not die at birth
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me
Job3:13 For now I would have lain down and been quiet; I would have slept then
Job3:14 With kings and with counselors of the earth
Job3:15 Or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or like a miscarriage which is discarded
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from raging
Job3:18 The prisoners are at ease together; They do not hear the voice of the taskmaster.
Job3:19 The small and the great are there
Job3:20 Why is light given to him who suffers
Job3:21 Who long for death
Job3:22 Who rejoice greatly
Job3:23 Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden
Job3:24 For my groaning comes at the sight of my food
Job3:25 For what I fear comes upon me
Job3:26 I am not at ease
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered
Job4:2 If one ventures a word with you
Job4:3 Behold you have admonished many
Job4:4 Your words have helped the tottering to stand
Job4:5 But now it has come to you
Job4:6 Is not your fear of God your confidence
Job4:7 Remember now
Job4:8 According to what I have seen
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 The roaring of the lion and the voice of the fierce lion
Job4:11 The lion perishes for lack of prey
Job4:12 Now a word was brought to me stealthily
Job4:13 Amid disquieting thoughts from the visions of the night
Job4:14 Dread came upon me
Job4:15 Then a spirit passed by my face; The hair of my flesh bristled up.
Job4:16 It stood still
Job4:17 'Can mankind be just before God? Can a man be pure before his Maker?
Job4:18 'He puts no trust even in His servants; And against His angels He charges error.
Job4:19 'How much more those who dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 'Between morning and evening they are broken in pieces; Unobserved
Job4:21 'Is not their tent-cord plucked up within them? They die
Job5:1 Call now
Job5:2 For anger slays the foolish man
Job5:3 I have seen the foolish taking root
Job5:4 His sons are far from safety
Job5:5 His harvest the hungry devour And take it to a place of thorns
Job5:6 For affliction does not come from the dust
Job5:7 For man is born for trouble
Job5:8 But as for me
Job5:9 Who does great and unsearchable things
Job5:10 He gives rain on the earth And sends water on the fields
Job5:11 So that He sets on high those who are lowly
Job5:12 He frustrates the plotting of the shrewd
Job5:13 He captures the wise by their own shrewdness
Job5:14 By day they meet with darkness
Job5:15 But He saves from the sword of their mouth
Job5:16 So the helpless has hope
Job5:17 Behold
Job5:18 For He inflicts pain
Job5:19 From six troubles He will deliver you
Job5:20 In famine He will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You will be hidden from the scourge of the tongue
Job5:22 You will laugh at violence and famine
Job5:23 For you will be in league with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You will know that your tent is secure
Job5:25 You will know also that your descendants will be many
Job5:26 You will come to the grave in full vigor
Job5:27 Behold this; we have investigated it
Job6:1 Then Job answered
Job6:2 Oh that my grief were actually weighed And laid in the balances together with my calamity!
Job6:3 For then it would be heavier than the sand of the seas; Therefore my words have been rash.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are within me
Job6:5 Does the wild donkey bray over his grass
Job6:6 Can something tasteless be eaten without salt
Job6:7 My soul refuses to touch them; They are like loathsome food to me.
Job6:8 Oh that my request might come to pass
Job6:9 Would that God were willing to crush me
Job6:10 But it is still my consolation
Job6:11 What is my strength
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones
Job6:13 Is it that my help is not within me
Job6:14 For the despairing man there should be kindness from his friend; So that he does not forsake the fear of the Almighty.
Job6:15 My brothers have acted deceitfully like a wadi
Job6:16 Which are turbid because of ice And into which the snow melts.
Job6:17 When they become waterless
Job6:18 The paths of their course wind along
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema looked
Job6:20 They were disappointed for they had trusted
Job6:21 Indeed
Job6:22 Have I said
Job6:23 Or
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How painful are honest words! But what does your argument prove?
Job6:26 Do you intend to reprove my words
Job6:27 You would even cast lots for the orphans And barter over your friend.
Job6:28 Now please look at me
Job6:29 Desist now
Job6:30 Is there injustice on my tongue? Cannot my palate discern calamities?
Job7:1 Is not man forced to labor on earth
Job7:2 As a slave who pants for the shade
Job7:3 So am I allotted months of vanity
Job7:4 When I lie down I say
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms and a crust of dirt
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle
Job7:7 Remember that my life is but breath; My eye will not again see good.
Job7:8 The eye of him who sees me will behold me no longer; Your eyes will be on me
Job7:9 When a cloud vanishes
Job7:10 He will not return again to his house
Job7:11 Therefore I will not restrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit
Job7:12 Am I the sea
Job7:13 If I say
Job7:14 Then You frighten me with dreams And terrify me by visions;
Job7:15 So that my soul would choose suffocation
Job7:16 I waste away; I will not live forever. Leave me alone
Job7:17 What is man that You magnify him
Job7:18 That You examine him every morning And try him every moment?
Job7:19 Will You never turn Your gaze away from me
Job7:20 Have I sinned? What have I done to You
Job7:21 Why then do You not pardon my transgression And take away my iniquity? For now I will lie down in the dust; And You will seek me
Job8:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered
Job8:2 How long will you say these things
Job8:3 Does God pervert justice? Or does the Almighty pervert what is right?
Job8:4 If your sons sinned against Him
Job8:5 If you would seek God And implore the compassion of the Almighty
Job8:6 If you are pure and upright
Job8:7 Though your beginning was insignificant
Job8:8 Please inquire of past generations
Job8:9 For we are only of yesterday and know nothing
Job8:10 Will they not teach you and tell you
Job8:11 Can the papyrus grow up without a marsh? Can the rushes grow without water?
Job8:12 While it is still green and not cut down
Job8:13 So are the paths of all who forget God; And the hope of the godless will perish
Job8:14 Whose confidence is fragile
Job8:15 He trusts in his house
Job8:16 He thrives before the sun
Job8:17 His roots wrap around a rock pile
Job8:18 If he is removed from his place
Job8:19 Behold
Job8:20 Lo
Job8:21 He will yet fill your mouth with laughter And your lips with shouting.
Job8:22 Those who hate you will be clothed with shame
Job9:1 Then Job answered
Job9:2 In truth I know that this is so; But how can a man be in the right before God?
Job9:3 If one wished to dispute with Him
Job9:4 Wise in heart and mighty in strength
Job9:5 It is God who removes the mountains
Job9:6 Who shakes the earth out of its place
Job9:7 Who commands the sun not to shine
Job9:8 Who alone stretches out the heavens And tramples down the waves of the sea;
Job9:9 Who makes the Bear
Job9:10 Who does great things
Job9:11 Were He to pass by me
Job9:12 Were He to snatch away
Job9:13 God will not turn back His anger; Beneath Him crouch the helpers of Rahab.
Job9:14 How then can I answer Him
Job9:15 For though I were right
Job9:16 If I called and He answered me
Job9:17 For He bruises me with a tempest And multiplies my wounds without cause.
Job9:18 He will not allow me to get my breath
Job9:19 If it is a matter of power
Job9:20 Though I am righteous
Job9:21 I am guiltless; I do not take notice of myself; I despise my life.
Job9:22 It is all one; therefore I say
Job9:23 If the scourge kills suddenly
Job9:24 The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; He covers the faces of its judges. If it is not He
Job9:25 Now my days are swifter than a runner; They flee away
Job9:26 They slip by like reed boats
Job9:27 Though I say
Job9:28 I am afraid of all my pains
Job9:29 I am accounted wicked
Job9:30 If I should wash myself with snow And cleanse my hands with lye
Job9:31 Yet You would plunge me into the pit
Job9:32 For He is not a man as I am that I may answer Him
Job9:33 There is no umpire between us
Job9:34 Let Him remove His rod from me
Job9:35 Then I would speak and not fear Him; But I am not like that in myself.
Job10:1 I loathe my own life; I will give full vent to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say to God
Job10:3 'Is it right for You indeed to oppress
Job10:4 'Have You eyes of flesh? Or do You see as a man sees?
Job10:5 'Are Your days as the days of a mortal
Job10:6 That You should seek for my guilt And search after my sin?
Job10:7 'According to Your knowledge I am indeed not guilty
Job10:8 'Your hands fashioned and made me altogether
Job10:9 'Remember now
Job10:10 'Did You not pour me out like milk And curdle me like cheese;
Job10:11 Clothe me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 'You have granted me life and lovingkindness; And Your care has preserved my spirit.
Job10:13 'Yet these things You have concealed in Your heart; I know that this is within You:
Job10:14 If I sin
Job10:15 'If I am wicked
Job10:16 'Should my head be lifted up
Job10:17 'You renew Your witnesses against me And increase Your anger toward me; Hardship after hardship is with me.
Job10:18 'Why then have You brought me out of the womb? Would that I had died and no eye had seen me!
Job10:19 'I should have been as though I had not been
Job10:20 Would He not let my few days alone? Withdraw from me that I may have a little cheer
Job10:21 Before I go--and I shall not return--To the land of darkness and deep shadow
Job10:22 The land of utter gloom as darkness itself
Job11:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered
Job11:2 Shall a multitude of words go unanswered
Job11:3 Shall your boasts silence men? And shall you scoff and none rebuke?
Job11:4 For you have said
Job11:5 But would that God might speak
Job11:6 And show you the secrets of wisdom! For sound wisdom has two sides. Know then that God forgets a part of your iniquity.
Job11:7 Can you discover the depths of God? Can you discover the limits of the Almighty?
Job11:8 They are high as the heavens
Job11:9 Its measure is longer than the earth And broader than the sea.
Job11:10 If He passes by or shuts up
Job11:11 For He knows false men
Job11:12 An idiot will become intelligent When the foal of a wild donkey is born a man.
Job11:13 If you would direct your heart right And spread out your hand to Him
Job11:14 If iniquity is in your hand
Job11:15 Then
Job11:16 For you would forget your trouble
Job11:17 Your life would be brighter than noonday; Darkness would be like the morning.
Job11:18 Then you would trust
Job11:19 You would lie down and none would disturb you
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked will fail
Job12:1 Then Job responded
Job12:2 Truly then you are the people
Job12:3 But I have intelligence as well as you; I am not inferior to you. And who does not know such things as these?
Job12:4 I am a joke to my friends
Job12:5 He who is at ease holds calamity in contempt
Job12:6 The tents of the destroyers prosper
Job12:7 But now ask the beasts
Job12:8 Or speak to the earth
Job12:9 Who among all these does not know That the hand of the LORD has done this
Job12:10 In whose hand is the life of every living thing
Job12:11 Does not the ear test words
Job12:12 Wisdom is with aged men
Job12:13 With Him are wisdom and might; To Him belong counsel and understanding.
Job12:14 Behold
Job12:15 Behold
Job12:16 With Him are strength and sound wisdom
Job12:17 He makes counselors walk barefoot And makes fools of judges.
Job12:18 He loosens the bond of kings And binds their loins with a girdle.
Job12:19 He makes priests walk barefoot And overthrows the secure ones.
Job12:20 He deprives the trusted ones of speech And takes away the discernment of the elders.
Job12:21 He pours contempt on nobles And loosens the belt of the strong.
Job12:22 He reveals mysteries from the darkness And brings the deep darkness into light.
Job12:23 He makes the nations great
Job12:24 He deprives of intelligence the chiefs of the earth's people And makes them wander in a pathless waste.
Job12:25 They grope in darkness with no light
Job13:1 Behold
Job13:2 What you know I also know; I am not inferior to you.
Job13:3 But I would speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 But you smear with lies; You are all worthless physicians.
Job13:5 O that you would be completely silent
Job13:6 Please hear my argument And listen to the contentions of my lips.
Job13:7 Will you speak what is unjust for God
Job13:8 Will you show partiality for Him? Will you contend for God?
Job13:9 Will it be well when He examines you? Or will you deceive Him as one deceives a man?
Job13:10 He will surely reprove you If you secretly show partiality.
Job13:11 Will not His majesty terrify you
Job13:12 Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes
Job13:13 Be silent before me so that I may speak; Then let come on me what may.
Job13:14 Why should I take my flesh in my teeth And put my life in my hands?
Job13:15 Though He slay me
Job13:16 This also will be my salvation
Job13:17 Listen carefully to my speech
Job13:18 Behold now
Job13:19 Who will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
Job13:20 Only two things do not do to me
Job13:21 Remove Your hand from me
Job13:22 Then call
Job13:23 How many are my iniquities and sins? Make known to me my rebellion and my sin.
Job13:24 Why do You hide Your face And consider me Your enemy?
Job13:25 Will You cause a driven leaf to tremble? Or will You pursue the dry chaff?
Job13:26 For You write bitter things against me And make me to inherit the iniquities of my youth.
Job13:27 You put my feet in the stocks And watch all my paths; You set a limit for the soles of my feet
Job13:28 While I am decaying like a rotten thing
Job14:1 Man
Job14:2 Like a flower he comes forth and withers. He also flees like a shadow and does not remain.
Job14:3 You also open Your eyes on him And bring him into judgment with Yourself.
Job14:4 Who can make the clean out of the unclean? No one!
Job14:5 Since his days are determined
Job14:6 Turn Your gaze from him that he may rest
Job14:7 For there is hope for a tree
Job14:8 Though its roots grow old in the ground And its stump dies in the dry soil
Job14:9 At the scent of water it will flourish And put forth sprigs like a plant.
Job14:10 But man dies and lies prostrate. Man expires
Job14:11 As water evaporates from the sea
Job14:12 So man lies down and does not rise. Until the heavens are no longer
Job14:13 Oh that You would hide me in Sheol
Job14:14 If a man dies
Job14:15 You will call
Job14:16 For now You number my steps
Job14:17 My transgression is sealed up in a bag
Job14:18 But the falling mountain crumbles away
Job14:19 Water wears away stones
Job14:20 You forever overpower him and he departs; You change his appearance and send him away.
Job14:21 His sons achieve honor
Job14:22 But his body pains him
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite responded
Job15:2 Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge And fill himself with the east wind?
Job15:3 Should he argue with useless talk
Job15:4 Indeed
Job15:5 For your guilt teaches your mouth
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 Were you the first man to be born
Job15:8 Do you hear the secret counsel of God
Job15:9 What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that we do not?
Job15:10 Both the gray-haired and the aged are among us
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God too small for you
Job15:12 Why does your heart carry you away? And why do your eyes flash
Job15:13 That you should turn your spirit against God And allow such words to go out of your mouth?
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 Behold
Job15:16 How much less one who is detestable and corrupt
Job15:17 I will tell you
Job15:18 What wise men have told
Job15:19 To whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked man writhes in pain all his days
Job15:21 Sounds of terror are in his ears; While at peace the destroyer comes upon him.
Job15:22 He does not believe that he will return from darkness
Job15:23 He wanders about for food
Job15:24 Distress and anguish terrify him
Job15:25 Because he has stretched out his hand against God And conducts himself arrogantly against the Almighty.
Job15:26 He rushes headlong at Him With his massive shield.
Job15:27 For he has covered his face with his fat And made his thighs heavy with flesh.
Job15:28 He has lived in desolate cities
Job15:29 He will not become rich
Job15:30 He will not escape from darkness; The flame will wither his shoots
Job15:31 Let him not trust in emptiness
Job15:32 It will be accomplished before his time
Job15:33 He will drop off his unripe grape like the vine
Job15:34 For the company of the godless is barren
Job15:35 They conceive mischief and bring forth iniquity
Job16:1 Then Job answered
Job16:2 I have heard many such things; Sorry comforters are you all.
Job16:3 Is there no limit to windy words? Or what plagues you that you answer?
Job16:4 I too could speak like you
Job16:5 I could strengthen you with my mouth
Job16:6 If I speak
Job16:7 But now He has exhausted me; You have laid waste all my company.
Job16:8 You have shriveled me up
Job16:9 His anger has torn me and hunted me down
Job16:10 They have gaped at me with their mouth
Job16:11 God hands me over to ruffians And tosses me into the hands of the wicked.
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 His arrows surround me. Without mercy He splits my kidneys open; He pours out my gall on the ground.
Job16:14 He breaks through me with breach after breach; He runs at me like a warrior.
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth over my skin And thrust my horn in the dust.
Job16:16 My face is flushed from weeping
Job16:17 Although there is no violence in my hands
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends are my scoffers; My eye weeps to God.
Job16:21 O that a man might plead with God As a man with his neighbor!
Job16:22 For when a few years are past
Job17:1 My spirit is broken
Job17:2 Surely mockers are with me
Job17:3 Lay down
Job17:4 For You have kept their heart from understanding
Job17:5 He who informs against friends for a share of the spoil
Job17:6 But He has made me a byword of the people
Job17:7 My eye has also grown dim because of grief
Job17:8 The upright will be appalled at this
Job17:9 Nevertheless the righteous will hold to his way
Job17:10 But come again all of you now
Job17:11 My days are past
Job17:12 They make night into day
Job17:13 If I look for Sheol as my home
Job17:14 If I call to the pit
Job17:15 Where now is my hope? And who regards my hope?
Job17:16 Will it go down with me to Sheol? Shall we together go down into the dust?
Job18:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite responded
Job18:2 How long will you hunt for words? Show understanding and then we can talk.
Job18:3 Why are we regarded as beasts
Job18:4 O you who tear yourself in your anger--For your sake is the earth to be abandoned
Job18:5 Indeed
Job18:6 The light in his tent is darkened
Job18:7 His vigorous stride is shortened
Job18:8 For he is thrown into the net by his own feet

Job18:10 A noose for him is hidden in the ground
Job18:11 All around terrors frighten him
Job18:12 His strength is famished
Job18:13 His skin is devoured by disease
Job18:14 He is torn from the security of his tent

Job18:17 Memory of him perishes from the earth

Job18:19 He has no offspring or posterity among his people

Job18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked
Job19:1 Then Job responded
Job19:2 How long will you torment me And crush me with words?
Job19:3 These ten times you have insulted me; You are not ashamed to wrong me.
Job19:4 Even if I have truly erred
Job19:5 If indeed you vaunt yourselves against me And prove my disgrace to me
Job19:6 Know then that God has wronged me And has closed His net around me.
Job19:7 Behold
Job19:8 He has walled up my way so that I cannot pass
Job19:9 He has stripped my honor from me And removed the crown from my head.
Job19:10 He breaks me down on every side
Job19:11 He has also kindled His anger against me And considered me as His enemy.
Job19:12 His troops come together
Job19:13 He has removed my brothers far from me
Job19:14 My relatives have failed
Job19:15 Those who live in my house and my maids consider me a stranger. I am a foreigner in their sight.

Job19:17 My breath is offensive to my wife
Job19:18 Even young children despise me; I rise up and they speak against me.
Job19:19 All my associates abhor me
Job19:20 My bone clings to my skin and my flesh

Job19:26 Even after my skin is destroyed
Job19:27 Whom I myself shall behold
Job19:28 If you say
Job19:29 Then be afraid of the sword for yourselves
Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered
Job20:2 Therefore my disquieting thoughts make me respond
Job20:3 I listened to the reproof which insults me
Job20:4 Do you know this from of old
Job20:5 That the triumphing of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Though his loftiness reaches the heavens
Job20:7 He perishes forever like his refuse; Those who have seen him will say
Job20:8 He flies away like a dream
Job20:9 The eye which saw him sees him no longer
Job20:10 His sons favor the poor
Job20:11 His bones are full of his youthful vigor
Job20:12 Though evil is sweet in his mouth And he hides it under his tongue
Job20:13 Though he desires it and will not let it go
Job20:14 Yet his food in his stomach is changed To the venom of cobras within him.
Job20:15 He swallows riches
Job20:16 He sucks the poison of cobras; The viper's tongue slays him.
Job20:17 He does not look at the streams
Job20:18 He returns what he has attained And cannot swallow it; As to the riches of his trading
Job20:19 For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor; He has seized a house which he has not built.
Job20:20 Because he knew no quiet within him
Job20:21 Nothing remains for him to devour
Job20:22 In the fullness of his plenty he will be cramped; The hand of everyone who suffers will come against him.
Job20:23 When he fills his belly
Job20:24 He may flee from the iron weapon
Job20:25 It is drawn forth and comes out of his back
Job20:26 Complete darkness is held in reserve for his treasures
Job20:27 The heavens will reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The increase of his house will depart; His possessions will flow away in the day of His anger.
Job20:29 This is the wicked man's portion from God
Job21:1 Then Job answered
Job21:2 Listen carefully to my speech
Job21:3 Bear with me that I may speak; Then after I have spoken
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Look at me
Job21:6 Even when I remember
Job21:7 Why do the wicked still live
Job21:8 Their descendants are established with them in their sight
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 His ox mates without fail; His cow calves and does not abort.
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like the flock
Job21:12 They sing to the timbrel and harp And rejoice at the sound of the flute.

Job21:14 They say to God

Job21:24 His sides are filled out with fat
Job21:25 While another dies with a bitter soul
Job21:26 Together they lie down in the dust

Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked wayfaring men

Job21:33 The clods of the valley will gently cover him; Moreover
