Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite responded
Job22:2 Can a vigorous man be of use to God
Job22:3 Is there any pleasure to the Almighty if you are righteous
Job22:4 Is it because of your reverence that He reproves you
Job22:5 Is not your wickedness great
Job22:6 For you have taken pledges of your brothers without cause
Job22:7 To the weary you have given no water to drink
Job22:8 But the earth belongs to the mighty man
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty
Job22:10 Therefore snares surround you
Job22:11 Or darkness
Job22:12 Is not God in the height of heaven? Look also at the distant stars
Job22:13 You say
Job22:14 'Clouds are a hiding place for Him
Job22:15 Will you keep to the ancient path Which wicked men have trod
Job22:16 Who were snatched away before their time
Job22:17 They said to God
Job22:18 Yet He filled their houses with good things; But the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
Job22:19 The righteous see and are glad
Job22:20 Saying
Job22:21 Yield now and be at peace with Him; Thereby good will come to you.
Job22:22 Please receive instruction from His mouth And establish His words in your heart.
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty
Job22:24 And place your gold in the dust
Job22:25 Then the Almighty will be your gold And choice silver to you.
Job22:26 For then you will delight in the Almighty And lift up your face to God.
Job22:27 You will pray to Him
Job22:28 You will also decree a thing
Job22:29 When you are cast down
Job22:30 He will deliver one who is not innocent
Job23:1 Then Job replied
Job23:2 Even today my complaint is rebellion; His hand is heavy despite my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh that I knew where I might find Him
Job23:4 I would present my case before Him And fill my mouth with arguments.
Job23:5 I would learn the words which He would answer
Job23:6 Would He contend with me by the greatness of His power? No
Job23:7 There the upright would reason with Him; And I would be delivered forever from my Judge.
Job23:8 Behold
Job23:9 When He acts on the left
Job23:10 But He knows the way I take; When He has tried me
Job23:11 My foot has held fast to His path; I have kept His way and not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the command of His lips; I have treasured the words of His mouth more than my necessary food.
Job23:13 But He is unique and who can turn Him? And what His soul desires
Job23:14 For He performs what is appointed for me
Job23:15 Therefore
Job23:16 It is God who has made my heart faint
Job23:17 But I am not silenced by the darkness
Job24:1 Why are times not stored up by the Almighty
Job24:2 Some remove the landmarks; They seize and devour flocks.
Job24:3 They drive away the donkeys of the orphans; They take the widow's ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They push the needy aside from the road; The poor of the land are made to hide themselves altogether.
Job24:5 Behold
Job24:6 They harvest their fodder in the field And glean the vineyard of the wicked.
Job24:7 They spend the night naked
Job24:8 They are wet with the mountain rains And hug the rock for want of a shelter.
Job24:9 Others snatch the orphan from the breast
Job24:10 They cause the poor to go about naked without clothing
Job24:11 Within the walls they produce oil; They tread wine presses but thirst.
Job24:12 From the city men groan
Job24:13 Others have been with those who rebel against the light; They do not want to know its ways Nor abide in its paths.
Job24:14 The murderer arises at dawn; He kills the poor and the needy
Job24:15 The eye of the adulterer waits for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they dig into houses
Job24:17 For the morning is the same to him as thick darkness
Job24:18 They are insignificant on the surface of the water; Their portion is cursed on the earth. They do not turn toward the vineyards.
Job24:19 Drought and heat consume the snow waters
Job24:20 A mother will forget him; The worm feeds sweetly till he is no longer remembered. And wickedness will be broken like a tree.
Job24:21 He wrongs the barren woman And does no good for the widow.
Job24:22 But He drags off the valiant by His power; He rises
Job24:23 He provides them with security
Job24:24 They are exalted a little while
Job24:25 Now if it is not so
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered
Job25:2 Dominion and awe belong to Him Who establishes peace in His heights.
Job25:3 Is there any number to His troops? And upon whom does His light not rise?
Job25:4 How then can a man be just with God? Or how can he be clean who is born of woman?
Job25:5 If even the moon has no brightness And the stars are not pure in His sight
Job25:6 How much less man
Job26:1 Then Job responded
Job26:2 What a help you are to the weak! How you have saved the arm without strength!
Job26:3 What counsel you have given to one without wisdom! What helpful insight you have abundantly provided!
Job26:4 To whom have you uttered words? And whose spirit was expressed through you?
Job26:5 The departed spirits tremble Under the waters and their inhabitants.
Job26:6 Naked is Sheol before Him
Job26:7 He stretches out the north over empty space And hangs the earth on nothing.
Job26:8 He wraps up the waters in His clouds
Job26:9 He obscures the face of the full moon And spreads His cloud over it.
Job26:10 He has inscribed a circle on the surface of the waters At the boundary of light and darkness.
Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble And are amazed at His rebuke.
Job26:12 He quieted the sea with His power
Job26:13 By His breath the heavens are cleared; His hand has pierced the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 Behold
Job27:1 Then Job continued his discourse and said
Job27:2 As God lives
Job27:3 For as long as life is in me
Job27:4 My lips certainly will not speak unjustly
Job27:5 Far be it from me that I should declare you right; Till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
Job27:6 I hold fast my righteousness and will not let it go. My heart does not reproach any of my days.
Job27:7 May my enemy be as the wicked And my opponent as the unjust.
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the godless when he is cut off
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry When distress comes upon him?
Job27:10 Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call on God at all times?
Job27:11 I will instruct you in the power of God; What is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 This is the portion of a wicked man from God
Job27:14 Though his sons are many
Job27:15 His survivors will be buried because of the plague
Job27:16 Though he piles up silver like dust And prepares garments as plentiful as the clay
Job27:17 He may prepare it
Job27:18 He has built his house like the spider's web
Job27:19 He lies down rich
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like a flood; A tempest steals him away in the night.
Job27:21 The east wind carries him away
Job27:22 For it will hurl at him without sparing; He will surely try to flee from its power.
Job27:23 Men will clap their hands at him And will hiss him from his place.
Job28:1 Surely there is a mine for silver And a place where they refine gold.
Job28:2 Iron is taken from the dust
Job28:3 Man puts an end to darkness
Job28:4 He sinks a shaft far from habitation
Job28:5 The earth
Job28:6 Its rocks are the source of sapphires
Job28:7 The path no bird of prey knows
Job28:8 The proud beasts have not trodden it
Job28:9 He puts his hand on the flint; He overturns the mountains at the base.
Job28:10 He hews out channels through the rocks
Job28:11 He dams up the streams from flowing
Job28:12 But where can wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man does not know its value
Job28:14 The deep says
Job28:15 Pure gold cannot be given in exchange for it
Job28:16 It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold or glass cannot equal it
Job28:18 Coral and crystal are not to be mentioned; And the acquisition of wisdom is above that of pearls.
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia cannot equal it
Job28:20 Where then does wisdom come from? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 Thus it is hidden from the eyes of all living And concealed from the birds of the sky.
Job28:22 Abaddon and Death say
Job28:23 God understands its way
Job28:24 For He looks to the ends of the earth And sees everything under the heavens.
Job28:25 When He imparted weight to the wind And meted out the waters by measure
Job28:26 When He set a limit for the rain And a course for the thunderbolt
Job28:27 Then He saw it and declared it; He established it and also searched it out.
Job28:28 And to man He said
Job29:1 And Job again took up his discourse and said
Job29:2 Oh that I were as in months gone by
Job29:3 When His lamp shone over my head
Job29:4 As I was in the prime of my days
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me
Job29:6 When my steps were bathed in butter
Job29:7 When I went out to the gate of the city
Job29:8 The young men saw me and hid themselves
Job29:9 The princes stopped talking And put their hands on their mouths;
Job29:10 The voice of the nobles was hushed
Job29:11 For when the ear heard
Job29:12 Because I delivered the poor who cried for help
Job29:13 The blessing of the one ready to perish came upon me
Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind And feet to the lame.
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy
Job29:17 I broke the jaws of the wicked And snatched the prey from his teeth.
Job29:18 Then I thought
Job29:19 'My root is spread out to the waters
Job29:20 'My glory is ever new with me
Job29:21 To me they listened and waited
Job29:22 After my words they did not speak again
Job29:23 They waited for me as for the rain
Job29:24 I smiled on them when they did not believe
Job29:25 I chose a way for them and sat as chief
Job30:1 But now those younger than I mock me
Job30:2 Indeed
Job30:3 From want and famine they are gaunt Who gnaw the dry ground by night in waste and desolation
Job30:4 Who pluck mallow by the bushes
Job30:5 They are driven from the community; They shout against them as against a thief
Job30:6 So that they dwell in dreadful valleys
Job30:7 Among the bushes they cry out; Under the nettles they are gathered together.
Job30:8 Fools
Job30:9 And now I have become their taunt
Job30:10 They abhor me and stand aloof from me
Job30:11 Because He has loosed His bowstring and afflicted me
Job30:12 On the right hand their brood arises; They thrust aside my feet and build up against me their ways of destruction.
Job30:13 They break up my path
Job30:14 As through a wide breach they come
Job30:15 Terrors are turned against me; They pursue my honor as the wind
Job30:16 And now my soul is poured out within me; Days of affliction have seized me.
Job30:17 At night it pierces my bones within me
Job30:18 By a great force my garment is distorted; It binds me about as the collar of my coat.
Job30:19 He has cast me into the mire
Job30:20 I cry out to You for help
Job30:21 You have become cruel to me; With the might of Your hand You persecute me.
Job30:22 You lift me up to the wind and cause me to ride; And You dissolve me in a storm.
Job30:23 For I know that You will bring me to death And to the house of meeting for all living.
Job30:24 Yet does not one in a heap of ruins stretch out his hand
Job30:25 Have I not wept for the one whose life is hard? Was not my soul grieved for the needy?
Job30:26 When I expected good
Job30:27 I am seething within and cannot relax; Days of affliction confront me.
Job30:28 I go about mourning without comfort; I stand up in the assembly and cry out for help.
Job30:29 I have become a brother to jackals And a companion of ostriches.
Job30:30 My skin turns black on me
Job30:31 Therefore my harp is turned to mourning
Job31:1 I have made a covenant with my eyes; How then could I gaze at a virgin?
Job31:2 And what is the portion of God from above Or the heritage of the Almighty from on high?
Job31:3 Is it not calamity to the unjust And disaster to those who work iniquity?
Job31:4 Does He not see my ways And number all my steps?
Job31:5 If I have walked with falsehood
Job31:6 Let Him weigh me with accurate scales
Job31:7 If my step has turned from the way
Job31:8 Let me sow and another eat
Job31:9 If my heart has been enticed by a woman
Job31:10 May my wife grind for another
Job31:11 For that would be a lustful crime; Moreover
Job31:12 For it would be fire that consumes to Abaddon
Job31:13 If I have despised the claim of my male or female slaves When they filed a complaint against me
Job31:14 What then could I do when God arises? And when He calls me to account
Job31:15 Did not He who made me in the womb make him
Job31:16 If I have kept the poor from their desire
Job31:17 Or have eaten my morsel alone
Job31:18 (But from my youth he grew up with me as with a father
Job31:19 If I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing
Job31:20 If his loins have not thanked me
Job31:21 If I have lifted up my hand against the orphan
Job31:22 Let my shoulder fall from the socket
Job31:23 For calamity from God is a terror to me
Job31:24 If I have put my confidence in gold
Job31:25 If I have gloated because my wealth was great
Job31:26 If I have looked at the sun when it shone Or the moon going in splendor
Job31:27 And my heart became secretly enticed
Job31:28 That too would have been an iniquity calling for judgment
Job31:29 Have I rejoiced at the extinction of my enemy
Job31:30 No
Job31:31 Have the men of my tent not said
Job31:32 The alien has not lodged outside
Job31:33 Have I covered my transgressions like Adam
Job31:34 Because I feared the great multitude
Job31:35 Oh that I had one to hear me! Behold
Job31:36 Surely I would carry it on my shoulder
Job31:37 I would declare to Him the number of my steps; Like a prince I would approach Him.
Job31:38 If my land cries out against me
Job31:39 If I have eaten its fruit without money
Job31:40 Let briars grow instead of wheat
Job32:1 Then these three men ceased answering Job
Job32:2 But the anger of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite
Job32:3 And his anger burned against his three friends because they had found no answer
Job32:4 Now Elihu had waited to speak to Job because they were years older than he.
Job32:5 And when Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of the three men his anger burned.
Job32:6 So Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite spoke out and said
Job32:7 I thought age should speak
Job32:8 But it is a spirit in man
Job32:9 The abundant in years may not be wise
Job32:10 So I say
Job32:11 Behold
Job32:12 I even paid close attention to you; Indeed
Job32:13 Do not say
Job32:14 For he has not arranged his words against me
Job32:15 They are dismayed
Job32:16 Shall I wait
Job32:17 I too will answer my share
Job32:18 For I am full of words; The spirit within me constrains me.
Job32:19 Behold
Job32:20 Let me speak that I may get relief; Let me open my lips and answer.
Job32:21 Let me now be partial to no one
Job32:22 For I do not know how to flatter

Job33:2 Behold now

Job33:4 The Spirit of God has made me

Job33:7 Behold
Job33:8 Surely you have spoken in my hearing
Job33:9 'I am pure
Job33:10 'Behold

Job33:12 Behold

Job33:15 In a dream

Job33:17 That He may turn man aside from his conduct

Job33:20 So that his life loathes bread
Job33:21 His flesh wastes away from sight
Job33:22 Then his soul draws near to the pit
Job33:23 If there is an angel as mediator for him
Job33:24 Then let him be gracious to him
Job33:25 Let his flesh become fresher than in youth
Job33:26 Then he will pray to God
Job33:27 He will sing to men and say
Job33:28 'He has redeemed my soul from going to the pit

Job33:30 To bring back his soul from the pit
Job33:31 Pay attention

Job34:1 Then Elihu continued and said
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 For the ear tests words As the palate tastes food.
Job34:4 Let us choose for ourselves what is right; Let us know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 For Job has said
Job34:6 Should I lie concerning my right? My wound is incurable
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 Who goes in company with the workers of iniquity
Job34:9 For he has said
Job34:10 Therefore
Job34:11 For He pays a man according to his work
Job34:12 Surely
Job34:13 Who gave Him authority over the earth? And who has laid on Him the whole world?
Job34:14 If He should determine to do so
Job34:15 All flesh would perish together
Job34:16 But if you have understanding
Job34:17 Shall one who hates justice rule? And will you condemn the righteous mighty One
Job34:18 Who says to a king
Job34:19 Who shows no partiality to princes Nor regards the rich above the poor
Job34:20 In a moment they die
Job34:21 For His eyes are upon the ways of a man
Job34:22 There is no darkness or deep shadow Where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.
Job34:23 For He does not need to consider a man further
Job34:24 He breaks in pieces mighty men without inquiry
Job34:25 Therefore He knows their works
Job34:26 He strikes them like the wicked In a public place
Job34:27 Because they turned aside from following Him
Job34:28 So that they caused the cry of the poor to come to Him
Job34:29 When He keeps quiet
Job34:30 So that godless men would not rule Nor be snares of the people.
Job34:31 For has anyone said to God
Job34:32 Teach me what I do not see; If I have done iniquity
Job34:33 Shall He recompense on your terms
Job34:34 Men of understanding will say to me
Job34:35 'Job speaks without knowledge
Job34:36 'Job ought to be tried to the limit
Job34:37 'For he adds rebellion to his sin; He claps his hands among us
Job35:1 Then Elihu continued and said
Job35:2 Do you think this is according to justice? Do you say
Job35:3 For you say
Job35:4 I will answer you
Job35:5 Look at the heavens and see; And behold the clouds--they are higher than you.
Job35:6 If you have sinned
Job35:7 If you are righteous
Job35:8 Your wickedness is for a man like yourself
Job35:9 Because of the multitude of oppressions they cry out; They cry for help because of the arm of the mighty.
Job35:10 But no one says
Job35:11 Who teaches us more than the beasts of the earth And makes us wiser than the birds of the heavens?'
Job35:12 There they cry out
Job35:13 Surely God will not listen to an empty cry
Job35:14 How much less when you say you do not behold Him
Job35:15 And now
Job35:16 So Job opens his mouth emptily; He multiplies words without knowledge.
Job36:1 Then Elihu continued and said
Job36:2 Wait for me a little
Job36:3 I will fetch my knowledge from afar
Job36:4 For truly my words are not false; One who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
Job36:5 Behold
Job36:6 He does not keep the wicked alive
Job36:7 He does not withdraw His eyes from the righteous; But with kings on the throne He has seated them forever
Job36:8 And if they are bound in fetters
Job36:9 Then He declares to them their work And their transgressions
Job36:10 He opens their ear to instruction
Job36:11 If they hear and serve Him
Job36:12 But if they do not hear
Job36:13 But the godless in heart lay up anger; They do not cry for help when He binds them.
Job36:14 They die in youth
Job36:15 He delivers the afflicted in their affliction
Job36:16 Then indeed
Job36:17 But you were full of judgment on the wicked; Judgment and justice take hold of you.
Job36:18 Beware that wrath does not entice you to scoffing; And do not let the greatness of the ransom turn you aside.
Job36:19 Will your riches keep you from distress
Job36:20 Do not long for the night
Job36:21 Be careful
Job36:22 Behold
Job36:23 Who has appointed Him His way
Job36:24 Remember that you should exalt His work
Job36:25 All men have seen it; Man beholds from afar.
Job36:26 Behold

Job36:28 Which the clouds pour down
Job36:29 Can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds
Job36:30 Behold
Job36:31 For by these He judges peoples; He gives food in abundance.
Job36:32 He covers His hands with the lightning
Job36:33 Its noise declares His presence; The cattle also
Job37:1 At this also my heart trembles
Job37:2 Listen closely to the thunder of His voice
Job37:3 Under the whole heaven He lets it loose
Job37:4 After it
Job37:5 God thunders with His voice wondrously
Job37:6 For to the snow He says
Job37:7 He seals the hand of every man
Job37:8 Then the beast goes into its lair And remains in its den.
Job37:9 Out of the south comes the storm
Job37:10 From the breath of God ice is made
Job37:11 Also with moisture He loads the thick cloud; He disperses the cloud of His lightning.
Job37:12 It changes direction
Job37:13 Whether for correction
Job37:14 Listen to this
Job37:15 Do you know how God establishes them
Job37:16 Do you know about the layers of the thick clouds
Job37:17 You whose garments are hot
Job37:18 Can you
Job37:19 Teach us what we shall say to Him; We cannot arrange our case because of darkness.
Job37:20 Shall it be told Him that I would speak? Or should a man say that he would be swallowed up?
Job37:21 Now men do not see the light which is bright in the skies; But the wind has passed and cleared them.
Job37:22 Out of the north comes golden splendor; Around God is awesome majesty.
Job37:23 The Almighty--we cannot find Him; He is exalted in power And He will not do violence to justice and abundant righteousness.
Job37:24 Therefore men fear Him; He does not regard any who are wise of heart.
Job38:1 Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind and said
Job38:2 Who is this that darkens counsel By words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Now gird up your loins like a man
Job38:4 Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell Me
Job38:5 Who set its measurements? Since you know. Or who stretched the line on it?
Job38:6 On what were its bases sunk? Or who laid its cornerstone
Job38:7 When the morning stars sang together And all the sons of God shouted for joy?
Job38:8 Or who enclosed the sea with doors When
Job38:9 When I made a cloud its garment And thick darkness its swaddling band
Job38:10 And I placed boundaries on it And set a bolt and doors
Job38:11 And I said
Job38:12 Have you ever in your life commanded the morning
Job38:13 That it might take hold of the ends of the earth
Job38:14 It is changed like clay under the seal; And they stand forth like a garment.
Job38:15 From the wicked their light is withheld
Job38:16 Have you entered into the springs of the sea Or walked in the recesses of the deep?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you
Job38:18 Have you understood the expanse of the earth? Tell Me
Job38:19 Where is the way to the dwelling of light? And darkness
Job38:20 That you may take it to its territory And that you may discern the paths to its home?
Job38:21 You know
Job38:22 Have you entered the storehouses of the snow
Job38:23 Which I have reserved for the time of distress
Job38:24 Where is the way that the light is divided
Job38:25 Who has cleft a channel for the flood
Job38:26 To bring rain on a land without people
Job38:27 To satisfy the waste and desolate land And to make the seeds of grass to sprout?
Job38:28 Has the rain a father? Or who has begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 From whose womb has come the ice? And the frost of heaven
Job38:30 Water becomes hard like stone
Job38:31 Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades
Job38:32 Can you lead forth a constellation in its season
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of the heavens
Job38:34 Can you lift up your voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send forth lightnings that they may go And say to you
Job38:36 Who has put wisdom in the innermost being Or given understanding to the mind?
Job38:37 Who can count the clouds by wisdom
Job38:38 When the dust hardens into a mass And the clods stick together?
Job38:39 Can you hunt the prey for the lion
Job38:40 When they crouch in their dens And lie in wait in their lair?
Job38:41 Who prepares for the raven its nourishment When its young cry to God And wander about without food?
Job39:1 Do you know the time the mountain goats give birth? Do you observe the calving of the deer?
Job39:2 Can you count the months they fulfill
Job39:3 They kneel down
Job39:4 Their offspring become strong
Job39:5 Who sent out the wild donkey free? And who loosed the bonds of the swift donkey
Job39:6 To whom I gave the wilderness for a home And the salt land for his dwelling place?
Job39:7 He scorns the tumult of the city
Job39:8 He explores the mountains for his pasture And searches after every green thing.
Job39:9 Will the wild ox consent to serve you
Job39:10 Can you bind the wild ox in a furrow with ropes
Job39:11 Will you trust him because his strength is great And leave your labor to him?
Job39:12 Will you have faith in him that he will return your grain And gather it from your threshing floor?
Job39:13 The ostriches' wings flap joyously With the pinion and plumage of love
Job39:14 For she abandons her eggs to the earth And warms them in the dust
Job39:15 And she forgets that a foot may crush them
Job39:16 She treats her young cruelly
Job39:17 Because God has made her forget wisdom
Job39:18 When she lifts herself on high
Job39:19 Do you give the horse his might? Do you clothe his neck with a mane?
Job39:20 Do you make him leap like the locust? His majestic snorting is terrible.
Job39:21 He paws in the valley
Job39:22 He laughs at fear and is not dismayed; And he does not turn back from the sword.
Job39:23 The quiver rattles against him
Job39:24 With shaking and rage he races over the ground
Job39:25 As often as the trumpet sounds he says
Job39:26 Is it by your understanding that the hawk soars
Job39:27 Is it at your command that the eagle mounts up And makes his nest on high?
Job39:28 On the cliff he dwells and lodges
Job39:29 From there he spies out food; His eyes see it from afar.
Job39:30 His young ones also suck up blood; And where the slain are
Job40:1 Then the LORD said to Job
Job40:2 Will the faultfinder contend with the Almighty? Let him who reproves God answer it.
Job40:3 Then Job answered the LORD and said
Job40:4 Behold
Job40:5 Once I have spoken
Job40:6 Then the LORD answered Job out of the storm and said
Job40:7 Now gird up your loins like a man; I will ask you
Job40:8 Will you really annul My judgment? Will you condemn Me that you may be justified?
Job40:9 Or do you have an arm like God
Job40:10 Adorn yourself with eminence and dignity
Job40:11 Pour out the overflowings of your anger
Job40:12 Look on everyone who is proud
Job40:13 Hide them in the dust together; Bind them in the hidden place.
Job40:14 Then I will also confess to you
Job40:15 Behold now
Job40:16 Behold now
Job40:17 He bends his tail like a cedar; The sinews of his thighs are knit together.
Job40:18 His bones are tubes of bronze; His limbs are like bars of iron.
Job40:19 He is the first of the ways of God; Let his maker bring near his sword.
Job40:20 Surely the mountains bring him food
Job40:21 Under the lotus plants he lies down
Job40:22 The lotus plants cover him with shade; The willows of the brook surround him.
Job40:23 If a river rages
Job40:24 Can anyone capture him when he is on watch
Job41:1 Can you draw out Leviathan with a fishhook? Or press down his tongue with a cord?
Job41:2 Can you put a rope in his nose Or pierce his jaw with a hook?
Job41:3 Will he make many supplications to you
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with you? Will you take him for a servant forever?
Job41:5 Will you play with him as with a bird
Job41:6 Will the traders bargain over him? Will they divide him among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill his skin with harpoons
Job41:8 Lay your hand on him; Remember the battle; you will not do it again!
Job41:9 Behold
Job41:10 No one is so fierce that he dares to arouse him; Who then is he that can stand before Me?
Job41:11 Who has given to Me that I should repay him? Whatever is under the whole heaven is Mine.
Job41:12 I will not keep silence concerning his limbs
Job41:13 Who can strip off his outer armor? Who can come within his double mail?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of his face? Around his teeth there is terror.
Job41:15 His strong scales are his pride
Job41:16 One is so near to another That no air can come between them.
Job41:17 They are joined one to another; They clasp each other and cannot be separated.
Job41:18 His sneezes flash forth light
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go burning torches; Sparks of fire leap forth.
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils smoke goes forth As from a boiling pot and burning rushes.
Job41:21 His breath kindles coals
Job41:22 In his neck lodges strength
Job41:23 The folds of his flesh are joined together
Job41:24 His heart is as hard as a stone
Job41:25 When he raises himself up
Job41:26 The sword that reaches him cannot avail
Job41:27 He regards iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make him flee; Slingstones are turned into stubble for him.
Job41:29 Clubs are regarded as stubble; He laughs at the rattling of the javelin.
Job41:30 His underparts are like sharp potsherds; He spreads out like a threshing sledge on the mire.
Job41:31 He makes the depths boil like a pot; He makes the sea like a jar of ointment.
Job41:32 Behind him he makes a wake to shine; One would think the deep to be gray-haired.
Job41:33 Nothing on earth is like him
Job41:34 He looks on everything that is high; He is king over all the sons of pride.
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD and said
Job42:2 I know that You can do all things
Job42:3 'Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?'Therefore I have declared that which I did not understand
Job42:4 'Hear
Job42:5 I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear; But now my eye sees You;
Job42:6 Therefore I retract
Job42:7 It came about after the LORD had spoken these words to Job
Job42:8 Now therefore
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as the LORD told them; and the LORD accepted Job.
Job42:10 The LORD restored the fortunes of Job when he prayed for his friends
Job42:11 Then all his brothers and all his sisters and all who had known him before came to him
Job42:12 The LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning; and he had 14
Job42:13 He had seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 He named the first Jemimah
Job42:15 In all the land no women were found so fair as Job's daughters; and their father gave them inheritance among their brothers.
Job42:16 After this
Job42:17 And Job died
Psalms1:1 How blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked
Psalms1:2 But his delight is in the law of the LORD
Psalms1:3 He will be like a tree firmly planted by streams of water
Psalms1:4 The wicked are not so
Psalms1:5 Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment
Psalms1:6 For the LORD knows the way of the righteous
Psalms2:1 Why are the nations in an uproar And the peoples devising a vain thing?
Psalms2:2 The kings of the earth take their stand And the rulers take counsel together Against the LORD and against His Anointed
Psalms2:3 Let us tear their fetters apart And cast away their cords from us!
Psalms2:4 He who sits in the heavens laughs
Psalms2:5 Then He will speak to them in His anger And terrify them in His fury
Psalms2:6 But as for Me
Psalms2:7 I will surely tell of the decree of the LORD: He said to Me
Psalms2:8 'Ask of Me
Psalms2:9 'You shall break them with a rod of iron
Psalms2:10 Now therefore
Psalms2:11 Worship the LORD with reverence And rejoice with trembling.
Psalms2:12 Do homage to the Son
Psalms3:1 O LORD
Psalms3:2 Many are saying of my soul
Psalms3:3 But You
Psalms3:4 I was crying to the LORD with my voice
Psalms3:5 I lay down and slept; I awoke
Psalms3:6 I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people Who have set themselves against me round about.
Psalms3:7 Arise
Psalms3:8 Salvation belongs to the LORD; Your blessing be upon Your people! Selah.
Psalms4:1 Answer me when I call
Psalms4:2 O sons of men
Psalms4:3 But know that the LORD has set apart the godly man for Himself; The LORD hears when I call to Him.
Psalms4:4 Tremble
Psalms4:5 Offer the sacrifices of righteousness
Psalms4:6 Many are saying
Psalms4:7 You have put gladness in my heart
Psalms4:8 In peace I will both lie down and sleep
Psalms5:1 Give ear to my words
Psalms5:2 Heed the sound of my cry for help
Psalms5:3 In the morning
Psalms5:4 For You are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness; No evil dwells with You.
Psalms5:5 The boastful shall not stand before Your eyes; You hate all who do iniquity.
Psalms5:6 You destroy those who speak falsehood; The LORD abhors the man of bloodshed and deceit.
Psalms5:7 But as for me
Psalms5:8 O LORD
Psalms5:9 There is nothing reliable in what they say; Their inward part is destruction itself. Their throat is an open grave; They flatter with their tongue.
Psalms5:10 Hold them guilty
Psalms5:11 But let all who take refuge in You be glad
Psalms5:12 For it is You who blesses the righteous man
Psalms6:1 O LORD
Psalms6:2 Be gracious to me
Psalms6:3 And my soul is greatly dismayed; But You
Psalms6:4 Return
Psalms6:5 For there is no mention of You in death; In Sheol who will give You thanks?
Psalms6:6 I am weary with my sighing; Every night I make my bed swim
Psalms6:7 My eye has wasted away with grief; It has become old because of all my adversaries.
Psalms6:8 Depart from me
Psalms6:9 The LORD has heard my supplication
Psalms6:10 All my enemies will be ashamed and greatly dismayed; They shall turn back
Psalms7:1 O LORD my God
Psalms7:2 Or he will tear my soul like a lion
Psalms7:3 O LORD my God
Psalms7:4 If I have rewarded evil to my friend
Psalms7:5 Let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it; And let him trample my life down to the ground And lay my glory in the dust. Selah.
Psalms7:6 Arise
Psalms7:7 Let the assembly of the peoples encompass You
Psalms7:8 The LORD judges the peoples; Vindicate me
Psalms7:9 O let the evil of the wicked come to an end
Psalms7:10 My shield is with God
Psalms7:11 God is a righteous judge
Psalms7:12 If a man does not repent
Psalms7:13 He has also prepared for Himself deadly weapons; He makes His arrows fiery shafts.
Psalms7:14 Behold
Psalms7:15 He has dug a pit and hollowed it out
Psalms7:16 His mischief will return upon his own head
Psalms7:17 I will give thanks to the LORD according to His righteousness And will sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.
Psalms8:1 O LORD
Psalms8:2 From the mouth of infants and nursing babes You have established strength Because of Your adversaries
Psalms8:3 When I consider Your heavens
Psalms8:4 What is man that You take thought of him
Psalms8:5 Yet You have made him a little lower than God
Psalms8:6 You make him to rule over the works of Your hands; You have put all things under his feet
Psalms8:7 All sheep and oxen
Psalms8:8 The birds of the heavens and the fish of the sea
Psalms8:9 O LORD
Psalms9:1 I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart; I will tell of all Your wonders.
Psalms9:2 I will be glad and exult in You; I will sing praise to Your name
Psalms9:3 When my enemies turn back
Psalms9:4 For You have maintained my just cause; You have sat on the throne judging righteously.
Psalms9:5 You have rebuked the nations
Psalms9:6 The enemy has come to an end in perpetual ruins
Psalms9:7 But the LORD abides forever; He has established His throne for judgment
Psalms9:8 And He will judge the world in righteousness; He will execute judgment for the peoples with equity.
Psalms9:9 The LORD also will be a stronghold for the oppressed
Psalms9:10 And those who know Your name will put their trust in You
Psalms9:11 Sing praises to the LORD
Psalms9:12 For He who requires blood remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
Psalms9:13 Be gracious to me
Psalms9:14 That I may tell of all Your praises
Psalms9:15 The nations have sunk down in the pit which they have made; In the net which they hid
Psalms9:16 The LORD has made Himself known; He has executed judgment. In the work of his own hands the wicked is snared. Higgaion Selah.
Psalms9:17 The wicked will return to Sheol
Psalms9:18 For the needy will not always be forgotten
Psalms9:19 Arise
Psalms9:20 Put them in fear
Psalms10:1 Why do You stand afar off
Psalms10:2 In pride the wicked hotly pursue the afflicted; Let them be caught in the plots which they have devised.
Psalms10:3 For the wicked boasts of his heart's desire
Psalms10:4 The wicked
Psalms10:5 His ways prosper at all times; Your judgments are on high
Psalms10:6 He says to himself
Psalms10:7 His mouth is full of curses and deceit and oppression; Under his tongue is mischief and wickedness.
Psalms10:8 He sits in the lurking places of the villages; In the hiding places he kills the innocent; His eyes stealthily watch for the unfortunate.
Psalms10:9 He lurks in a hiding place as a lion in his lair; He lurks to catch the afflicted; He catches the afflicted when he draws him into his net.
Psalms10:10 He crouches
Psalms10:11 He says to himself
Psalms10:12 Arise
Psalms10:13 Why has the wicked spurned God? He has said to himself
Psalms10:14 You have seen it
Psalms10:15 Break the arm of the wicked and the evildoer
Psalms10:16 The LORD is King forever and ever; Nations have perished from His land.
Psalms10:17 O LORD
Psalms10:18 To vindicate the orphan and the oppressed
Psalms11:1 In the LORD I take refuge; How can you say to my soul
Psalms11:2 For
Psalms11:3 If the foundations are destroyed
Psalms11:4 The LORD is in His holy temple; the LORD'S throne is in heaven; His eyes behold
Psalms11:5 The LORD tests the righteous and the wicked
Psalms11:6 Upon the wicked He will rain snares; Fire and brimstone and burning wind will be the portion of their cup.
Psalms11:7 For the LORD is righteous
Psalms12:1 Help
Psalms12:2 They speak falsehood to one another; With flattering lips and with a double heart they speak.
Psalms12:3 May the LORD cut off all flattering lips
Psalms12:4 Who have said
Psalms12:5 Because of the devastation of the afflicted
Psalms12:6 The words of the LORD are pure words; As silver tried in a furnace on the earth
Psalms12:7 You
Psalms12:8 The wicked strut about on every side When vileness is exalted among the sons of men.
Psalms13:1 How long
Psalms13:2 How long shall I take counsel in my soul
Psalms13:3 Consider and answer me
Psalms13:4 And my enemy will say
Psalms13:5 But I have trusted in Your lovingkindness; My heart shall rejoice in Your salvation.
Psalms13:6 I will sing to the LORD
Psalms14:1 The fool has said in his heart
Psalms14:2 The LORD has looked down from heaven upon the sons of men To see if there are any who understand
Psalms14:3 They have all turned aside
Psalms14:4 Do all the workers of wickedness not know
Psalms14:5 There they are in great dread
Psalms14:6 You would put to shame the counsel of the afflicted
Psalms14:7 Oh
Psalms15:1 O LORD
Psalms15:2 He who walks with integrity
Psalms15:3 He does not slander with his tongue
Psalms15:4 In whose eyes a reprobate is despised
Psalms15:5 He does not put out his money at interest
Psalms16:1 Preserve me
Psalms16:2 I said to the LORD
Psalms16:3 As for the saints who are in the earth
Psalms16:4 The sorrows of those who have bartered for another god will be multiplied; I shall not pour out their drink offerings of blood
Psalms16:5 The LORD is the portion of my inheritance and my cup; You support my lot.
Psalms16:6 The lines have fallen to me in pleasant places; Indeed
Psalms16:7 I will bless the LORD who has counseled me; Indeed
Psalms16:8 I have set the LORD continually before me; Because He is at my right hand
Psalms16:9 Therefore my heart is glad and my glory rejoices; My flesh also will dwell securely.
Psalms16:10 For You will not abandon my soul to Sheol; Nor will You allow Your Holy One to undergo decay.
Psalms16:11 You will make known to me the path of life; In Your presence is fullness of joy; In Your right hand there are pleasures forever.
Psalms17:1 Hear a just cause
Psalms17:2 Let my judgment come forth from Your presence; Let Your eyes look with equity.
Psalms17:3 You have tried my heart; You have visited me by night; You have tested me and You find nothing; I have purposed that my mouth will not transgress.
Psalms17:4 As for the deeds of men
Psalms17:5 My steps have held fast to Your paths. My feet have not slipped.
Psalms17:6 I have called upon You
Psalms17:7 Wondrously show Your lovingkindness
Psalms17:8 Keep me as the apple of the eye; Hide me in the shadow of Your wings
Psalms17:9 From the wicked who despoil me
Psalms17:10 They have closed their unfeeling heart
Psalms17:11 They have now surrounded us in our steps; They set their eyes to cast us down to the ground.
Psalms17:12 He is like a lion that is eager to tear
Psalms17:13 Arise
Psalms17:14 From men with Your hand
Psalms17:15 As for me
Psalms18:1 I love You
Psalms18:2 The LORD is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer
Psalms18:3 I call upon the LORD
Psalms18:4 The cords of death encompassed me
Psalms18:5 The cords of Sheol surrounded me; The snares of death confronted me.
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon the LORD
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and quaked; And the foundations of the mountains were trembling And were shaken
Psalms18:8 Smoke went up out of His nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens also
Psalms18:10 He rode upon a cherub and flew; And He sped upon the wings of the wind.
Psalms18:11 He made darkness His hiding place
Psalms18:12 From the brightness before Him passed His thick clouds
Psalms18:13 The LORD also thundered in the heavens
Psalms18:14 He sent out His arrows
Psalms18:15 Then the channels of water appeared
Psalms18:16 He sent from on high
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy
Psalms18:18 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
Psalms18:19 He brought me forth also into a broad place; He rescued me
Psalms18:20 The LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD
Psalms18:22 For all His ordinances were before me
Psalms18:23 I was also blameless with Him
Psalms18:24 Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the kind You show Yourself kind; With the blameless You show Yourself blameless;
Psalms18:26 With the pure You show Yourself pure
Psalms18:27 For You save an afflicted people
Psalms18:28 For You light my lamp; The LORD my God illumines my darkness.
Psalms18:29 For by You I can run upon a troop; And by my God I can leap over a wall.
Psalms18:30 As for God
Psalms18:31 For who is God
Psalms18:32 The God who girds me with strength And makes my way blameless?
Psalms18:33 He makes my feet like hinds' feet
Psalms18:34 He trains my hands for battle
Psalms18:35 You have also given me the shield of Your salvation
Psalms18:36 You enlarge my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I pursued my enemies and overtook them
Psalms18:38 I shattered them
Psalms18:39 For You have girded me with strength for battle; You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
Psalms18:40 You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me
Psalms18:41 They cried for help
Psalms18:42 Then I beat them fine as the dust before the wind; I emptied them out as the mire of the streets.
Psalms18:43 You have delivered me from the contentions of the people; You have placed me as head of the nations; A people whom I have not known serve me.
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear
Psalms18:45 Foreigners fade away
Psalms18:46 The LORD lives
Psalms18:47 The God who executes vengeance for me
Psalms18:48 He delivers me from my enemies; Surely You lift me above those who rise up against me; You rescue me from the violent man.
Psalms18:49 Therefore I will give thanks to You among the nations
Psalms18:50 He gives great deliverance to His king
Psalms19:1 The heavens are telling of the glory of God; And their expanse is declaring the work of His hands.
Psalms19:2 Day to day pours forth speech
Psalms19:3 There is no speech
Psalms19:4 Their line has gone out through all the earth
Psalms19:5 Which is as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber; It rejoices as a strong man to run his course.
Psalms19:6 Its rising is from one end of the heavens
Psalms19:7 The law of the LORD is perfect
Psalms19:8 The precepts of the LORD are right
Psalms19:9 The fear of the LORD is clean
Psalms19:10 They are more desirable than gold
Psalms19:11 Moreover
Psalms19:12 Who can discern his errors? Acquit me of hidden faults.
Psalms19:13 Also keep back Your servant from presumptuous sins; Let them not rule over me; Then I will be blameless
Psalms19:14 Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart Be acceptable in Your sight
Psalms20:1 May the LORD answer you in the day of trouble! May the name of the God of Jacob set you securely on high!
Psalms20:2 May He send you help from the sanctuary And support you from Zion!
Psalms20:3 May He remember all your meal offerings And find your burnt offering acceptable! Selah.
Psalms20:4 May He grant you your heart's desire And fulfill all your counsel!
Psalms20:5 We will sing for joy over your victory
Psalms20:6 Now I know that the LORD saves His anointed; He will answer him from His holy heaven With the saving strength of His right hand.
Psalms20:7 Some boast in chariots and some in horses
Psalms20:8 They have bowed down and fallen
Psalms20:9 Save
Psalms21:1 O LORD
Psalms21:2 You have given him his heart's desire
Psalms21:3 For You meet him with the blessings of good things; You set a crown of fine gold on his head.
Psalms21:4 He asked life of You
Psalms21:5 His glory is great through Your salvation
Psalms21:6 For You make him most blessed forever; You make him joyful with gladness in Your presence.
Psalms21:7 For the king trusts in the LORD
Psalms21:8 Your hand will find out all your enemies; Your right hand will find out those who hate you.
Psalms21:9 You will make them as a fiery oven in the time of your anger; The LORD will swallow them up in His wrath
Psalms21:10 Their offspring You will destroy from the earth
Psalms21:11 Though they intended evil against You And devised a plot
Psalms21:12 For You will make them turn their back; You will aim with Your bowstrings at their faces.
Psalms21:13 Be exalted
Psalms22:1 My God
Psalms22:2 O my God
Psalms22:3 Yet You are holy
Psalms22:4 In You our fathers trusted; They trusted and You delivered them.
Psalms22:5 To You they cried out and were delivered; In You they trusted and were not disappointed.
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm and not a man
Psalms22:7 All who see me sneer at me; They separate with the lip
Psalms22:8 Commit yourself to the LORD; let Him deliver him; Let Him rescue him
Psalms22:9 Yet You are He who brought me forth from the womb; You made me trust when upon my mother's breasts.
Psalms22:10 Upon You I was cast from birth; You have been my God from my mother's womb.
Psalms22:11 Be not far from me
Psalms22:12 Many bulls have surrounded me; Strong bulls of Bashan have encircled me.
Psalms22:13 They open wide their mouth at me
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd
Psalms22:16 For dogs have surrounded me; A band of evildoers has encompassed me; They pierced my hands and my feet.
Psalms22:17 I can count all my bones. They look
Psalms22:18 They divide my garments among them
Psalms22:19 But You
Psalms22:20 Deliver my soul from the sword
Psalms22:21 Save me from the lion's mouth; From the horns of the wild oxen You answer me.
Psalms22:22 I will tell of Your name to my brethren; In the midst of the assembly I will praise You.
Psalms22:23 You who fear the LORD
Psalms22:24 For He has not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; Nor has He hidden His face from him; But when he cried to Him for help
Psalms22:25 From You comes my praise in the great assembly; I shall pay my vows before those who fear Him.
Psalms22:26 The afflicted will eat and be satisfied; Those who seek Him will praise the LORD. Let your heart live forever!
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD
Psalms22:28 For the kingdom is the LORD'S And He rules over the nations.
Psalms22:29 All the prosperous of the earth will eat and worship
Psalms22:30 Posterity will serve Him; It will be told of the Lord to the coming generation.
Psalms22:31 They will come and will declare His righteousness To a people who will be born
Psalms23:1 The LORD is my shepherd
Psalms23:2 He makes me lie down in green pastures; He leads me beside quiet waters.
Psalms23:3 He restores my soul; He guides me in the paths of righteousness For His name's sake.
Psalms23:4 Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death
Psalms23:5 You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; You have anointed my head with oil; My cup overflows.
Psalms23:6 Surely goodness and lovingkindness will follow me all the days of my life

Psalms24:2 For He has founded it upon the seas And established it upon the rivers.
Psalms24:3 Who may ascend into the hill of the LORD? And who may stand in His holy place?
Psalms24:4 He who has clean hands and a pure heart
Psalms24:5 He shall receive a blessing from the LORD And righteousness from the God of his salvation.
Psalms24:6 This is the generation of those who seek Him
Psalms24:7 Lift up your heads
Psalms24:8 Who is the King of glory? The LORD strong and mighty
Psalms24:9 Lift up your heads
Psalms24:10 Who is this King of glory? The LORD of hosts

Psalms25:2 O my God
Psalms25:3 Indeed
Psalms25:4 Make me know Your ways
Psalms25:5 Lead me in Your truth and teach me
Psalms25:6 Remember
Psalms25:7 Do not remember the sins of my youth or my transgressions; According to Your lovingkindness remember me

Psalms25:9 He leads the humble in justice
Psalms25:10 All the paths of the LORD are lovingkindness and truth To those who keep His covenant and His testimonies.

Psalms25:12 Who is the man who fears the LORD? He will instruct him in the way he should choose.
Psalms25:13 His soul will abide in prosperity

Psalms26:10 In whose hands is a wicked scheme
Psalms26:11 But as for me
Psalms26:12 My foot stands on a level place; In the congregations I shall bless the LORD.
Psalms27:1 The LORD is my light and my salvation; Whom shall I fear? The LORD is the defense of my life; Whom shall I dread?
Psalms27:2 When evildoers came upon me to devour my flesh
Psalms27:3 Though a host encamp against me
Psalms27:4 One thing I have asked from the LORD
Psalms27:5 For in the day of trouble He will conceal me in His tabernacle; In the secret place of His tent He will hide me; He will lift me up on a rock.
Psalms27:6 And now my head will be lifted up above my enemies around me

Psalms27:9 Do not hide Your face from me
Psalms27:10 For my father and my mother have forsaken me

Psalms28:2 Hear the voice of my supplications when I cry to You for help
Psalms28:3 Do not drag me away with the wicked And with those who work iniquity

Psalms28:5 Because they do not regard the works of the LORD Nor the deeds of His hands
Psalms28:6 Blessed be the LORD
Psalms28:7 The LORD is my strength and my shield; My heart trusts in Him
Psalms28:8 The LORD is their strength

Psalms29:2 Ascribe to the LORD the glory due to His name; Worship the LORD in holy array.
Psalms29:3 The voice of the LORD is upon the waters; The God of glory thunders
Psalms29:4 The voice of the LORD is powerful
Psalms29:5 The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars; Yes
Psalms29:6 He makes Lebanon skip like a calf
Psalms29:7 The voice of the LORD hews out flames of fire.
Psalms29:8 The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; The LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
Psalms29:9 The voice of the LORD makes the deer to calve And strips the forests bare; And in His temple everything says
Psalms29:10 The LORD sat as King at the flood; Yes
Psalms29:11 The LORD will give strength to His people; The LORD will bless His people with peace.

Psalms31:1 In You
Psalms31:2 Incline Your ear to me
Psalms31:3 For You are my rock and my fortress; For Your name's sake You will lead me and guide me.
Psalms31:4 You will pull me out of the net which they have secretly laid for me
Psalms31:5 Into Your hand I commit my spirit; You have ransomed me
Psalms31:6 I hate those who regard vain idols
Psalms31:7 I will rejoice and be glad in Your lovingkindness
Psalms31:8 And You have not given me over into the hand of the enemy; You have set my feet in a large place.
Psalms31:9 Be gracious to me
Psalms31:10 For my life is spent with sorrow And my years with sighing; My strength has failed because of my iniquity
Psalms31:11 Because of all my adversaries
Psalms31:12 I am forgotten as a dead man
Psalms31:13 For I have heard the slander of many
Psalms31:14 But as for me
Psalms31:15 My times are in Your hand; Deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from those who persecute me.
Psalms31:16 Make Your face to shine upon Your servant; Save me in Your lovingkindness.
Psalms31:17 Let me not be put to shame
Psalms31:18 Let the lying lips be mute
Psalms31:19 How great is Your goodness
Psalms31:20 You hide them in the secret place of Your presence from the conspiracies of man; You keep them secretly in a shelter from the strife of tongues.
Psalms31:21 Blessed be the LORD
Psalms31:22 As for me
Psalms31:23 O love the LORD
Psalms31:24 Be strong and let your heart take courage
Psalms32:1 How blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven
Psalms32:2 How blessed is the man to whom the LORD does not impute iniquity
Psalms32:3 When I kept silent about my sin
Psalms32:4 For day and night Your hand was heavy upon me; My vitality was drained away as with the fever heat of summer. Selah.
Psalms32:5 I acknowledged my sin to You
Psalms32:6 Therefore
Psalms32:7 You are my hiding place; You preserve me from trouble; You surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah.
Psalms32:8 I will instruct you and teach you in the way which you should go; I will counsel you with My eye upon you.
Psalms32:9 Do not be as the horse or as the mule which have no understanding
Psalms32:10 Many are the sorrows of the wicked
Psalms32:11 Be glad in the LORD and rejoice
Psalms33:1 Sing for joy in the LORD
Psalms33:2 Give thanks to the LORD with the lyre; Sing praises to Him with a harp of ten strings.
Psalms33:3 Sing to Him a new song; Play skillfully with a shout of joy.
Psalms33:4 For the word of the LORD is upright
Psalms33:5 He loves righteousness and justice; The earth is full of the lovingkindness of the LORD.
Psalms33:6 By the word of the LORD the heavens were made
Psalms33:7 He gathers the waters of the sea together as a heap; He lays up the deeps in storehouses.
Psalms33:8 Let all the earth fear the LORD; Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him.
Psalms33:9 For He spoke
Psalms33:10 The LORD nullifies the counsel of the nations; He frustrates the plans of the peoples.
Psalms33:11 The counsel of the LORD stands forever
Psalms33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD
Psalms33:13 The LORD looks from heaven; He sees all the sons of men;
Psalms33:14 From His dwelling place He looks out On all the inhabitants of the earth
Psalms33:15 He who fashions the hearts of them all
Psalms33:16 The king is not saved by a mighty army; A warrior is not delivered by great strength.
Psalms33:17 A horse is a false hope for victory; Nor does it deliver anyone by its great strength.
Psalms33:18 Behold
Psalms33:19 To deliver their soul from death And to keep them alive in famine.
Psalms33:20 Our soul waits for the LORD; He is our help and our shield.
Psalms33:21 For our heart rejoices in Him
Psalms33:22 Let Your lovingkindness
Psalms34:1 I will bless the LORD at all times; His praise shall continually be in my mouth.
Psalms34:2 My soul will make its boast in the LORD; The humble will hear it and rejoice.
Psalms34:3 O magnify the LORD with me
Psalms34:4 I sought the LORD
Psalms34:5 They looked to Him and were radiant
Psalms34:6 This poor man cried
Psalms34:7 The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear Him
Psalms34:8 O taste and see that the LORD is good; How blessed is the man who takes refuge in Him!
Psalms34:9 O fear the LORD
Psalms34:10 The young lions do lack and suffer hunger; But they who seek the LORD shall not be in want of any good thing.
Psalms34:11 Come
Psalms34:12 Who is the man who desires life And loves length of days that he may see good?
Psalms34:13 Keep your tongue from evil And your lips from speaking deceit.
Psalms34:14 Depart from evil and do good; Seek peace and pursue it.
Psalms34:15 The eyes of the LORD are toward the righteous And His ears are open to their cry.
Psalms34:16 The face of the LORD is against evildoers
Psalms34:17 The righteous cry
Psalms34:18 The LORD is near to the brokenhearted And saves those who are crushed in spirit.
Psalms34:19 Many are the afflictions of the righteous
Psalms34:20 He keeps all his bones
Psalms34:21 Evil shall slay the wicked
Psalms34:22 The LORD redeems the soul of His servants
Psalms35:1 Contend
Psalms35:2 Take hold of buckler and shield And rise up for my help.
Psalms35:3 Draw also the spear and the battle-axe to meet those who pursue me; Say to my soul
Psalms35:4 Let those be ashamed and dishonored who seek my life; Let those be turned back and humiliated who devise evil against me.
Psalms35:5 Let them be like chaff before the wind
Psalms35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery
Psalms35:7 For without cause they hid their net for me; Without cause they dug a pit for my soul.
Psalms35:8 Let destruction come upon him unawares
Psalms35:9 And my soul shall rejoice in the LORD; It shall exult in His salvation.
Psalms35:10 All my bones will say
Psalms35:11 Malicious witnesses rise up; They ask me of things that I do not know.
Psalms35:12 They repay me evil for good
Psalms35:13 But as for me
Psalms35:14 I went about as though it were my friend or brother; I bowed down mourning
Psalms35:15 But at my stumbling they rejoiced and gathered themselves together; The smiters whom I did not know gathered together against me
Psalms35:16 Like godless jesters at a feast
Psalms35:17 Lord
Psalms35:18 I will give You thanks in the great congregation; I will praise You among a mighty throng.
Psalms35:19 Do not let those who are wrongfully my enemies rejoice over me; Nor let those who hate me without cause wink maliciously.
Psalms35:20 For they do not speak peace
Psalms35:21 They opened their mouth wide against me; They said
Psalms35:22 You have seen it
Psalms35:23 Stir up Yourself
Psalms35:24 Judge me
Psalms35:25 Do not let them say in their heart
Psalms35:26 Let those be ashamed and humiliated altogether who rejoice at my distress; Let those be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves over me.
Psalms35:27 Let them shout for joy and rejoice
Psalms35:28 And my tongue shall declare Your righteousness And Your praise all day long.
Psalms36:1 Transgression speaks to the ungodly within his heart; There is no fear of God before his eyes.
Psalms36:2 For it flatters him in his own eyes Concerning the discovery of his iniquity and the hatred of it.
Psalms36:3 The words of his mouth are wickedness and deceit; He has ceased to be wise and to do good.
Psalms36:4 He plans wickedness upon his bed; He sets himself on a path that is not good; He does not despise evil.
Psalms36:5 Your lovingkindness
Psalms36:6 Your righteousness is like the mountains of God; Your judgments are like a great deep. O LORD
Psalms36:7 How precious is Your lovingkindness
Psalms36:8 They drink their fill of the abundance of Your house; And You give them to drink of the river of Your delights.
Psalms36:9 For with You is the fountain of life; In Your light we see light.
Psalms36:10 O continue Your lovingkindness to those who know You
Psalms36:11 Let not the foot of pride come upon me
Psalms36:12 There the doers of iniquity have fallen; They have been thrust down and cannot rise.
Psalms37:1 Do not fret because of evildoers
Psalms37:2 For they will wither quickly like the grass And fade like the green herb.
Psalms37:3 Trust in the LORD and do good; Dwell in the land and cultivate faithfulness.
Psalms37:4 Delight yourself in the LORD; And He will give you the desires of your heart.
Psalms37:5 Commit your way to the LORD
Psalms37:6 He will bring forth your righteousness as the light And your judgment as the noonday.
Psalms37:7 Rest in the LORD and wait patiently for Him; Do not fret because of him who prospers in his way
Psalms37:8 Cease from anger and forsake wrath; Do not fret; it leads only to evildoing.
Psalms37:9 For evildoers will be cut off
Psalms37:10 Yet a little while and the wicked man will be no more; And you will look carefully for his place and he will not be there.
Psalms37:11 But the humble will inherit the land And will delight themselves in abundant prosperity.
Psalms37:12 The wicked plots against the righteous And gnashes at him with his teeth.
Psalms37:13 The Lord laughs at him
Psalms37:14 The wicked have drawn the sword and bent their bow To cast down the afflicted and the needy
Psalms37:15 Their sword will enter their own heart
Psalms37:16 Better is the little of the righteous Than the abundance of many wicked.
Psalms37:17 For the arms of the wicked will be broken
Psalms37:18 The LORD knows the days of the blameless
Psalms37:19 They will not be ashamed in the time of evil
Psalms37:20 But the wicked will perish; And the enemies of the LORD will be like the glory of the pastures
Psalms37:21 The wicked borrows and does not pay back
Psalms37:22 For those blessed by Him will inherit the land
Psalms37:23 The steps of a man are established by the LORD
Psalms37:24 When he falls
Psalms37:25 I have been young and now I am old
Psalms37:26 All day long he is gracious and lends
Psalms37:27 Depart from evil and do good
Psalms37:28 For the LORD loves justice And does not forsake His godly ones; They are preserved forever
Psalms37:29 The righteous will inherit the land And dwell in it forever.
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom
Psalms37:31 The law of his God is in his heart; His steps do not slip.
Psalms37:32 The wicked spies upon the righteous And seeks to kill him.
Psalms37:33 The LORD will not leave him in his hand Or let him be condemned when he is judged.
Psalms37:34 Wait for the LORD and keep His way
Psalms37:35 I have seen a wicked
Psalms37:36 Then he passed away
Psalms37:37 Mark the blameless man
Psalms37:38 But transgressors will be altogether destroyed; The posterity of the wicked will be cut off.
Psalms37:39 But the salvation of the righteous is from the LORD; He is their strength in time of trouble.
Psalms37:40 The LORD helps them and delivers them; He delivers them from the wicked and saves them
Psalms38:1 O LORD
Psalms38:2 For Your arrows have sunk deep into me
Psalms38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh because of Your indignation; There is no health in my bones because of my sin.
Psalms38:4 For my iniquities are gone over my head; As a heavy burden they weigh too much for me.
Psalms38:5 My wounds grow foul and fester Because of my folly.
Psalms38:6 I am bent over and greatly bowed down; I go mourning all day long.
Psalms38:7 For my loins are filled with burning
Psalms38:8 I am benumbed and badly crushed; I groan because of the agitation of my heart.
Psalms38:9 Lord
Psalms38:10 My heart throbs
Psalms38:11 My loved ones and my friends stand aloof from my plague; And my kinsmen stand afar off.
Psalms38:12 Those who seek my life lay snares for me; And those who seek to injure me have threatened destruction
Psalms38:13 But I
Psalms38:14 Yes
Psalms38:15 For I hope in You
Psalms38:16 For I said
Psalms38:17 For I am ready to fall
Psalms38:18 For I confess my iniquity; I am full of anxiety because of my sin.
Psalms38:19 But my enemies are vigorous and strong
Psalms38:20 And those who repay evil for good
Psalms38:21 Do not forsake me
Psalms38:22 Make haste to help me
Psalms39:1 I said
Psalms39:2 I was mute and silent
Psalms39:3 My heart was hot within me
Psalms39:4 LORD
Psalms39:5 Behold
Psalms39:6 Surely every man walks about as a phantom; Surely they make an uproar for nothing; He amasses riches and does not know who will gather them.
Psalms39:7 And now
Psalms39:8 Deliver me from all my transgressions; Make me not the reproach of the foolish.
Psalms39:9 I have become mute
Psalms39:10 Remove Your plague from me; Because of the opposition of Your hand I am perishing.
Psalms39:11 With reproofs You chasten a man for iniquity; You consume as a moth what is precious to him; Surely every man is a mere breath. Selah.
Psalms39:12 Hear my prayer
Psalms39:13 Turn Your gaze away from me
Psalms40:1 I waited patiently for the LORD; And He inclined to me and heard my cry.
Psalms40:2 He brought me up out of the pit of destruction
Psalms40:3 He put a new song in my mouth
Psalms40:4 How blessed is the man who has made the LORD his trust
Psalms40:5 Many
Psalms40:6 Sacrifice and meal offering You have not desired; My ears You have opened; Burnt offering and sin offering You have not required.
Psalms40:7 Then I said
Psalms40:8 I delight to do Your will
Psalms40:9 I have proclaimed glad tidings of righteousness in the great congregation; Behold
Psalms40:10 I have not hidden Your righteousness within my heart; I have spoken of Your faithfulness and Your salvation; I have not concealed Your lovingkindness and Your truth from the great congregation.
Psalms40:11 You
Psalms40:12 For evils beyond number have surrounded me; My iniquities have overtaken me
Psalms40:13 Be pleased
Psalms40:14 Let those be ashamed and humiliated together Who seek my life to destroy it; Let those be turned back and dishonored Who delight in my hurt.
Psalms40:15 Let those be appalled because of their shame Who say to me
Psalms40:16 Let all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; Let those who love Your salvation say continually
Psalms40:17 Since I am afflicted and needy
Psalms41:1 How blessed is he who considers the helpless; The LORD will deliver him in a day of trouble.
Psalms41:2 The LORD will protect him and keep him alive
Psalms41:3 The LORD will sustain him upon his sickbed; In his illness
Psalms41:4 As for me
Psalms41:5 My enemies speak evil against me
Psalms41:6 And when he comes to see me
Psalms41:7 All who hate me whisper together against me; Against me they devise my hurt
Psalms41:8 A wicked thing is poured out upon him
Psalms41:9 Even my close friend in whom I trusted
Psalms41:10 But You
Psalms41:11 By this I know that You are pleased with me
Psalms41:12 As for me
Psalms41:13 Blessed be the LORD
Psalms42:1 As the deer pants for the water brooks
Psalms42:2 My soul thirsts for God
Psalms42:3 My tears have been my food day and night
Psalms42:4 These things I remember and I pour out my soul within me. For I used to go along with the throng and lead them in procession to the house of God
Psalms42:5 Why are you in despair
Psalms42:6 O my God
Psalms42:7 Deep calls to deep at the sound of Your waterfalls; All Your breakers and Your waves have rolled over me.
Psalms42:8 The LORD will command His lovingkindness in the daytime; And His song will be with me in the night
Psalms42:9 I will say to God my rock
Psalms42:10 As a shattering of my bones
Psalms42:11 Why are you in despair
Psalms43:1 Vindicate me
Psalms43:2 For You are the God of my strength; why have You rejected me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
Psalms43:3 O send out Your light and Your truth
Psalms43:4 Then I will go to the altar of God
Psalms43:5 Why are you in despair
Psalms44:1 O God
Psalms44:2 You with Your own hand drove out the nations; Then You planted them; You afflicted the peoples
Psalms44:3 For by their own sword they did not possess the land
Psalms44:4 You are my King
Psalms44:5 Through You we will push back our adversaries; Through Your name we will trample down those who rise up against us.
Psalms44:6 For I will not trust in my bow
Psalms44:7 But You have saved us from our adversaries
Psalms44:8 In God we have boasted all day long
Psalms44:9 Yet You have rejected us and brought us to dishonor
Psalms44:10 You cause us to turn back from the adversary; And those who hate us have taken spoil for themselves.
Psalms44:11 You give us as sheep to be eaten And have scattered us among the nations.
Psalms44:12 You sell Your people cheaply
Psalms44:13 You make us a reproach to our neighbors
Psalms44:14 You make us a byword among the nations
Psalms44:15 All day long my dishonor is before me And my humiliation has overwhelmed me
Psalms44:16 Because of the voice of him who reproaches and reviles
Psalms44:17 All this has come upon us
Psalms44:18 Our heart has not turned back
Psalms44:19 Yet You have crushed us in a place of jackals And covered us with the shadow of death.
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God Or extended our hands to a strange god
Psalms44:21 Would not God find this out? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
Psalms44:22 But for Your sake we are killed all day long; We are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.
Psalms44:23 Arouse Yourself
Psalms44:24 Why do You hide Your face And forget our affliction and our oppression?
Psalms44:25 For our soul has sunk down into the dust; Our body cleaves to the earth.
Psalms44:26 Rise up
Psalms45:1 My heart overflows with a good theme; I address my verses to the King; My tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
Psalms45:2 You are fairer than the sons of men; Grace is poured upon Your lips; Therefore God has blessed You forever.
Psalms45:3 Gird Your sword on Your thigh
Psalms45:4 And in Your majesty ride on victoriously
Psalms45:5 Your arrows are sharp; The peoples fall under You; Your arrows are in the heart of the King's enemies.
Psalms45:6 Your throne
Psalms45:7 You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; Therefore God
Psalms45:8 All Your garments are fragrant with myrrh and aloes and cassia; Out of ivory palaces stringed instruments have made You glad.
Psalms45:9 Kings' daughters are among Your noble ladies; At Your right hand stands the queen in gold from Ophir.
Psalms45:10 Listen
Psalms45:11 Then the King will desire your beauty. Because He is your Lord
Psalms45:12 The daughter of Tyre will come with a gift; The rich among the people will seek your favor.
Psalms45:13 The King's daughter is all glorious within; Her clothing is interwoven with gold.
Psalms45:14 She will be led to the King in embroidered work; The virgins
Psalms45:15 They will be led forth with gladness and rejoicing; They will enter into the King's palace.
Psalms45:16 In place of your fathers will be your sons; You shall make them princes in all the earth.
Psalms45:17 I will cause Your name to be remembered in all generations; Therefore the peoples will give You thanks forever and ever.
Psalms46:1 God is our refuge and strength
Psalms46:2 Therefore we will not fear
Psalms46:3 Though its waters roar and foam
Psalms46:4 There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God
Psalms46:5 God is in the midst of her
Psalms46:6 The nations made an uproar
Psalms46:7 The LORD of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our stronghold. Selah.
Psalms46:8 Come
Psalms46:9 He makes wars to cease to the end of the earth; He breaks the bow and cuts the spear in two; He burns the chariots with fire.
Psalms46:10 Cease striving and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations
Psalms46:11 The LORD of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our stronghold. Selah.
Psalms47:1 O clap your hands
Psalms47:2 For the LORD Most High is to be feared
Psalms47:3 He subdues peoples under us And nations under our feet.
Psalms47:4 He chooses our inheritance for us
Psalms47:5 God has ascended with a shout
Psalms47:6 Sing praises to God
Psalms47:7 For God is the King of all the earth; Sing praises with a skillful psalm.
Psalms47:8 God reigns over the nations
Psalms47:9 The princes of the people have assembled themselves as the people of the God of Abraham
Psalms48:1 Great is the LORD
Psalms48:2 Beautiful in elevation
Psalms48:3 God
Psalms48:4 For
Psalms48:5 They saw it
Psalms48:6 Panic seized them there
Psalms48:7 With the east wind You break the ships of Tarshish.
Psalms48:8 As we have heard
Psalms48:9 We have thought on Your lovingkindness
Psalms48:10 As is Your name
Psalms48:11 Let Mount Zion be glad
Psalms48:12 Walk about Zion and go around her; Count her towers;
Psalms48:13 Consider her ramparts; Go through her palaces
Psalms48:14 For such is God
Psalms49:1 Hear this
Psalms49:2 Both low and high
Psalms49:3 My mouth will speak wisdom
Psalms49:4 I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will express my riddle on the harp.
Psalms49:5 Why should I fear in days of adversity
Psalms49:6 Even those who trust in their wealth And boast in the abundance of their riches?
Psalms49:7 No man can by any means redeem his brother Or give to God a ransom for him--
Psalms49:8 For the redemption of his soul is costly
Psalms49:9 That he should live on eternally
Psalms49:10 For he sees that even wise men die; The stupid and the senseless alike perish And leave their wealth to others.
Psalms49:11 Their inner thought is that their houses are forever And their dwelling places to all generations; They have called their lands after their own names.
Psalms49:12 But man in his pomp will not endure; He is like the beasts that perish.
Psalms49:13 This is the way of those who are foolish
Psalms49:14 As sheep they are appointed for Sheol; Death shall be their shepherd; And the upright shall rule over them in the morning
Psalms49:15 But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol
Psalms49:16 Do not be afraid when a man becomes rich
Psalms49:17 For when he dies he will carry nothing away; His glory will not descend after him.
Psalms49:18 Though while he lives he congratulates himself--And though men praise you when you do well for yourself--
Psalms49:19 He shall go to the generation of his fathers; They will never see the light.
Psalms49:20 Man in his pomp
Psalms50:1 The Mighty One
Psalms50:2 Out of Zion
Psalms50:3 May our God come and not keep silence; Fire devours before Him
Psalms50:4 He summons the heavens above
Psalms50:5 Gather My godly ones to Me
Psalms50:6 And the heavens declare His righteousness
Psalms50:7 Hear
Psalms50:8 I do not reprove you for your sacrifices
Psalms50:9 I shall take no young bull out of your house Nor male goats out of your folds.
Psalms50:10 For every beast of the forest is Mine
Psalms50:11 I know every bird of the mountains
Psalms50:12 If I were hungry I would not tell you
Psalms50:13 Shall I eat the flesh of bulls Or drink the blood of male goats?
Psalms50:14 Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving And pay your vows to the Most High;
Psalms50:15 Call upon Me in the day of trouble; I shall rescue you
Psalms50:16 But to the wicked God says
Psalms50:17 For you hate discipline
Psalms50:18 When you see a thief
Psalms50:19 You let your mouth loose in evil And your tongue frames deceit.
Psalms50:20 You sit and speak against your brother; You slander your own mother's son.
Psalms50:21 These things you have done and I kept silence; You thought that I was just like you; I will reprove you and state the case in order before your eyes.
Psalms50:22 Now consider this
Psalms50:23 He who offers a sacrifice of thanksgiving honors Me; And to him who orders his way aright I shall show the salvation of God.
Psalms51:1 Be gracious to me
Psalms51:2 Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity And cleanse me from my sin.
Psalms51:3 For I know my transgressions
Psalms51:4 Against You
Psalms51:5 Behold
Psalms51:6 Behold
Psalms51:7 Purify me with hyssop
Psalms51:8 Make me to hear joy and gladness
Psalms51:9 Hide Your face from my sins And blot out all my iniquities.
Psalms51:10 Create in me a clean heart
Psalms51:11 Do not cast me away from Your presence And do not take Your Holy Spirit from me.
Psalms51:12 Restore to me the joy of Your salvation And sustain me with a willing spirit.
Psalms51:13 Then I will teach transgressors Your ways
Psalms51:14 Deliver me from bloodguiltiness
Psalms51:15 O Lord
Psalms51:16 For You do not delight in sacrifice
Psalms51:17 The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; A broken and a contrite heart
Psalms51:18 By Your favor do good to Zion; Build the walls of Jerusalem.
Psalms51:19 Then You will delight in righteous sacrifices
