Psalms52:2 Your tongue devises destruction
Psalms52:3 You love evil more than good
Psalms52:4 You love all words that devour
Psalms52:5 But God will break you down forever; He will snatch you up and tear you away from your tent
Psalms52:6 The righteous will see and fear
Psalms52:7 Behold
Psalms52:8 But as for me
Psalms52:9 I will give You thanks forever
Psalms53:1 The fool has said in his heart
Psalms53:2 God has looked down from heaven upon the sons of men To see if there is anyone who understands
Psalms53:3 Every one of them has turned aside; together they have become corrupt; There is no one who does good
Psalms53:4 Have the workers of wickedness no knowledge
Psalms53:5 There they were in great fear where no fear had been; For God scattered the bones of him who encamped against you; You put them to shame
Psalms53:6 Oh
Psalms54:1 . Save me
Psalms54:2 Hear my prayer
Psalms54:3 For strangers have risen against me And violent men have sought my life; They have not set God before them. Selah.
Psalms54:4 Behold
Psalms54:5 He will recompense the evil to my foes; Destroy them in Your faithfulness.
Psalms54:6 Willingly I will sacrifice to You; I will give thanks to Your name
Psalms54:7 For He has delivered me from all trouble
Psalms55:1 Give ear to my prayer
Psalms55:2 Give heed to me and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and am surely distracted
Psalms55:3 Because of the voice of the enemy
Psalms55:4 My heart is in anguish within me
Psalms55:5 Fear and trembling come upon me
Psalms55:6 I said
Psalms55:7 Behold
Psalms55:8 I would hasten to my place of refuge From the stormy wind and tempest.
Psalms55:9 Confuse
Psalms55:10 Day and night they go around her upon her walls
Psalms55:11 Destruction is in her midst; Oppression and deceit do not depart from her streets.
Psalms55:12 For it is not an enemy who reproaches me
Psalms55:13 But it is you
Psalms55:14 We who had sweet fellowship together Walked in the house of God in the throng.
Psalms55:15 Let death come deceitfully upon them; Let them go down alive to Sheol
Psalms55:16 As for me
Psalms55:17 Evening and morning and at noon
Psalms55:18 He will redeem my soul in peace from the battle which is against me
Psalms55:19 God will hear and answer them--Even the one who sits enthroned from of old--Selah. With whom there is no change
Psalms55:20 He has put forth his hands against those who were at peace with him; He has violated his covenant.
Psalms55:21 His speech was smoother than butter
Psalms55:22 Cast your burden upon the LORD and He will sustain you; He will never allow the righteous to be shaken.
Psalms55:23 But You
Psalms56:1 Be gracious to me
Psalms56:2 My foes have trampled upon me all day long
Psalms56:3 When I am afraid
Psalms56:4 In God
Psalms56:5 All day long they distort my words; All their thoughts are against me for evil.
Psalms56:6 They attack
Psalms56:7 Because of wickedness
Psalms56:8 You have taken account of my wanderings; Put my tears in Your bottle. Are they not in Your book?
Psalms56:9 Then my enemies will turn back in the day when I call; This I know
Psalms56:10 In God
Psalms56:11 In God I have put my trust
Psalms56:12 Your vows are binding upon me
Psalms56:13 For You have delivered my soul from death
Psalms57:1 Be gracious to me
Psalms57:2 I will cry to God Most High
Psalms57:3 He will send from heaven and save me; He reproaches him who tramples upon me. Selah. God will send forth His lovingkindness and His truth.
Psalms57:4 My soul is among lions; I must lie among those who breathe forth fire
Psalms57:5 Be exalted above the heavens
Psalms57:6 They have prepared a net for my steps; My soul is bowed down; They dug a pit before me; They themselves have fallen into the midst of it. Selah.
Psalms57:7 My heart is steadfast
Psalms57:8 Awake
Psalms57:9 I will give thanks to You
Psalms57:10 For Your lovingkindness is great to the heavens And Your truth to the clouds.
Psalms57:11 Be exalted above the heavens
Psalms58:1 Do you indeed speak righteousness
Psalms58:2 No
Psalms58:3 The wicked are estranged from the womb; These who speak lies go astray from birth.
Psalms58:4 They have venom like the venom of a serpent; Like a deaf cobra that stops up its ear
Psalms58:5 So that it does not hear the voice of charmers
Psalms58:6 O God

Psalms69:1 Save me

Psalms69:5 O God
Psalms69:6 May those who wait for You not be ashamed through me
Psalms69:7 Because for Your sake I have borne reproach; Dishonor has covered my face.

Psalms69:9 For zeal for Your house has consumed me
Psalms69:10 When I wept in my soul with fasting
Psalms69:11 When I made sackcloth my clothing
Psalms69:12 Those who sit in the gate talk about me
Psalms69:13 But as for me

Psalms69:21 They also gave me gall for my food And for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.

Psalms74:6 And now all its carved work They smash with hatchet and hammers.

Psalms78:1 Listen
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings of old
Psalms78:3 Which we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 We will not conceal them from their children
Psalms78:5 For He established a testimony in Jacob And appointed a law in Israel
Psalms78:6 That the generation to come might know
Psalms78:7 That they should put their confidence in God And not forget the works of God
Psalms78:8 And not be like their fathers
Psalms78:9 The sons of Ephraim were archers equipped with bows
Psalms78:10 They did not keep the covenant of God And refused to walk in His law;
Psalms78:11 They forgot His deeds And His miracles that He had shown them.
Psalms78:12 He wrought wonders before their fathers In the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and caused them to pass through
Psalms78:14 Then He led them with the cloud by day And all the night with a light of fire.
Psalms78:15 He split the rocks in the wilderness And gave them abundant drink like the ocean depths.
Psalms78:16 He brought forth streams also from the rock And caused waters to run down like rivers.
Psalms78:17 Yet they still continued to sin against Him
Psalms78:18 And in their heart they put God to the test By asking food according to their desire.
Psalms78:19 Then they spoke against God; They said
Psalms78:20 Behold
Psalms78:21 Therefore the LORD heard and was full of wrath; And a fire was kindled against Jacob And anger also mounted against Israel
Psalms78:22 Because they did not believe in God And did not trust in His salvation.
Psalms78:23 Yet He commanded the clouds above And opened the doors of heaven;
Psalms78:24 He rained down manna upon them to eat And gave them food from heaven.
Psalms78:25 Man did eat the bread of angels; He sent them food in abundance.
Psalms78:26 He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens And by His power He directed the south wind.
Psalms78:27 When He rained meat upon them like the dust
Psalms78:28 Then He let them fall in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 So they ate and were well filled
Psalms78:30 Before they had satisfied their desire
Psalms78:31 The anger of God rose against them And killed some of their stoutest ones
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this they still sinned And did not believe in His wonderful works.
Psalms78:33 So He brought their days to an end in futility And their years in sudden terror.
Psalms78:34 When He killed them
Psalms78:35 And they remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they deceived Him with their mouth And lied to Him with their tongue.
Psalms78:37 For their heart was not steadfast toward Him
Psalms78:38 But He
Psalms78:39 Thus He remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against Him in the wilderness And grieved Him in the desert!
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tempted God
Psalms78:42 They did not remember His power
Psalms78:43 When He performed His signs in Egypt And His marvels in the field of Zoan
Psalms78:44 And turned their rivers to blood
Psalms78:45 He sent among them swarms of flies which devoured them
Psalms78:46 He gave also their crops to the grasshopper And the product of their labor to the locust.
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hailstones And their sycamore trees with frost.
Psalms78:48 He gave over their cattle also to the hailstones And their herds to bolts of lightning.
Psalms78:49 He sent upon them His burning anger
Psalms78:50 He leveled a path for His anger; He did not spare their soul from death
Psalms78:51 And smote all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 But He led forth His own people like sheep And guided them in the wilderness like a flock;
Psalms78:53 He led them safely
Psalms78:54 So He brought them to His holy land
Psalms78:55 He also drove out the nations before them And apportioned them for an inheritance by measurement
Psalms78:56 Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God And did not keep His testimonies
Psalms78:57 But turned back and acted treacherously like their fathers; They turned aside like a treacherous bow.
Psalms78:58 For they provoked Him with their high places And aroused His jealousy with their graven images.
Psalms78:59 When God heard
Psalms78:60 So that He abandoned the dwelling place at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 And gave up His strength to captivity And His glory into the hand of the adversary.
Psalms78:62 He also delivered His people to the sword
Psalms78:63 Fire devoured His young men
Psalms78:64 His priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as if from sleep
Psalms78:66 He drove His adversaries backward; He put on them an everlasting reproach.
Psalms78:67 He also rejected the tent of Joseph
Psalms78:68 But chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 And He built His sanctuary like the heights
Psalms78:70 He also chose David His servant And took him from the sheepfolds;
Psalms78:71 From the care of the ewes with suckling lambs He brought him To shepherd Jacob His people
Psalms78:72 So he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart

Psalms79:3 They have poured out their blood like water round about Jerusalem; And there was no one to bury them.
Psalms79:4 We have become a reproach to our neighbors
Psalms79:5 How long

Psalms79:7 For they have devoured Jacob And laid waste his habitation.

Psalms79:13 So we Your people and the sheep of Your pasture Will give thanks to You forever; To all generations we will tell of Your praise.

Psalms89:28 My lovingkindness I will keep for him forever
Psalms89:29 So I will establish his descendants forever And his throne as the days of heaven.

Psalms89:31 If they violate My statutes And do not keep My commandments
Psalms89:32 Then I will punish their transgression with the rod And their iniquity with stripes.
Psalms89:33 But I will not break off My lovingkindness from him
Psalms89:34 My covenant I will not violate
Psalms89:35 Once I have sworn by My holiness; I will not lie to David.
Psalms89:36 His descendants shall endure forever And his throne as the sun before Me.
Psalms89:37 It shall be established forever like the moon
Psalms89:38 But You have cast off and rejected
Psalms89:39 You have spurned the covenant of Your servant; You have profaned his crown in the dust.
Psalms89:40 You have broken down all his walls; You have brought his strongholds to ruin.
Psalms89:41 All who pass along the way plunder him; He has become a reproach to his neighbors.
Psalms89:42 You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries; You have made all his enemies rejoice.
Psalms89:43 You also turn back the edge of his sword And have not made him stand in battle.
Psalms89:44 You have made his splendor to cease And cast his throne to the ground.
Psalms89:45 You have shortened the days of his youth; You have covered him with shame. Selah.

Psalms104:4 He makes the winds His messengers

Psalms104:6 You covered it with the deep as with a garment; The waters were standing above the mountains.
Psalms104:7 At Your rebuke they fled
Psalms104:8 The mountains rose; the valleys sank down To the place which You established for them.
Psalms104:9 You set a boundary that they may not pass over
Psalms104:10 He sends forth springs in the valleys; They flow between the mountains;
Psalms104:11 They give drink to every beast of the field; The wild donkeys quench their thirst.
Psalms104:12 Beside them the birds of the heavens dwell; They lift up their voices among the branches.
Psalms104:13 He waters the mountains from His upper chambers; The earth is satisfied with the fruit of His works.

Psalms104:15 And wine which makes man's heart glad
Psalms104:16 The trees of the LORD drink their fill
Psalms104:17 Where the birds build their nests

Psalms105:21 He made him lord of his house And ruler over all his possessions
Psalms105:22 To imprison his princes at will
Psalms105:23 Israel also came into Egypt; Thus Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
Psalms105:24 And He caused His people to be very fruitful
Psalms105:25 He turned their heart to hate His people
Psalms105:26 He sent Moses His servant
Psalms105:27 They performed His wondrous acts among them
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness and made it dark; And they did not rebel against His words.
Psalms105:29 He turned their waters into blood And caused their fish to die.
Psalms105:30 Their land swarmed with frogs Even in the chambers of their kings.
Psalms105:31 He spoke
Psalms105:32 He gave them hail for rain
Psalms105:33 He struck down their vines also and their fig trees
Psalms105:34 He spoke
Psalms105:35 And ate up all vegetation in their land
Psalms105:36 He also struck down all the firstborn in their land
Psalms105:37 Then He brought them out with silver and gold
Psalms105:38 Egypt was glad when they departed
Psalms105:39 He spread a cloud for a covering
Psalms105:40 They asked
Psalms105:41 He opened the rock and water flowed out; It ran in the dry places like a river.
Psalms105:42 For He remembered His holy word With Abraham His servant;
Psalms105:43 And He brought forth His people with joy
Psalms105:44 He gave them also the lands of the nations
Psalms105:45 So that they might keep His statutes And observe His laws
Psalms106:1 Praise the LORD! Oh give thanks to the LORD
Psalms106:2 Who can speak of the mighty deeds of the LORD
Psalms106:3 How blessed are those who keep justice
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 That I may see the prosperity of Your chosen ones
Psalms106:6 We have sinned like our fathers
Psalms106:7 Our fathers in Egypt did not understand Your wonders; They did not remember Your abundant kindnesses
Psalms106:8 Nevertheless He saved them for the sake of His name
Psalms106:9 Thus He rebuked the Red Sea and it dried up
Psalms106:10 So He saved them from the hand of the one who hated them
Psalms106:11 The waters covered their adversaries; Not one of them was left.
Psalms106:12 Then they believed His words; They sang His praise.
Psalms106:13 They quickly forgot His works; They did not wait for His counsel
Psalms106:14 But craved intensely in the wilderness
Psalms106:15 So He gave them their request
Psalms106:16 When they became envious of Moses in the camp
Psalms106:17 The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan
Psalms106:18 And a fire blazed up in their company; The flame consumed the wicked.
Psalms106:19 They made a calf in Horeb And worshiped a molten image.
Psalms106:20 Thus they exchanged their glory For the image of an ox that eats grass.
Psalms106:21 They forgot God their Savior
Psalms106:22 Wonders in the land of Ham And awesome things by the Red Sea.
Psalms106:23 Therefore He said that He would destroy them
Psalms106:24 Then they despised the pleasant land; They did not believe in His word
Psalms106:25 But grumbled in their tents; They did not listen to the voice of the LORD.
Psalms106:26 Therefore He swore to them That He would cast them down in the wilderness
Psalms106:27 And that He would cast their seed among the nations And scatter them in the lands.
Psalms106:28 They joined themselves also to Baal-peor
Psalms106:29 Thus they provoked Him to anger with their deeds
Psalms106:30 Then Phinehas stood up and interposed
Psalms106:31 And it was reckoned to him for righteousness
Psalms106:32 They also provoked Him to wrath at the waters of Meribah
Psalms106:33 Because they were rebellious against His Spirit
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples
Psalms106:35 But they mingled with the nations And learned their practices
Psalms106:36 And served their idols
Psalms106:37 They even sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the demons
Psalms106:38 And shed innocent blood
Psalms106:39 Thus they became unclean in their practices
Psalms106:40 Therefore the anger of the LORD was kindled against His people And He abhorred His inheritance.
Psalms106:41 Then He gave them into the hand of the nations
Psalms106:42 Their enemies also oppressed them
Psalms106:43 Many times He would deliver them; They
Psalms106:44 Nevertheless He looked upon their distress When He heard their cry;
Psalms106:45 And He remembered His covenant for their sake
Psalms106:46 He also made them objects of compassion In the presence of all their captors.
Psalms106:47 Save us
Psalms106:48 Blessed be the LORD
Psalms107:1 Oh give thanks to the LORD
Psalms107:2 Let the redeemed of the LORD say so
Psalms107:3 And gathered from the lands
Psalms107:4 They wandered in the wilderness in a desert region; They did not find a way to an inhabited city.
Psalms107:5 They were hungry and thirsty; Their soul fainted within them.
Psalms107:6 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He delivered them out of their distresses.
Psalms107:7 He led them also by a straight way
Psalms107:8 Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness
Psalms107:9 For He has satisfied the thirsty soul
Psalms107:10 There were those who dwelt in darkness and in the shadow of death
Psalms107:11 Because they had rebelled against the words of God And spurned the counsel of the Most High.
Psalms107:12 Therefore He humbled their heart with labor; They stumbled and there was none to help.
Psalms107:13 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them out of their distresses.
Psalms107:14 He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death And broke their bands apart.
Psalms107:15 Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness
Psalms107:16 For He has shattered gates of bronze And cut bars of iron asunder.
Psalms107:17 Fools
Psalms107:18 Their soul abhorred all kinds of food
Psalms107:19 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them out of their distresses.
Psalms107:20 He sent His word and healed them
Psalms107:21 Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness
Psalms107:22 Let them also offer sacrifices of thanksgiving
Psalms107:23 Those who go down to the sea in ships
Psalms107:24 They have seen the works of the LORD
Psalms107:25 For He spoke and raised up a stormy wind
Psalms107:26 They rose up to the heavens
Psalms107:27 They reeled and staggered like a drunken man
Psalms107:28 Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble
Psalms107:29 He caused the storm to be still
Psalms107:30 Then they were glad because they were quiet
Psalms107:31 Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness
Psalms107:32 Let them extol Him also in the congregation of the people
Psalms107:33 He changes rivers into a wilderness And springs of water into a thirsty ground;
Psalms107:34 A fruitful land into a salt waste
Psalms107:35 He changes a wilderness into a pool of water And a dry land into springs of water;
Psalms107:36 And there He makes the hungry to dwell
Psalms107:37 And sow fields and plant vineyards
Psalms107:38 Also He blesses them and they multiply greatly
Psalms107:39 When they are diminished and bowed down Through oppression
Psalms107:40 He pours contempt upon princes And makes them wander in a pathless waste.
Psalms107:41 But He sets the needy securely on high away from affliction
Psalms107:42 The upright see it and are glad; But all unrighteousness shuts its mouth.
Psalms107:43 Who is wise? Let him give heed to these things

Psalms108:2 Awake

Psalms108:4 For Your lovingkindness is great above the heavens
Psalms108:5 Be exalted
Psalms108:6 That Your beloved may be delivered
Psalms108:7 God has spoken in His holiness: I will exult
Psalms108:8 Gilead is Mine
Psalms108:9 Moab is My washbowl; Over Edom I shall throw My shoe; Over Philistia I will shout aloud.
Psalms108:10 Who will bring me into the besieged city? Who will lead me to Edom?
Psalms108:11 Have not You Yourself
Psalms108:12 Oh give us help against the adversary
Psalms108:13 Through God we will do valiantly
Psalms109:1 O God of my praise
Psalms109:2 For they have opened the wicked and deceitful mouth against me; They have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
Psalms109:3 They have also surrounded me with words of hatred
Psalms109:4 In return for my love they act as my accusers; But I am in prayer.
Psalms109:5 Thus they have repaid me evil for good And hatred for my love.
Psalms109:6 Appoint a wicked man over him
Psalms109:7 When he is judged
Psalms109:8 Let his days be few; Let another take his office.
Psalms109:9 Let his children be fatherless And his wife a widow.
Psalms109:10 Let his children wander about and beg; And let them seek sustenance far from their ruined homes.
Psalms109:11 Let the creditor seize all that he has
Psalms109:12 Let there be none to extend lovingkindness to him
Psalms109:13 Let his posterity be cut off; In a following generation let their name be blotted out.
Psalms109:14 Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD
Psalms109:15 Let them be before the LORD continually
Psalms109:16 Because he did not remember to show lovingkindness
Psalms109:17 He also loved cursing
Psalms109:18 But he clothed himself with cursing as with his garment
Psalms109:19 Let it be to him as a garment with which he covers himself
Psalms109:20 Let this be the reward of my accusers from the LORD
Psalms109:21 But You
Psalms109:22 For I am afflicted and needy
Psalms109:23 I am passing like a shadow when it lengthens; I am shaken off like the locust.
Psalms109:24 My knees are weak from fasting
Psalms109:25 I also have become a reproach to them; When they see me
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 And let them know that this is Your hand; You
Psalms109:28 Let them curse
Psalms109:29 Let my accusers be clothed with dishonor
Psalms109:30 With my mouth I will give thanks abundantly to the LORD; And in the midst of many I will praise Him.
Psalms109:31 For He stands at the right hand of the needy

Psalms110:5 The Lord is at Your right hand; He will shatter kings in the day of His wrath.

Psalms110:7 He will drink from the brook by the wayside; Therefore He will lift up His head.
Psalms111:1 Praise the LORD! I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart
Psalms111:2 Great are the works of the LORD; They are studied by all who delight in them.
Psalms111:3 Splendid and majestic is His work
Psalms111:4 He has made His wonders to be remembered; The LORD is gracious and compassionate.
Psalms111:5 He has given food to those who fear Him; He will remember His covenant forever.
Psalms111:6 He has made known to His people the power of His works
Psalms111:7 The works of His hands are truth and justice; All His precepts are sure.
Psalms111:8 They are upheld forever and ever; They are performed in truth and uprightness.
Psalms111:9 He has sent redemption to His people; He has ordained His covenant forever; Holy and awesome is His name.
Psalms111:10 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; A good understanding have all those who do His commandments; His praise endures forever.

Psalms119:1 How blessed are those whose way is blameless
Psalms119:2 How blessed are those who observe His testimonies
Psalms119:3 They also do no unrighteousness; They walk in His ways.
Psalms119:4 You have ordained Your precepts
Psalms119:5 Oh that my ways may be established To keep Your statutes!
Psalms119:6 Then I shall not be ashamed When I look upon all Your commandments.
Psalms119:7 I shall give thanks to You with uprightness of heart
Psalms119:8 I shall keep Your statutes; Do not forsake me utterly!
Psalms119:9 How can a young man keep his way pure? By keeping it according to Your word.
Psalms119:10 With all my heart I have sought You; Do not let me wander from Your commandments.
Psalms119:11 Your word I have treasured in my heart
Psalms119:12 Blessed are You
Psalms119:13 With my lips I have told of All the ordinances of Your mouth.
Psalms119:14 I have rejoiced in the way of Your testimonies
Psalms119:15 I will meditate on Your precepts And regard Your ways.
Psalms119:16 I shall delight in Your statutes; I shall not forget Your word.
Psalms119:17 Deal bountifully with Your servant
Psalms119:18 Open my eyes
Psalms119:19 I am a stranger in the earth; Do not hide Your commandments from me.
Psalms119:20 My soul is crushed with longing After Your ordinances at all times.
Psalms119:21 You rebuke the arrogant
Psalms119:22 Take away reproach and contempt from me
Psalms119:23 Even though princes sit and talk against me
Psalms119:24 Your testimonies also are my delight; They are my counselors.
Psalms119:25 My soul cleaves to the dust; Revive me according to Your word.
Psalms119:26 I have told of my ways
Psalms119:27 Make me understand the way of Your precepts
Psalms119:28 My soul weeps because of grief; Strengthen me according to Your word.
Psalms119:29 Remove the false way from me
Psalms119:30 I have chosen the faithful way; I have placed Your ordinances before me.
Psalms119:31 I cling to Your testimonies; O LORD
Psalms119:32 I shall run the way of Your commandments
Psalms119:33 Teach me
Psalms119:34 Give me understanding
Psalms119:35 Make me walk in the path of Your commandments
Psalms119:36 Incline my heart to Your testimonies And not to dishonest gain.
Psalms119:37 Turn away my eyes from looking at vanity
Psalms119:38 Establish Your word to Your servant
Psalms119:39 Turn away my reproach which I dread
Psalms119:40 Behold
Psalms119:41 May Your lovingkindnesses also come to me
Psalms119:42 So I will have an answer for him who reproaches me
Psalms119:43 And do not take the word of truth utterly out of my mouth
Psalms119:44 So I will keep Your law continually
Psalms119:45 And I will walk at liberty
Psalms119:46 I will also speak of Your testimonies before kings And shall not be ashamed.
Psalms119:47 I shall delight in Your commandments
Psalms119:48 And I shall lift up my hands to Your commandments
Psalms119:49 Remember the word to Your servant
Psalms119:50 This is my comfort in my affliction
Psalms119:51 The arrogant utterly deride me
Psalms119:52 I have remembered Your ordinances from of old
Psalms119:53 Burning indignation has seized me because of the wicked
Psalms119:54 Your statutes are my songs In the house of my pilgrimage.
Psalms119:55 O LORD
Psalms119:56 This has become mine
Psalms119:57 The LORD is my portion; I have promised to keep Your words.
Psalms119:58 I sought Your favor with all my heart; Be gracious to me according to Your word.
Psalms119:59 I considered my ways And turned my feet to Your testimonies.
Psalms119:60 I hastened and did not delay To keep Your commandments.
Psalms119:61 The cords of the wicked have encircled me
Psalms119:62 At midnight I shall rise to give thanks to You Because of Your righteous ordinances.
Psalms119:63 I am a companion of all those who fear You
Psalms119:64 The earth is full of Your lovingkindness
Psalms119:65 You have dealt well with Your servant
Psalms119:66 Teach me good discernment and knowledge
Psalms119:67 Before I was afflicted I went astray
Psalms119:68 You are good and do good; Teach me Your statutes.
Psalms119:69 The arrogant have forged a lie against me; With all my heart I will observe Your precepts.
Psalms119:70 Their heart is covered with fat
Psalms119:71 It is good for me that I was afflicted
Psalms119:72 The law of Your mouth is better to me Than thousands of gold and silver pieces.
Psalms119:73 Your hands made me and fashioned me; Give me understanding
Psalms119:74 May those who fear You see me and be glad
Psalms119:75 I know
Psalms119:76 O may Your lovingkindness comfort me
Psalms119:77 May Your compassion come to me that I may live
Psalms119:78 May the arrogant be ashamed
Psalms119:79 May those who fear You turn to me
Psalms119:80 May my heart be blameless in Your statutes
Psalms119:81 My soul languishes for Your salvation; I wait for Your word.
Psalms119:82 My eyes fail with longing for Your word
Psalms119:83 Though I have become like a wineskin in the smoke
Psalms119:84 How many are the days of Your servant? When will You execute judgment on those who persecute me?
Psalms119:85 The arrogant have dug pits for me
Psalms119:86 All Your commandments are faithful; They have persecuted me with a lie; help me!
Psalms119:87 They almost destroyed me on earth
Psalms119:88 Revive me according to Your lovingkindness
Psalms119:89 Forever
Psalms119:90 Your faithfulness continues throughout all generations; You established the earth
Psalms119:91 They stand this day according to Your ordinances
Psalms119:92 If Your law had not been my delight
Psalms119:93 I will never forget Your precepts
Psalms119:94 I am Yours
Psalms119:95 The wicked wait for me to destroy me; I shall diligently consider Your testimonies.
Psalms119:96 I have seen a limit to all perfection; Your commandment is exceedingly broad.
Psalms119:97 O how I love Your law! It is my meditation all the day.
Psalms119:98 Your commandments make me wiser than my enemies
Psalms119:99 I have more insight than all my teachers
Psalms119:100 I understand more than the aged
Psalms119:101 I have restrained my feet from every evil way
Psalms119:102 I have not turned aside from Your ordinances
Psalms119:103 How sweet are Your words to my taste! Yes
Psalms119:104 From Your precepts I get understanding; Therefore I hate every false way.
Psalms119:105 Your word is a lamp to my feet And a light to my path.
Psalms119:106 I have sworn and I will confirm it
Psalms119:107 I am exceedingly afflicted; Revive me
Psalms119:108 O accept the freewill offerings of my mouth
Psalms119:109 My life is continually in my hand
Psalms119:110 The wicked have laid a snare for me
Psalms119:111 I have inherited Your testimonies forever
Psalms119:112 I have inclined my heart to perform Your statutes Forever
Psalms119:113 I hate those who are double-minded
Psalms119:114 You are my hiding place and my shield; I wait for Your word.
Psalms119:115 Depart from me
Psalms119:116 Sustain me according to Your word
Psalms119:117 Uphold me that I may be safe
Psalms119:118 You have rejected all those who wander from Your statutes
Psalms119:119 You have removed all the wicked of the earth like dross; Therefore I love Your testimonies.
Psalms119:120 My flesh trembles for fear of You
Psalms119:121 I have done justice and righteousness; Do not leave me to my oppressors.
Psalms119:122 Be surety for Your servant for good; Do not let the arrogant oppress me.
Psalms119:123 My eyes fail with longing for Your salvation And for Your righteous word.
Psalms119:124 Deal with Your servant according to Your lovingkindness And teach me Your statutes.
Psalms119:125 I am Your servant; give me understanding
Psalms119:126 It is time for the LORD to act
Psalms119:127 Therefore I love Your commandments Above gold
Psalms119:128 Therefore I esteem right all Your precepts concerning everything
Psalms119:129 Your testimonies are wonderful; Therefore my soul observes them.
Psalms119:130 The unfolding of Your words gives light; It gives understanding to the simple.
Psalms119:131 I opened my mouth wide and panted
Psalms119:132 Turn to me and be gracious to me
Psalms119:133 Establish my footsteps in Your word
Psalms119:134 Redeem me from the oppression of man
Psalms119:135 Make Your face shine upon Your servant
Psalms119:136 My eyes shed streams of water
Psalms119:137 Righteous are You
Psalms119:138 You have commanded Your testimonies in righteousness And exceeding faithfulness.
Psalms119:139 My zeal has consumed me
Psalms119:140 Your word is very pure
Psalms119:141 I am small and despised
Psalms119:142 Your righteousness is an everlasting righteousness
Psalms119:143 Trouble and anguish have come upon me
Psalms119:144 Your testimonies are righteous forever; Give me understanding that I may live.
Psalms119:145 I cried with all my heart; answer me
Psalms119:146 I cried to You; save me And I shall keep Your testimonies.
Psalms119:147 I rise before dawn and cry for help; I wait for Your words.
Psalms119:148 My eyes anticipate the night watches
Psalms119:149 Hear my voice according to Your lovingkindness; Revive me
Psalms119:150 Those who follow after wickedness draw near; They are far from Your law.
Psalms119:151 You are near
Psalms119:152 Of old I have known from Your testimonies That You have founded them forever.
Psalms119:153 Look upon my affliction and rescue me
Psalms119:154 Plead my cause and redeem me; Revive me according to Your word.
Psalms119:155 Salvation is far from the wicked
Psalms119:156 Great are Your mercies
Psalms119:157 Many are my persecutors and my adversaries
Psalms119:158 I behold the treacherous and loathe them
Psalms119:159 Consider how I love Your precepts; Revive me
Psalms119:160 The sum of Your word is truth
Psalms119:161 Princes persecute me without cause
Psalms119:162 I rejoice at Your word
Psalms119:163 I hate and despise falsehood
Psalms119:164 Seven times a day I praise You
Psalms119:165 Those who love Your law have great peace

Psalms147:3 He heals the brokenhearted And binds up their wounds.
Psalms147:4 He counts the number of the stars; He gives names to all of them.
Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord and abundant in strength; His understanding is infinite.
Psalms147:6 The LORD supports the afflicted; He brings down the wicked to the ground.

Psalms147:8 Who covers the heavens with clouds
Psalms147:9 He gives to the beast its food

Psalms147:13 For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your sons within you.
Psalms147:14 He makes peace in your borders; He satisfies you with the finest of the wheat.

Psalms147:16 He gives snow like wool; He scatters the frost like ashes.

Psalms147:18 He sends forth His word and melts them; He causes His wind to blow and the waters to flow.

Psalms149:2 Let Israel be glad in his Maker; Let the sons of Zion rejoice in their King.
Psalms149:3 Let them praise His name with dancing; Let them sing praises to Him with timbrel and lyre.
Psalms149:4 For the LORD takes pleasure in His people; He will beautify the afflicted ones with salvation.

Psalms149:7 To execute vengeance on the nations And punishment on the peoples

Psalms150:1 Praise the LORD! Praise God in His sanctuary; Praise Him in His mighty expanse.
Psalms150:2 Praise Him for His mighty deeds; Praise Him according to His excellent greatness.
Psalms150:3 Praise Him with trumpet sound; Praise Him with harp and lyre.
Psalms150:4 Praise Him with timbrel and dancing; Praise Him with stringed instruments and pipe.
Psalms150:5 Praise Him with loud cymbals; Praise Him with resounding cymbals.
Psalms150:6 Let everything that has breath praise the LORD. Praise the LORD!
Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon the son of David
Proverbs1:2 To know wisdom and instruction
Proverbs1:3 To receive instruction in wise behavior
Proverbs1:4 To give prudence to the naive
Proverbs1:5 A wise man will hear and increase in learning
Proverbs1:6 To understand a proverb and a figure
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; Fools despise wisdom and instruction.
Proverbs1:8 Hear
Proverbs1:9 Indeed
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 Let us swallow them alive like Sheol
Proverbs1:13 We will find all kinds of precious wealth
Proverbs1:14 Throw in your lot with us
Proverbs1:15 My son
Proverbs1:16 For their feet run to evil And they hasten to shed blood.
Proverbs1:17 Indeed
Proverbs1:18 But they lie in wait for their own blood; They ambush their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 So are the ways of everyone who gains by violence; It takes away the life of its possessors.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom shouts in the street
Proverbs1:21 At the head of the noisy streets she cries out; At the entrance of the gates in the city she utters her sayings:
Proverbs1:22 How long
Proverbs1:23 Turn to my reproof
Proverbs1:24 Because I called and you refused
Proverbs1:25 And you neglected all my counsel And did not want my reproof;
Proverbs1:26 I will also laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your dread comes
Proverbs1:27 When your dread comes like a storm And your calamity comes like a whirlwind
Proverbs1:28 Then they will call on me
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge And did not choose the fear of the LORD.
Proverbs1:30 They would not accept my counsel
Proverbs1:31 So they shall eat of the fruit of their own way And be satiated with their own devices.
Proverbs1:32 For the waywardness of the naive will kill them
Proverbs1:33 But he who listens to me shall live securely And will be at ease from the dread of evil.
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 Make your ear attentive to wisdom
Proverbs2:3 For if you cry for discernment
Proverbs2:4 If you seek her as silver And search for her as for hidden treasures;
Proverbs2:5 Then you will discern the fear of the LORD And discover the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For the LORD gives wisdom; From His mouth come knowledge and understanding.
Proverbs2:7 He stores up sound wisdom for the upright; He is a shield to those who walk in integrity
Proverbs2:8 Guarding the paths of justice
Proverbs2:9 Then you will discern righteousness and justice And equity and every good course.
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom will enter your heart And knowledge will be pleasant to your soul;
Proverbs2:11 Discretion will guard you
Proverbs2:12 To deliver you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 From those who leave the paths of uprightness To walk in the ways of darkness;
Proverbs2:14 Who delight in doing evil And rejoice in the perversity of evil;
Proverbs2:15 Whose paths are crooked
Proverbs2:16 To deliver you from the strange woman
Proverbs2:17 That leaves the companion of her youth And forgets the covenant of her God;
Proverbs2:18 For her house sinks down to death And her tracks lead to the dead;
Proverbs2:19 None who go to her return again
Proverbs2:20 So you will walk in the way of good men And keep to the paths of the righteous.
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will live in the land And the blameless will remain in it;
Proverbs2:22 But the wicked will be cut off from the land And the treacherous will be uprooted from it.
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 For length of days and years of life And peace they will add to you.
Proverbs3:3 Do not let kindness and truth leave you; Bind them around your neck
Proverbs3:4 So you will find favor and good repute In the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart And do not lean on your own understanding.
Proverbs3:6 In all your ways acknowledge Him
Proverbs3:7 Do not be wise in your own eyes; Fear the LORD and turn away from evil.
Proverbs3:8 It will be healing to your body And refreshment to your bones.
Proverbs3:9 Honor the LORD from your wealth And from the first of all your produce;
Proverbs3:10 So your barns will be filled with plenty And your vats will overflow with new wine.
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 For whom the LORD loves He reproves
Proverbs3:13 How blessed is the man who finds wisdom And the man who gains understanding.
Proverbs3:14 For her profit is better than the profit of silver And her gain better than fine gold.
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than jewels; And nothing you desire compares with her.
Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand; In her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are pleasant ways And all her paths are peace.
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her
Proverbs3:19 The LORD by wisdom founded the earth
Proverbs3:20 By His knowledge the deeps were broken up And the skies drip with dew.
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 So they will be life to your soul And adornment to your neck.
Proverbs3:23 Then you will walk in your way securely And your foot will not stumble.
Proverbs3:24 When you lie down
Proverbs3:25 Do not be afraid of sudden fear Nor of the onslaught of the wicked when it comes;
Proverbs3:26 For the LORD will be your confidence And will keep your foot from being caught.
Proverbs3:27 Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due
Proverbs3:28 Do not say to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Do not devise harm against your neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Do not contend with a man without cause
Proverbs3:31 Do not envy a man of violence And do not choose any of his ways.
Proverbs3:32 For the devious are an abomination to the LORD; But He is intimate with the upright.
Proverbs3:33 The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 Though He scoffs at the scoffers
Proverbs3:35 The wise will inherit honor
Proverbs4:1 Hear
Proverbs4:2 For I give you sound teaching; Do not abandon my instruction.
Proverbs4:3 When I was a son to my father
Proverbs4:4 Then he taught me and said to me
Proverbs4:5 Acquire wisdom! Acquire understanding! Do not forget nor turn away from the words of my mouth.
Proverbs4:6 Do not forsake her
Proverbs4:7 The beginning of wisdom is: Acquire wisdom; And with all your acquiring
Proverbs4:8 Prize her
Proverbs4:9 She will place on your head a garland of grace; She will present you with a crown of beauty.
Proverbs4:10 Hear
Proverbs4:11 I have directed you in the way of wisdom; I have led you in upright paths.
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Take hold of instruction; do not let go. Guard her
Proverbs4:14 Do not enter the path of the wicked And do not proceed in the way of evil men.
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it
Proverbs4:16 For they cannot sleep unless they do evil; And they are robbed of sleep unless they make someone stumble.
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness And drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is like darkness; They do not know over what they stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Do not let them depart from your sight; Keep them in the midst of your heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to those who find them And health to all their body.
Proverbs4:23 Watch over your heart with all diligence
Proverbs4:24 Put away from you a deceitful mouth And put devious speech far from you.
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look directly ahead And let your gaze be fixed straight in front of you.
Proverbs4:26 Watch the path of your feet And all your ways will be established.
Proverbs4:27 Do not turn to the right nor to the left; Turn your foot from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 That you may observe discretion And your lips may reserve knowledge.
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of an adulteress drip honey And smoother than oil is her speech;
Proverbs5:4 But in the end she is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death
Proverbs5:6 She does not ponder the path of life; Her ways are unstable
Proverbs5:7 Now then
Proverbs5:8 Keep your way far from her And do not go near the door of her house
Proverbs5:9 Or you will give your vigor to others And your years to the cruel one;
Proverbs5:10 And strangers will be filled with your strength And your hard-earned goods will go to the house of an alien;
Proverbs5:11 And you groan at your final end
Proverbs5:12 And you say
Proverbs5:13 I have not listened to the voice of my teachers
Proverbs5:14 I was almost in utter ruin In the midst of the assembly and congregation.
Proverbs5:15 Drink water from your own cistern And fresh water from your own well.
Proverbs5:16 Should your springs be dispersed abroad
Proverbs5:17 Let them be yours alone And not for strangers with you.
Proverbs5:18 Let your fountain be blessed
Proverbs5:19 As a loving hind and a graceful doe
Proverbs5:20 For why should you
Proverbs5:21 For the ways of a man are before the eyes of the LORD
Proverbs5:22 His own iniquities will capture the wicked
Proverbs5:23 He will die for lack of instruction
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 If you have been snared with the words of your mouth
Proverbs6:3 Do this then
Proverbs6:4 Give no sleep to your eyes
Proverbs6:5 Deliver yourself like a gazelle from the hunter's hand And like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant
Proverbs6:7 Which
Proverbs6:8 Prepares her food in the summer And gathers her provision in the harvest.
Proverbs6:9 How long will you lie down
Proverbs6:10 A little sleep
Proverbs6:11 Your poverty will come in like a vagabond And your need like an armed man.
Proverbs6:12 A worthless person
Proverbs6:13 Who winks with his eyes
Proverbs6:14 Who with perversity in his heart continually devises evil
Proverbs6:15 Therefore his calamity will come suddenly; Instantly he will be broken and there will be no healing.
Proverbs6:16 There are six things which the LORD hates
Proverbs6:17 Haughty eyes
Proverbs6:18 A heart that devises wicked plans
Proverbs6:19 A false witness who utters lies
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 Bind them continually on your heart; Tie them around your neck.
Proverbs6:22 When you walk about
Proverbs6:23 For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching is light; And reproofs for discipline are the way of life
Proverbs6:24 To keep you from the evil woman
Proverbs6:25 Do not desire her beauty in your heart
Proverbs6:26 For on account of a harlot one is reduced to a loaf of bread
Proverbs6:27 Can a man take fire in his bosom And his clothes not be burned?
Proverbs6:28 Or can a man walk on hot coals And his feet not be scorched?
Proverbs6:29 So is the one who goes in to his neighbor's wife; Whoever touches her will not go unpunished.
Proverbs6:30 Men do not despise a thief if he steals To satisfy himself when he is hungry;
Proverbs6:31 But when he is found
Proverbs6:32 The one who commits adultery with a woman is lacking sense; He who would destroy himself does it.
Proverbs6:33 Wounds and disgrace he will find
Proverbs6:34 For jealousy enrages a man
Proverbs6:35 He will not accept any ransom
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commandments and live
Proverbs7:3 Bind them on your fingers; Write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 That they may keep you from an adulteress
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house I looked out through my lattice
Proverbs7:7 And I saw among the naive
Proverbs7:8 Passing through the street near her corner; And he takes the way to her house
Proverbs7:9 In the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And behold
Proverbs7:11 She is boisterous and rebellious
Proverbs7:12 She is now in the streets
Proverbs7:13 So she seizes him and kisses him And with a brazen face she says to him:
Proverbs7:14 I was due to offer peace offerings; Today I have paid my vows.
Proverbs7:15 Therefore I have come out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 I have spread my couch with coverings
Proverbs7:17 I have sprinkled my bed With myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home
Proverbs7:20 He has taken a bag of money with him
Proverbs7:21 With her many persuasions she entices him; With her flattering lips she seduces him.
Proverbs7:22 Suddenly he follows her As an ox goes to the slaughter
Proverbs7:23 Until an arrow pierces through his liver; As a bird hastens to the snare
Proverbs7:24 Now therefore
Proverbs7:25 Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways
Proverbs7:26 For many are the victims she has cast down
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to Sheol
Proverbs8:1 Does not wisdom call
Proverbs8:2 On top of the heights beside the way
Proverbs8:3 Beside the gates
Proverbs8:4 To you
Proverbs8:5 O naive ones
Proverbs8:6 Listen
Proverbs8:7 For my mouth will utter truth; And wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Proverbs8:8 All the utterances of my mouth are in righteousness; There is nothing crooked or perverted in them.
Proverbs8:9 They are all straightforward to him who understands
Proverbs8:10 Take my instruction and not silver
Proverbs8:11 For wisdom is better than jewels; And all desirable things cannot compare with her.
Proverbs8:12 I
Proverbs8:13 The fear of the LORD is to hate evil; Pride and arrogance and the evil way And the perverted mouth
Proverbs8:14 Counsel is mine and sound wisdom; I am understanding
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 By me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me; And those who diligently seek me will find me.
Proverbs8:18 Riches and honor are with me
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 To endow those who love me with wealth
Proverbs8:22 The LORD possessed me at the beginning of His way
Proverbs8:23 From everlasting I was established
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths I was brought forth
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains were settled
Proverbs8:26 While He had not yet made the earth and the fields
Proverbs8:27 When He established the heavens
Proverbs8:28 When He made firm the skies above
Proverbs8:29 When He set for the sea its boundary So that the water would not transgress His command
Proverbs8:30 Then I was beside Him
Proverbs8:31 Rejoicing in the world
Proverbs8:32 Now therefore
Proverbs8:33 Heed instruction and be wise
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the man who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 For he who finds me finds life And obtains favor from the LORD.
Proverbs8:36 But he who sins against me injures himself; All those who hate me love death.

Proverbs30:1 The words of Agur the son of Jakeh
Proverbs30:2 Surely I am more stupid than any man
Proverbs30:3 Neither have I learned wisdom
Proverbs30:4 Who has ascended into heaven and descended? Who has gathered the wind in His fists? Who has wrapped the waters in His garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is His name or His son's name? Surely you know!
Proverbs30:5 Every word of God is tested; He is a shield to those who take refuge in Him.
Proverbs30:6 Do not add to His words Or He will reprove you
Proverbs30:7 Two things I asked of You
Proverbs30:8 Keep deception and lies far from me
Proverbs30:9 That I not be full and deny You and say
Proverbs30:10 Do not slander a slave to his master
Proverbs30:11 There is a kind of man who curses his father And does not bless his mother.
Proverbs30:12 There is a kind who is pure in his own eyes
Proverbs30:13 There is a kind--oh how lofty are his eyes! And his eyelids are raised in arrogance.
Proverbs30:14 There is a kind of man whose teeth are like swords And his jaw teeth like knives
Proverbs30:15 The leech has two daughters
Proverbs30:16 Sheol
Proverbs30:17 The eye that mocks a father And scorns a mother

Proverbs30:22 Under a slave when he becomes king
Proverbs30:23 Under an unloved woman when she gets a husband

Proverbs30:25 The ants are not a strong people
Proverbs30:26 The shephanim are not mighty people

Proverbs31:1 The words of King Lemuel
Proverbs31:2 What

Proverbs31:5 For they will drink and forget what is decreed

Proverbs31:8 Open your mouth for the mute
Proverbs31:9 Open your mouth
Proverbs31:10 An excellent wife
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband trusts in her
Proverbs31:12 She does him good and not evil All the days of her life.
Proverbs31:13 She looks for wool and flax And works with her hands in delight.
Proverbs31:14 She is like merchant ships; She brings her food from afar.
Proverbs31:15 She rises also while it is still night And gives food to her household And portions to her maidens.
Proverbs31:16 She considers a field and buys it; From her earnings she plants a vineyard.
Proverbs31:17 She girds herself with strength And makes her arms strong.
Proverbs31:18 She senses that her gain is good; Her lamp does not go out at night.
Proverbs31:19 She stretches out her hands to the distaff
Proverbs31:20 She extends her hand to the poor
Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid of the snow for her household
Proverbs31:22 She makes coverings for herself; Her clothing is fine linen and purple.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is known in the gates
Proverbs31:24 She makes linen garments and sells them
Proverbs31:25 Strength and dignity are her clothing
Proverbs31:26 She opens her mouth in wisdom
Proverbs31:27 She looks well to the ways of her household
Proverbs31:28 Her children rise up and bless her; Her husband also
Proverbs31:29 Many daughters have done nobly
Proverbs31:30 Charm is deceitful and beauty is vain
Proverbs31:31 Give her the product of her hands
Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What advantage does man have in all his work Which he does under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 A generation goes and a generation comes
Ecclesiastes1:5 Also
Ecclesiastes1:6 Blowing toward the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the rivers flow into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are wearisome; Man is not able to tell it. The eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 That which has been is that which will be
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there anything of which one might say
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of earlier things; And also of the later things which will occur
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I set my mind to seek and explore by wisdom concerning all that has been done under heaven. It is a grievous task which God has given to the sons of men to be afflicted with.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the works which have been done under the sun
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be straightened and what is lacking cannot be counted.
Ecclesiastes1:16 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I set my mind to know wisdom and to know madness and folly; I realized that this also is striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes1:18 Because in much wisdom there is much grief
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I explored with my mind how to stimulate my body with wine while my mind was guiding me wisely
Ecclesiastes2:4 I enlarged my works: I built houses for myself
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and parks for myself and I planted in them all kinds of fruit trees;
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made ponds of water for myself from which to irrigate a forest of growing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male and female slaves and I had homeborn slaves. Also I possessed flocks and herds larger than all who preceded me in Jerusalem.
Ecclesiastes2:8 Also
Ecclesiastes2:9 Then I became great and increased more than all who preceded me in Jerusalem. My wisdom also stood by me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 All that my eyes desired I did not refuse them. I did not withhold my heart from any pleasure
Ecclesiastes2:11 Thus I considered all my activities which my hands had done and the labor which I had exerted
Ecclesiastes2:12 So I turned to consider wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 And I saw that wisdom excels folly as light excels darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man's eyes are in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:16 For there is no lasting remembrance of the wise man as with the fool
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 Thus I hated all the fruit of my labor for which I had labored under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows whether he will be a wise man or a fool? Yet he will have control over all the fruit of my labor for which I have labored by acting wisely under the sun. This too is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:20 Therefore I completely despaired of all the fruit of my labor for which I had labored under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 When there is a man who has labored with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what does a man get in all his labor and in his striving with which he labors under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 Because all his days his task is painful and grievous; even at night his mind does not rest. This too is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for a man than to eat and drink and tell himself that his labor is good. This also I have seen that it is from the hand of God.
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can eat and who can have enjoyment without Him?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to a person who is good in His sight He has given wisdom and knowledge and joy
Ecclesiastes3:1 There is an appointed time for everything. And there is a time for every event under heaven--
Ecclesiastes3:2 A time to give birth and a time to die; A time to plant and a time to uproot what is planted.
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to kill and a time to heal; A time to tear down and a time to build up.
Ecclesiastes3:4 A time to weep and a time to laugh; A time to mourn and a time to dance.
Ecclesiastes3:5 A time to throw stones and a time to gather stones; A time to embrace and a time to shun embracing.
Ecclesiastes3:6 A time to search and a time to give up as lost; A time to keep and a time to throw away.
Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to tear apart and a time to sew together; A time to be silent and a time to speak.
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love and a time to hate; A time for war and a time for peace.
Ecclesiastes3:9 What profit is there to the worker from that in which he toils?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the task which God has given the sons of men with which to occupy themselves.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything appropriate in its time. He has also set eternity in their heart
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for them than to rejoice and to do good in one's lifetime;
Ecclesiastes3:13 moreover
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that everything God does will remain forever; there is nothing to add to it and there is nothing to take from it
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is has been already and that which will be has already been
Ecclesiastes3:16 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said to myself concerning the sons of men
Ecclesiastes3:19 For the fate of the sons of men and the fate of beasts is the same. As one dies so dies the other; indeed
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to the same place. All came from the dust and all return to the dust.
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows that the breath of man ascends upward and the breath of the beast descends downward to the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 I have seen that nothing is better than that man should be happy in his activities
Ecclesiastes4:1 Then I looked again at all the acts of oppression which were being done under the sun. And behold I saw the tears of the oppressed and that they had no one to comfort them; and on the side of their oppressors was power
Ecclesiastes4:2 So I congratulated the dead who are already dead more than the living who are still living.
Ecclesiastes4:3 But better off than both of them is the one who has never existed
Ecclesiastes4:4 I have seen that every labor and every skill which is done is the result of rivalry between a man and his neighbor. This too is vanity and striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands and consumes his own flesh.
Ecclesiastes4:6 One hand full of rest is better than two fists full of labor and striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:7 Then I looked again at vanity under the sun.
Ecclesiastes4:8 There was a certain man without a dependent
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one because they have a good return for their labor.
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if either of them falls
Ecclesiastes4:11 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes4:12 And if one can overpower him who is alone
Ecclesiastes4:13 A poor yet wise lad is better than an old and foolish king who no longer knows how to receive instruction.
Ecclesiastes4:14 For he has come out of prison to become king
Ecclesiastes4:15 I have seen all the living under the sun throng to the side of the second lad who replaces him.
Ecclesiastes4:16 There is no end to all the people
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps as you go to the house of God and draw near to listen rather than to offer the sacrifice of fools; for they do not know they are doing evil.
Ecclesiastes5:2 Do not be hasty in word or impulsive in thought to bring up a matter in the presence of God. For God is in heaven and you are on the earth; therefore let your words be few.
Ecclesiastes5:3 For the dream comes through much effort and the voice of a fool through many words.
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not let your speech cause you to sin and do not say in the presence of the messenger of God that it was a mistake. Why should God be angry on account of your voice and destroy the work of your hands?
Ecclesiastes5:7 For in many dreams and in many words there is emptiness. Rather
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see oppression of the poor and denial of justice and righteousness in the province
Ecclesiastes5:9 After all
Ecclesiastes5:10 He who loves money will not be satisfied with money
Ecclesiastes5:11 When good things increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of the working man is pleasant
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a grievous evil which I have seen under the sun: riches being hoarded by their owner to his hurt.
Ecclesiastes5:14 When those riches were lost through a bad investment and he had fathered a son
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he had come naked from his mother's womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 This also is a grievous evil--exactly as a man is born
Ecclesiastes5:17 Throughout his life he also eats in darkness with great vexation
Ecclesiastes5:18 Here is what I have seen to be good and fitting: to eat
Ecclesiastes5:19 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he will not often consider the years of his life

Ecclesiastes6:2 Do not be hasty in word or impulsive in thought to bring up a matter in the presence of God. For God is in heaven and you are on the earth; therefore let your words be few.
Ecclesiastes6:3 For the dream comes through much effort and the voice of a fool through many words.
Ecclesiastes6:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes6:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.
Ecclesiastes6:6 Do not let your speech cause you to sin and do not say in the presence of the messenger of God that it was a mistake. Why should God be angry on account of your voice and destroy the work of your hands?
Ecclesiastes6:7 For in many dreams and in many words there is emptiness. Rather
Ecclesiastes6:8 If you see oppression of the poor and denial of justice and righteousness in the province
Ecclesiastes6:9 After all
Ecclesiastes6:10 He who loves money will not be satisfied with money
Ecclesiastes6:11 When good things increase

Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than a good ointment
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to a house of mourning Than to go to a house of feasting
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The mind of the wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to listen to the rebuke of a wise man Than for one to listen to the song of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:6 For as the crackling of thorn bushes under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 For oppression makes a wise man mad
Ecclesiastes7:8 The end of a matter is better than its beginning; Patience of spirit is better than haughtiness of spirit.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Do not be eager in your heart to be angry
Ecclesiastes7:10 Do not say
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom along with an inheritance is good And an advantage to those who see the sun.
Ecclesiastes7:12 For wisdom is protection just as money is protection
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be happy
Ecclesiastes7:15 I have seen everything during my lifetime of futility; there is a righteous man who perishes in his righteousness and there is a wicked man who prolongs his life in his wickedness.
Ecclesiastes7:16 Do not be excessively righteous and do not be overly wise. Why should you ruin yourself?
Ecclesiastes7:17 Do not be excessively wicked and do not be a fool. Why should you die before your time?
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that you grasp one thing and also not let go of the other; for the one who fears God comes forth with both of them.
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom strengthens a wise man more than ten rulers who are in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Indeed
Ecclesiastes7:21 Also
Ecclesiastes7:22 For you also have realized that you likewise have many times cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 I tested all this with wisdom
Ecclesiastes7:24 What has been is remote and exceedingly mysterious. Who can discover it?
Ecclesiastes7:25 I directed my mind to know
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I discovered more bitter than death the woman whose heart is snares and nets
Ecclesiastes7:27 Behold
Ecclesiastes7:28 which I am still seeking but have not found. I have found one man among a thousand
Ecclesiastes7:29 Behold
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like the wise man and who knows the interpretation of a matter? A man's wisdom illumines him and causes his stern face to beam.
Ecclesiastes8:2 I say
Ecclesiastes8:3 Do not be in a hurry to leave him. Do not join in an evil matter
Ecclesiastes8:4 Since the word of the king is authoritative
Ecclesiastes8:5 He who keeps a royal command experiences no trouble
Ecclesiastes8:6 For there is a proper time and procedure for every delight
Ecclesiastes8:7 If no one knows what will happen
Ecclesiastes8:8 No man has authority to restrain the wind with the wind
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I have seen and applied my mind to every deed that has been done under the sun wherein a man has exercised authority over another man to his hurt.
Ecclesiastes8:10 So then
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because the sentence against an evil deed is not executed quickly
Ecclesiastes8:12 Although a sinner does evil a hundred times and may lengthen his life
Ecclesiastes8:13 But it will not be well for the evil man and he will not lengthen his days like a shadow
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is futility which is done on the earth
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I commended pleasure
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I gave my heart to know wisdom and to see the task which has been done on the earth (even though one should never sleep day or night)
Ecclesiastes8:17 and I saw every work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 For I have taken all this to my heart and explain it that righteous men
Ecclesiastes9:2 It is the same for all. There is one fate for the righteous and for the wicked; for the good
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:4 For whoever is joined with all the living
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know they will die; but the dead do not know anything
Ecclesiastes9:6 Indeed their love
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go then
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let your clothes be white all the time
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with the woman whom you love all the days of your fleeting life which He has given to you under the sun; for this is your reward in life and in your toil in which you have labored under the sun.
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 I again saw under the sun that the race is not to the swift and the battle is not to the warriors
Ecclesiastes9:12 Moreover
Ecclesiastes9:13 Also this I came to see as wisdom under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small city with few men in it and a great king came to it
Ecclesiastes9:15 But there was found in it a poor wise man and he delivered the city by his wisdom. Yet no one remembered that poor man.
Ecclesiastes9:16 So I said
Ecclesiastes9:17 The words of the wise heard in quietness are better than the shouting of a ruler among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies make a perfumer's oil stink
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise man's heart directs him toward the right
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even when the fool walks along the road
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the ruler's temper rises against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 folly is set in many exalted places while rich men sit in humble places.
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves riding on horses and princes walking like slaves on the land.
Ecclesiastes10:8 He who digs a pit may fall into it
Ecclesiastes10:9 He who quarries stones may be hurt by them
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the axe is dull and he does not sharpen its edge
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the serpent bites before being charmed
Ecclesiastes10:12 Words from the mouth of a wise man are gracious
Ecclesiastes10:13 the beginning of his talking is folly and the end of it is wicked madness.
Ecclesiastes10:14 Yet the fool multiplies words. No man knows what will happen
Ecclesiastes10:15 The toil of a fool so wearies him that he does not even know how to go to a city.
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Blessed are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 Through indolence the rafters sag
Ecclesiastes10:19 Men prepare a meal for enjoyment
Ecclesiastes10:20 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes11:1 Cast your bread on the surface of the waters
Ecclesiastes11:2 Divide your portion to seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds are full
Ecclesiastes11:4 He who watches the wind will not sow and he who looks at the clouds will not reap.
Ecclesiastes11:5 Just as you do not know the path of the wind and how bones are formed in the womb of the pregnant woman
Ecclesiastes11:6 Sow your seed in the morning and do not be idle in the evening
Ecclesiastes11:7 The light is pleasant
Ecclesiastes11:8 Indeed
Ecclesiastes11:9 Rejoice
Ecclesiastes11:10 So
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember also your Creator in the days of your youth
Ecclesiastes12:2 before the sun and the light
Ecclesiastes12:3 in the day that the watchmen of the house tremble
Ecclesiastes12:4 and the doors on the street are shut as the sound of the grinding mill is low
Ecclesiastes12:5 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes12:6 Remember Him before the silver cord is broken and the golden bowl is crushed
Ecclesiastes12:7 then the dust will return to the earth as it was
Ecclesiastes12:8 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes12:9 In addition to being a wise man
Ecclesiastes12:10 The Preacher sought to find delightful words and to write words of truth correctly.
Ecclesiastes12:11 The words of wise men are like goads
Ecclesiastes12:12 But beyond this
Ecclesiastes12:13 The conclusion
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God will bring every act to judgment

Isaiah1:1 The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz concerning Judah and Jerusalem
Isaiah1:2 Listen
Isaiah1:3 An ox knows its owner
Isaiah1:4 Alas
Isaiah1:5 Where will you be stricken again
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head There is nothing sound in it
Isaiah1:7 Your land is desolate
Isaiah1:8 The daughter of Zion is left like a shelter in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 Unless the LORD of hosts Had left us a few survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the LORD
Isaiah1:11 What are your multiplied sacrifices to Me? Says the LORD. I have had enough of burnt offerings of rams And the fat of fed cattle; And I take no pleasure in the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before Me
Isaiah1:13 Bring your worthless offerings no longer
Isaiah1:14 I hate your new moon festivals and your appointed feasts
Isaiah1:15 So when you spread out your hands in prayer
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves
Isaiah1:17 Learn to do good; Seek justice
Isaiah1:18 Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you consent and obey
Isaiah1:20 But if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How the faithful city has become a harlot
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your rulers are rebels And companions of thieves; Everyone loves a bribe And chases after rewards. They do not defend the orphan
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord GOD of hosts
Isaiah1:25 I will also turn My hand against you
Isaiah1:26 Then I will restore your judges as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion will be redeemed with justice And her repentant ones with righteousness.
Isaiah1:28 But transgressors and sinners will be crushed together
Isaiah1:29 Surely you will be ashamed of the oaks which you have desired
Isaiah1:30 For you will be like an oak whose leaf fades away Or as a garden that has no water.
Isaiah1:31 The strong man will become tinder
Isaiah2:1 The word which Isaiah the son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
Isaiah2:2 Now it will come about that In the last days The mountain of the house of the LORD Will be established as the chief of the mountains
Isaiah2:3 And many peoples will come and say
Isaiah2:4 And He will judge between the nations
Isaiah2:5 Come
Isaiah2:6 For You have abandoned Your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land has also been filled with silver and gold And there is no end to their treasures; Their land has also been filled with horses And there is no end to their chariots.
Isaiah2:8 Their land has also been filled with idols; They worship the work of their hands
Isaiah2:9 So the common man has been humbled And the man of importance has been abased
Isaiah2:10 Enter the rock and hide in the dust From the terror of the LORD and from the splendor of His majesty.
Isaiah2:11 The proud look of man will be abased And the loftiness of man will be humbled
Isaiah2:12 For the LORD of hosts will have a day of reckoning Against everyone who is proud and lofty And against everyone who is lifted up
Isaiah2:13 And it will be against all the cedars of Lebanon that are lofty and lifted up
Isaiah2:14 Against all the lofty mountains
Isaiah2:15 Against every high tower
Isaiah2:16 Against all the ships of Tarshish And against all the beautiful craft.
Isaiah2:17 The pride of man will be humbled And the loftiness of men will be abased; And the LORD alone will be exalted in that day
Isaiah2:18 But the idols will completely vanish.
Isaiah2:19 Men will go into caves of the rocks And into holes of the ground Before the terror of the LORD And the splendor of His majesty
Isaiah2:20 In that day men will cast away to the moles and the bats Their idols of silver and their idols of gold
Isaiah2:21 In order to go into the caverns of the rocks and the clefts of the cliffs Before the terror of the LORD and the splendor of His majesty
Isaiah2:22 Stop regarding man
Isaiah3:1 For behold
Isaiah3:2 The mighty man and the warrior
Isaiah3:3 The captain of fifty and the honorable man
Isaiah3:4 And I will make mere lads their princes
Isaiah3:5 And the people will be oppressed
Isaiah3:6 When a man lays hold of his brother in his father's house
Isaiah3:7 He will protest on that day
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen
Isaiah3:9 The expression of their faces bears witness against them
Isaiah3:10 Say to the righteous that it will go well with them
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked! It will go badly with him
Isaiah3:12 O My people! Their oppressors are children
Isaiah3:13 The LORD arises to contend
Isaiah3:14 The LORD enters into judgment with the elders and princes of His people
Isaiah3:15 What do you mean by crushing My people And grinding the face of the poor? Declares the Lord GOD of hosts.
Isaiah3:16 Moreover
Isaiah3:17 Therefore the Lord will afflict the scalp of the daughters of Zion with scabs
Isaiah3:18 In that day the Lord will take away the beauty of their anklets

Isaiah3:23 hand mirrors

Isaiah4:1 For seven women will take hold of one man in that day
Isaiah4:2 In that day the Branch of the LORD will be beautiful and glorious
Isaiah4:3 It will come about that he who is left in Zion and remains in Jerusalem will be called holy--everyone who is recorded for life in Jerusalem.
Isaiah4:4 When the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and purged the bloodshed of Jerusalem from her midst
Isaiah4:5 then the LORD will create over the whole area of Mount Zion and over her assemblies a cloud by day

Isaiah5:1 Let me sing now for my well-beloved A song of my beloved concerning His vineyard. My well-beloved had a vineyard on a fertile hill.
Isaiah5:2 He dug it all around
Isaiah5:3 And now
Isaiah5:4 What more was there to do for My vineyard that I have not done in it? Why
Isaiah5:5 So now let Me tell you what I am going to do to My vineyard: I will remove its hedge and it will be consumed; I will break down its wall and it will become trampled ground.
Isaiah5:6 I will lay it waste; It will not be pruned or hoed
Isaiah5:7 For the vineyard of the LORD of hosts is the house of Israel And the men of Judah His delightful plant. Thus He looked for justice
Isaiah5:8 Woe to those who add house to house and join field to field
Isaiah5:9 In my ears the LORD of hosts has sworn
Isaiah5:10 For ten acres of vineyard will yield only one bath of wine
Isaiah5:11 Woe to those who rise early in the morning that they may pursue strong drink
Isaiah5:12 Their banquets are accompanied by lyre and harp
Isaiah5:13 Therefore My people go into exile for their lack of knowledge; And their honorable men are famished
Isaiah5:14 Therefore Sheol has enlarged its throat and opened its mouth without measure; And Jerusalem's splendor
Isaiah5:15 So the common man will be humbled and the man of importance abased
Isaiah5:16 But the LORD of hosts will be exalted in judgment
Isaiah5:17 Then the lambs will graze as in their pasture
Isaiah5:18 Woe to those who drag iniquity with the cords of falsehood
Isaiah5:19 Who say
Isaiah5:20 Woe to those who call evil good
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes And clever in their own sight!
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are heroes in drinking wine And valiant men in mixing strong drink
Isaiah5:23 Who justify the wicked for a bribe
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 On this account the anger of the LORD has burned against His people
Isaiah5:26 He will also lift up a standard to the distant nation
Isaiah5:27 No one in it is weary or stumbles
Isaiah5:28 Its arrows are sharp and all its bows are bent; The hoofs of its horses seem like flint and its chariot wheels like a whirlwind.
Isaiah5:29 Its roaring is like a lioness
Isaiah5:30 And it will growl over it in that day like the roaring of the sea. If one looks to the land
Isaiah6:1 In the year of King Uzziah’s death I saw the Lord sitting on a throne
Isaiah6:2 Seraphim stood above Him
Isaiah6:3 And one called out to another and said

Isaiah7:23 And it will come about in that day

Jeremiah6:21 Therefore
Jeremiah6:22 Thus says the LORD
Jeremiah6:23 They seize bow and spear; They are cruel and have no mercy; Their voice roars like the sea
Jeremiah6:24 We have heard the report of it; Our hands are limp. Anguish has seized us
Jeremiah6:25 Do not go out into the field And do not walk on the road
Jeremiah6:26 O daughter of my people
Jeremiah6:27 I have made you an assayer and a tester among My people
Jeremiah6:28 All of them are stubbornly rebellious
Jeremiah6:29 The bellows blow fiercely
Jeremiah6:30 They call them rejected silver

Jeremiah7:3 Thus says the LORD of hosts

Jeremiah7:5 For if you truly amend your ways and your deeds

Jeremiah7:15 I will cast you out of My sight

Jeremiah7:20 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD
Jeremiah7:21 Thus says the LORD of hosts

Jeremiah7:27 You shall speak all these words to them
Jeremiah7:28 You shall say to them
Jeremiah7:29 'Cut off your hair and cast it away

Jeremiah7:31 They have built the high places of Topheth
Jeremiah7:32 Therefore
Jeremiah7:33 The dead bodies of this people will be food for the birds of the sky and for the beasts of the earth; and no one will frighten them away.

Jeremiah8:2 They will spread them out to the sun
Jeremiah8:3 And death will be chosen rather than life by all the remnant that remains of this evil family
Jeremiah8:4 You shall say to them
Jeremiah8:5 Why then has this people
Jeremiah8:6 I have listened and heard
Jeremiah8:7 Even the stork in the sky Knows her seasons; And the turtledove and the swift and the thrush Observe the time of their migration; But My people do not know The ordinance of the LORD.

Jeremiah8:9 The wise men are put to shame

Jeremiah8:13 I will surely snatch them away
Jeremiah8:14 Why are we sitting still? Assemble yourselves
Jeremiah8:15 We waited for peace

Jeremiah8:18 My sorrow is beyond healing
Jeremiah8:19 Behold
