Jeremiah9:1 Oh that my head were waters And my eyes a fountain of tears
Jeremiah9:2 Oh that I had in the desert A wayfarers' lodging place; That I might leave my people And go from them! For all of them are adulterers
Jeremiah9:3 They bend their tongue like their bow; Lies and not truth prevail in the land; For they proceed from evil to evil
Jeremiah9:4 Let everyone be on guard against his neighbor
Jeremiah9:5 Everyone deceives his neighbor And does not speak the truth
Jeremiah9:6 Your dwelling is in the midst of deceit; Through deceit they refuse to know Me
Jeremiah9:7 Therefore thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah9:8 Their tongue is a deadly arrow; It speaks deceit; With his mouth one speaks peace to his neighbor
Jeremiah9:9 Shall I not punish them for these things? declares the LORD. On a nation such as this Shall I not avenge Myself?
Jeremiah9:10 For the mountains I will take up a weeping and wailing
Jeremiah9:11 I will make Jerusalem a heap of ruins

Jeremiah10:2 Thus says the Lord

Jeremiah10:6 There is none like You
Jeremiah10:7 Who would not fear You

Jeremiah23:12 Therefore their way will be like slippery paths to them
Jeremiah23:13 Moreover

Jeremiah23:22 But if they had stood in My council
Jeremiah23:23 Am I a God who is near
Jeremiah23:24 Can a man hide himself in hiding places So I do not see him? declares the LORD. Do I not fill the heavens and the earth? declares the LORD.
Jeremiah23:25 I have heard what the prophets have said who prophesy falsely in My name
Jeremiah23:26 How long? Is there anything in the hearts of the prophets who prophesy falsehood
Jeremiah23:27 who intend to make My people forget My name by their dreams which they relate to one another

Jeremiah24:2 One basket had very good figs

Jeremiah24:4 Then the word of the LORD came to me

Jeremiah24:9 'I will make them a terror and an evil for all the kingdoms of the earth
Jeremiah24:10 'I will send the sword
Jeremiah25:1 The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah

Jeremiah25:14 '(For many nations and great kings will make slaves of them

Jeremiah25:37 And the peaceful folds are made silent Because of the fierce anger of the LORD.
Jeremiah25:38 He has left His hiding place like the lion; For their land has become a horror Because of the fierceness of the oppressing sword And because of His fierce anger.

Jeremiah38:28 So Jeremiah stayed in the court of the guardhouse until the day that Jerusalem was captured.

Jeremiah39:7 He then blinded Zedekiah's eyes and bound him in fetters of bronze to bring him to Babylon.

Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
Jeremiah52:2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD like all that Jehoiakim had done.
Jeremiah52:3 For through the anger of the LORD this came about in Jerusalem and Judah until He cast them out from His presence. And Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
Jeremiah52:4 Now it came about in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was under siege until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city was broken into
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho
Jeremiah52:9 Then they captured the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 The king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
Jeremiah52:11 Then he blinded the eyes of Zedekiah; and the king of Babylon bound him with bronze fetters and brought him to Babylon and put him in prison until the day of his death.
Jeremiah52:12 Now on the tenth day of the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 He burned the house of the LORD
Jeremiah52:14 So all the army of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the guard broke down all the walls around Jerusalem.
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away into exile some of the poorest of the people
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and plowmen.
Jeremiah52:17 Now the bronze pillars which belonged to the house of the LORD and the stands and the bronze sea
Jeremiah52:18 They also took away the pots
Jeremiah52:19 The captain of the guard also took away the bowls
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 As for the pillars
Jeremiah52:22 Now a capital of bronze was on it; and the height of each capital was five cubits
Jeremiah52:23 There were ninety-six exposed pomegranates; all the pomegranates numbered a hundred on the network all around.
Jeremiah52:24 Then the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest and Zephaniah the second priest
Jeremiah52:25 He also took from the city one official who was overseer of the men of war
Jeremiah52:26 Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 Then the king of Babylon struck them down and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Judah was led away into exile from its land.
Jeremiah52:28 These are the people whom Nebuchadnezzar carried away into exile: in the seventh year 3
Jeremiah52:29 in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar 832 persons from Jerusalem;
Jeremiah52:30 in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:31 Now it came about in the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 Then he spoke kindly to him and set his throne above the thrones of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin changed his prison clothes
Jeremiah52:34 For his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How lonely sits the city That was full of people! She has become like a widow Who was once great among the nations! She who was a princess among the provinces Has become a forced laborer!
Lamentations1:2 She weeps bitterly in the night And her tears are on her cheeks; She has none to comfort her Among all her lovers. All her friends have dealt treacherously with her; They have become her enemies.
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile under affliction And under harsh servitude; She dwells among the nations
Lamentations1:4 The roads of Zion are in mourning Because no one comes to the appointed feasts. All her gates are desolate; Her priests are groaning
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries have become her masters
Lamentations1:6 All her majesty Has departed from the daughter of Zion; Her princes have become like deer That have found no pasture; And they have fled without strength Before the pursuer.
Lamentations1:7 In the days of her affliction and homelessness Jerusalem remembers all her precious things That were from the days of old
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem sinned greatly
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness was in her skirts; She did not consider her future. Therefore she has fallen astonishingly; She has no comforter. See
Lamentations1:10 The adversary has stretched out his hand Over all her precious things
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan seeking bread; They have given their precious things for food To restore their lives themselves. See
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to all you who pass this way? Look and see if there is any pain like my pain Which was severely dealt out to me
Lamentations1:13 From on high He sent fire into my bones
Lamentations1:14 The yoke of my transgressions is bound; By His hand they are knit together. They have come upon my neck; He has made my strength fail. The Lord has given me into the hands Of those against whom I am not able to stand.
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has rejected all my strong men In my midst; He has called an appointed time against me To crush my young men; The Lord has trodden as in a wine press The virgin daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; My eyes run down with water; Because far from me is a comforter
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands; There is no one to comfort her; The LORD has commanded concerning Jacob That the ones round about him should be his adversaries; Jerusalem has become an unclean thing among them.
Lamentations1:18 The LORD is righteous; For I have rebelled against His command; Hear now
Lamentations1:19 I called to my lovers
Lamentations1:20 See
Lamentations1:21 They have heard that I groan; There is no one to comfort me; All my enemies have heard of my calamity; They are glad that You have done it. Oh
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before You; And deal with them as You have dealt with me For all my transgressions; For my groans are many and my heart is faint.
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord has covered the daughter of Zion With a cloud in His anger! He has cast from heaven to earth The glory of Israel
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has swallowed up; He has not spared All the habitations of Jacob. In His wrath He has thrown down The strongholds of the daughter of Judah; He has brought them down to the ground; He has profaned the kingdom and its princes.
Lamentations2:3 In fierce anger He has cut off All the strength of Israel; He has drawn back His right hand From before the enemy. And He has burned in Jacob like a flaming fire Consuming round about.
Lamentations2:4 He has bent His bow like an enemy; He has set His right hand like an adversary And slain all that were pleasant to the eye; In the tent of the daughter of Zion He has poured out His wrath like fire.
Lamentations2:5 The Lord has become like an enemy. He has swallowed up Israel; He has swallowed up all its palaces
Lamentations2:6 And He has violently treated His tabernacle like a garden booth; He has destroyed His appointed meeting place. The LORD has caused to be forgotten The appointed feast and sabbath in Zion
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected His altar
Lamentations2:8 The LORD determined to destroy The wall of the daughter of Zion. He has stretched out a line
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion Sit on the ground
Lamentations2:11 My eyes fail because of tears
Lamentations2:12 They say to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 How shall I admonish you? To what shall I compare you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have seen for you False and foolish visions; And they have not exposed your iniquity So as to restore you from captivity
Lamentations2:15 All who pass along the way Clap their hands in derision at you; They hiss and shake their heads At the daughter of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies Have opened their mouths wide against you; They hiss and gnash their teeth. They say
Lamentations2:17 The LORD has done what He purposed; He has accomplished His word Which He commanded from days of old. He has thrown down without sparing
Lamentations2:18 Their heart cried out to the Lord
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 See
Lamentations2:21 On the ground in the streets Lie young and old; My virgins and my young men Have fallen by the sword. You have slain them in the day of Your anger
Lamentations2:22 You called as in the day of an appointed feast My terrors on every side; And there was no one who escaped or survived In the day of the LORD'S anger. Those whom I bore and reared
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction Because of the rod of His wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has driven me and made me walk In darkness and not in light.
Lamentations3:3 Surely against me He has turned His hand Repeatedly all the day.
Lamentations3:4 He has caused my flesh and my skin to waste away
Lamentations3:5 He has besieged and encompassed me with bitterness and hardship.
Lamentations3:6 In dark places He has made me dwell
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in so that I cannot go out; He has made my chain heavy.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry out and call for help
Lamentations3:9 He has blocked my ways with hewn stone; He has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is to me like a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He has turned aside my ways and torn me to pieces; He has made me desolate.
Lamentations3:12 He bent His bow And set me as a target for the arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He made the arrows of His quiver To enter into my inward parts.
Lamentations3:14 I have become a laughingstock to all my people
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He has broken my teeth with gravel; He has made me cower in the dust.
Lamentations3:17 My soul has been rejected from peace; I have forgotten happiness.
Lamentations3:18 So I say
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and my wandering
Lamentations3:20 Surely my soul remembers And is bowed down within me.
Lamentations3:21 This I recall to my mind
Lamentations3:22 The LORD'S lovingkindnesses indeed never cease
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; Great is Your faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 The LORD is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The LORD is good to those who wait for Him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that he waits silently For the salvation of the LORD.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man that he should bear The yoke in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone and be silent Since He has laid it on him.
Lamentations3:29 Let him put his mouth in the dust
Lamentations3:30 Let him give his cheek to the smiter
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not reject forever
Lamentations3:32 For if He causes grief
Lamentations3:33 For He does not afflict willingly Or grieve the sons of men.
Lamentations3:34 To crush under His feet All the prisoners of the land
Lamentations3:35 To deprive a man of justice In the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 To defraud a man in his lawsuit--Of these things the Lord does not approve.
Lamentations3:37 Who is there who speaks and it comes to pass
Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the mouth of the Most High That both good and ill go forth?
Lamentations3:39 Why should any living mortal
Lamentations3:40 Let us examine and probe our ways
Lamentations3:41 We lift up our heart and hands Toward God in heaven;
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and rebelled
Lamentations3:43 You have covered Yourself with anger And pursued us; You have slain and have not spared.
Lamentations3:44 You have covered Yourself with a cloud So that no prayer can pass through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us mere offscouring and refuse In the midst of the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.
Lamentations3:47 Panic and pitfall have befallen us
Lamentations3:48 My eyes run down with streams of water Because of the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes pour down unceasingly
Lamentations3:50 Until the LORD looks down And sees from heaven.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes bring pain to my soul Because of all the daughters of my city.
Lamentations3:52 My enemies without cause Hunted me down like a bird;
Lamentations3:53 They have silenced me in the pit And have placed a stone on me.
Lamentations3:54 Waters flowed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called on Your name
Lamentations3:56 You have heard my voice
Lamentations3:57 You drew near when I called on You; You said
Lamentations3:58 O Lord
Lamentations3:59 O LORD
Lamentations3:60 You have seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 You have heard their reproach
Lamentations3:62 The lips of my assailants and their whispering Are against me all day long.
Lamentations3:63 Look on their sitting and their rising; I am their mocking song.
Lamentations3:64 You will recompense them
Lamentations3:65 You will give them hardness of heart
Lamentations3:66 You will pursue them in anger and destroy them From under the heavens of the LORD!

Lamentations4:2 The precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even jackals offer the breast

Lamentations4:5 Those who ate delicacies Are desolate in the streets; Those reared in purple Embrace ash pits.

Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth did not believe

Lamentations4:20 The breath of our nostrils
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of your iniquity has been completed
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans without a father
Lamentations5:4 We have to pay for our drinking water
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are at our necks; We are worn out
Lamentations5:6 We have submitted to Egypt and Assyria to get enough bread.
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; There is no one to deliver us from their hand.

Lamentations5:11 They ravished the women in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes were hung by their hands; Elders were not respected.

Lamentations5:14 Elders are gone from the gate

Lamentations5:18 Because of Mount Zion which lies desolate
Lamentations5:19 You
Lamentations5:20 Why do You forget us forever? Why do You forsake us so long?
Lamentations5:21 Restore us to You
Lamentations5:22 Unless You have utterly rejected us And are exceedingly angry with us.
Ezekiel1:1 Now it came about in the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 (On the fifth of the month in the fifth year of King Jehoiachin's exile
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the LORD came expressly to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked
Ezekiel1:5 Within it there were figures resembling four living beings. And this was their appearance: they had human form.
Ezekiel1:6 Each of them had four faces and four wings.
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight and their feet were like a calf's hoof
Ezekiel1:8 Under their wings on their four sides were human hands. As for the faces and wings of the four of them
Ezekiel1:9 their wings touched one another; their faces did not turn when they moved
Ezekiel1:10 As for the form of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 Such were their faces. Their wings were spread out above; each had two touching another being
Ezekiel1:12 And each went straight forward; wherever the spirit was about to go
Ezekiel1:13 In the midst of the living beings there was something that looked like burning coals of fire
Ezekiel1:14 And the living beings ran to and fro like bolts of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 Now as I looked at the living beings
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their workmanship was like sparkling beryl
Ezekiel1:17 Whenever they moved
Ezekiel1:18 As for their rims they were lofty and awesome
Ezekiel1:19 Whenever the living beings moved
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the spirit was about to go
Ezekiel1:21 Whenever those went
Ezekiel1:22 Now over the heads of the living beings there was something like an expanse
Ezekiel1:23 Under the expanse their wings were stretched out straight
Ezekiel1:24 I also heard the sound of their wings like the sound of abundant waters as they went
Ezekiel1:25 And there came a voice from above the expanse that was over their heads; whenever they stood still
Ezekiel1:26 Now above the expanse that was over their heads there was something resembling a throne
Ezekiel1:27 Then I noticed from the appearance of His loins and upward something like glowing metal that looked like fire all around within it
Ezekiel1:28 As the appearance of the rainbow in the clouds on a rainy day

Ezekiel26:5 'She will be a place for the spreading of nets in the midst of the sea
Ezekiel26:6 'Also her daughters who are on the mainland will be slain by the sword
Ezekiel26:7 For thus says the Lord GOD
Ezekiel26:8 He will slay your daughters on the mainland with the sword; and he will make siege walls against you
Ezekiel26:9 The blow of his battering rams he will direct against your walls
Ezekiel26:10 Because of the multitude of his horses
Ezekiel26:11 With the hoofs of his horses he will trample all your streets. He will slay your people with the sword; and your strong pillars will come down to the ground.
Ezekiel26:12 Also they will make a spoil of your riches and a prey of your merchandise
Ezekiel26:13 So I will silence the sound of your songs
Ezekiel26:14 I will make you a bare rock; you will be a place for the spreading of nets. You will be built no more
Ezekiel26:15 Thus says the Lord GOD to Tyre
Ezekiel26:16 Then all the princes of the sea will go down from their thrones
Ezekiel26:17 They will take up a lamentation over you and say to you
Ezekiel26:18 'Now the coastlands will tremble On the day of your fall; Yes
Ezekiel26:19 For thus says the Lord GOD
Ezekiel26:20 then I will bring you down with those who go down to the pit
Ezekiel26:21 I will bring terrors on you and you will be no more; though you will be sought
Ezekiel27:1 Moreover
Ezekiel27:2 And you
Ezekiel27:3 and say to Tyre
Ezekiel27:4 Your borders are in the heart of the seas; Your builders have perfected your beauty.
Ezekiel27:5 They have made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; They have taken a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
Ezekiel27:6 Of oaks from Bashan they have made your oars; With ivory they have inlaid your deck of boxwood from the coastlands of Cyprus.
Ezekiel27:7 Your sail was of fine embroidered linen from Egypt So that it became your distinguishing mark; Your awning was blue and purple from the coastlands of Elishah.
Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; Your wise men
Ezekiel27:9 The elders of Gebal and her wise men were with you repairing your seams; All the ships of the sea and their sailors were with you in order to deal in your merchandise.
Ezekiel27:10 Persia and Lud and Put were in your army
Ezekiel27:11 The sons of Arvad and your army were on your walls
Ezekiel27:12 Tarshish was your customer because of the abundance of all kinds of wealth; with silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 Those from Beth-togarmah gave horses and war horses and mules for your wares.
Ezekiel27:15 The sons of Dedan were your traders. Many coastlands were your market; ivory tusks and ebony they brought as your payment.
Ezekiel27:16 Aram was your customer because of the abundance of your goods; they paid for your wares with emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus was your customer because of the abundance of your goods
Ezekiel27:19 Vedan and Javan paid for your wares from Uzal; wrought iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar
Ezekiel27:22 The traders of Sheba and Raamah
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 They traded with you in choice garments
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish were the carriers for your merchandise. And you were filled and were very glorious In the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:26 Your rowers have brought you Into great waters; The east wind has broken you In the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Your wealth
Ezekiel27:28 At the sound of the cry of your pilots The pasture lands will shake.
Ezekiel27:29 All who handle the oar
Ezekiel27:30 And they will make their voice heard over you And will cry bitterly. They will cast dust on their heads
Ezekiel27:31 Also they will make themselves bald for you And gird themselves with sackcloth; And they will weep for you in bitterness of soul With bitter mourning.
Ezekiel27:32 Moreover
Ezekiel27:33 'When your wares went out from the seas
Ezekiel27:34 'Now that you are broken by the seas In the depths of the waters
Ezekiel27:35 'All the inhabitants of the coastlands Are appalled at you
Ezekiel27:36 'The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; You have become terrified And you will cease to be forever.''

Ezekiel39:25 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD
Ezekiel39:26 They will forget their disgrace and all their treachery which they perpetrated against Me
Ezekiel39:27 When I bring them back from the peoples and gather them from the lands of their enemies
Ezekiel39:28 Then they will know that I am the LORD their God because I made them go into exile among the nations
Ezekiel39:29 I will not hide My face from them any longer
Ezekiel40:1 In the twenty-fifth year of our exile
Ezekiel40:2 In the visions of God He brought me into the land of Israel and set me on a very high mountain
Ezekiel40:3 So He brought me there; and behold
Ezekiel40:4 The man said to me
Ezekiel40:5 And behold
Ezekiel40:6 Then he went to the gate which faced east
Ezekiel40:7 The guardroom was one rod long and one rod wide; and there were five cubits between the guardrooms. And the threshold of the gate by the porch of the gate facing inward was one rod.
Ezekiel40:8 Then he measured the porch of the gate facing inward
Ezekiel40:9 He measured the porch of the gate
Ezekiel40:10 The guardrooms of the gate toward the east numbered three on each side; the three of them had the same measurement. The side pillars also had the same measurement on each side.
Ezekiel40:11 And he measured the width of the gateway
Ezekiel40:12 There was a barrier wall one cubit wide in front of the guardrooms on each side; and the guardrooms were six cubits square on each side.
Ezekiel40:13 He measured the gate from the roof of the one guardroom to the roof of the other
Ezekiel40:14 He made the side pillars sixty cubits high; the gate extended round about to the side pillar of the courtyard.
Ezekiel40:15 From the front of the entrance gate to the front of the inner porch of the gate was fifty cubits.
Ezekiel40:16 There were shuttered windows looking toward the guardrooms

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 The Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 Then the king ordered Ashpenaz
Daniel1:4 youths in whom was no defect
Daniel1:5 The king appointed for them a daily ration from the king's choice food and from the wine which he drank
Daniel1:6 Now among them from the sons of Judah were Daniel
Daniel1:7 Then the commander of the officials assigned new names to them; and to Daniel he assigned the name Belteshazzar
Daniel1:8 But Daniel made up his mind that he would not defile himself with the king's choice food or with the wine which he drank; so he sought permission from the commander of the officials that he might not defile himself.
Daniel1:9 Now God granted Daniel favor and compassion in the sight of the commander of the officials
Daniel1:10 and the commander of the officials said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 But Daniel said to the overseer whom the commander of the officials had appointed over Daniel
Daniel1:12 Please test your servants for ten days
Daniel1:13 Then let our appearance be observed in your presence and the appearance of the youths who are eating the king's choice food; and deal with your servants according to what you see.
Daniel1:14 So he listened to them in this matter and tested them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 At the end of ten days their appearance seemed better and they were fatter than all the youths who had been eating the king's choice food.
Daniel1:16 So the overseer continued to withhold their choice food and the wine they were to drink
Daniel1:17 As for these four youths
Daniel1:18 Then at the end of the days which the king had specified for presenting them
Daniel1:19 The king talked with them
Daniel1:20 As for every matter of wisdom and understanding about which the king consulted them
Daniel1:21 And Daniel continued until the first year of Cyrus the king.

Daniel5:14 Now I have heard about you that a spirit of the gods is in you
Daniel5:15 Just now the wise men and the conjurers were brought in before me that they might read this inscription and make its interpretation known to me
Daniel5:16 But I personally have heard about you
Daniel5:17 Then Daniel answered and said before the king
Daniel5:18 O king
Daniel5:19 Because of the grandeur which He bestowed on him
Daniel5:20 But when his heart was lifted up and his spirit became so proud that he behaved arrogantly
Daniel5:21 He was also driven away from mankind
Daniel5:22 Yet you
Daniel5:23 but you have exalted yourself against the Lord of heaven; and they have brought the vessels of His house before you
Daniel5:24 Then the hand was sent from Him and this inscription was written out.
Daniel5:25 Now this is the inscription that was written out: 'MENE
Daniel5:26 This is the interpretation of the message: 'MENE'--God has numbered your kingdom and put an end to it.
Daniel5:27 'TEKEL'--you have been weighed on the scales and found deficient.
Daniel5:28 'PERES'--your kingdom has been divided and given over to the Medes and Persians.
Daniel5:29 Then Belshazzar gave orders
Daniel5:30 That same night Belshazzar the Chaldean king was slain.
Daniel5:31 So Darius the Mede received the kingdom at about the age of sixty-two.
Daniel6:1 It seemed good to Darius to appoint 120 satraps over the kingdom
Daniel6:2 and over them three commissioners (of whom Daniel was one)
Daniel6:3 Then this Daniel began distinguishing himself among the commissioners and satraps because he possessed an extraordinary spirit
Daniel6:4 Then the commissioners and satraps began trying to find a ground of accusation against Daniel in regard to government affairs; but they could find no ground of accusation or evidence of corruption
Daniel6:5 Then these men said
Daniel6:6 Then these commissioners and satraps came by agreement to the king and spoke to him as follows: King Darius
Daniel6:7 All the commissioners of the kingdom
Daniel6:8 Now
Daniel6:9 Therefore King Darius signed the document
Daniel6:10 Now when Daniel knew that the document was signed
Daniel6:11 Then these men came by agreement and found Daniel making petition and supplication before his God.
Daniel6:12 Then they approached and spoke before the king about the king's injunction
Daniel6:13 Then they answered and spoke before the king
Daniel6:14 Then
Daniel6:15 Then these men came by agreement to the king and said to the king
Daniel6:16 Then the king gave orders
Daniel6:17 A stone was brought and laid over the mouth of the den; and the king sealed it with his own signet ring and with the signet rings of his nobles
Daniel6:18 Then the king went off to his palace and spent the night fasting
Daniel6:19 Then the king arose at dawn
Daniel6:20 When he had come near the den to Daniel
Daniel6:21 Then Daniel spoke to the king
Daniel6:22 My God sent His angel and shut the lions' mouths and they have not harmed me
Daniel6:23 Then the king was very pleased and gave orders for Daniel to be taken up out of the den. So Daniel was taken up out of the den and no injury whatever was found on him
Daniel6:24 The king then gave orders
Daniel6:25 Then Darius the king wrote to all the peoples
Daniel6:26 I make a decree that in all the dominion of my kingdom men are to fear and tremble before the God of Daniel; For He is the living God and enduring forever
Daniel6:27 He delivers and rescues and performs signs and wonders In heaven and on earth
Daniel6:28 So this Daniel enjoyed success in the reign of Darius and in the reign of Cyrus the Persian.

Daniel7:2 Daniel said
Daniel7:3 And four great beasts were coming up from the sea
Daniel7:4 The first was like a lion and had the wings of an eagle. I kept looking until its wings were plucked
Daniel7:5 And behold
Daniel7:6 After this I kept looking
Daniel7:7 After this I kept looking in the night visions
Daniel7:8 While I was contemplating the horns
Daniel7:9 I kept looking Until thrones were set up
Daniel7:10 A river of fire was flowing And coming out from before Him; Thousands upon thousands were attending Him
Daniel7:11 Then I kept looking because of the sound of the boastful words which the horn was speaking; I kept looking until the beast was slain
Daniel7:12 As for the rest of the beasts
Daniel7:13 I kept looking in the night visions
Daniel7:14 And to Him was given dominion
Daniel7:15 As for me
Daniel7:16 I approached one of those who were standing by and began asking him the exact meaning of all this. So he told me and made known to me the interpretation of these things:
Daniel7:17 'These great beasts
Daniel7:18 'But the saints of the Highest One will receive the kingdom and possess the kingdom forever
Daniel7:19 Then I desired to know the exact meaning of the fourth beast
Daniel7:20 and the meaning of the ten horns that were on its head and the other horn which came up
Daniel7:21 I kept looking
Daniel7:22 until the Ancient of Days came and judgment was passed in favor of the saints of the Highest One

Daniel7:24 'As for the ten horns
Daniel7:25 'He will speak out against the Most High and wear down the saints of the Highest One
Daniel7:26 'But the court will sit for judgment
Daniel7:27 'Then the sovereignty

Daniel8:1 In the third year of the reign of Belshazzar the king a vision appeared to me
Daniel8:2 I looked in the vision
Daniel8:3 Then I lifted my eyes and looked
Daniel8:4 I saw the ram butting westward
Daniel8:5 While I was observing
Daniel8:6 He came up to the ram that had the two horns
Daniel8:7 I saw him come beside the ram
Daniel8:8 Then the male goat magnified himself exceedingly. But as soon as he was mighty
Daniel8:9 Out of one of them came forth a rather small horn which grew exceedingly great toward the south
Daniel8:10 It grew up to the host of heaven and caused some of the host and some of the stars to fall to the earth
Daniel8:11 It even magnified itself to be equal with the Commander of the host; and it removed the regular sacrifice from Him
Daniel8:12 And on account of transgression the host will be given over to the horn along with the regular sacrifice; and it will fling truth to the ground and perform its will and prosper.

Daniel8:14 He said to me

Daniel8:23 In the latter period of their rule
Daniel8:24 His power will be mighty
Daniel8:25 And through his shrewdness He will cause deceit to succeed by his influence; And he will magnify himself in his heart
Daniel8:26 The vision of the evenings and mornings Which has been told is true; But keep the vision secret

Daniel11:1 In the first year of Darius the Mede
Daniel11:2 And now I will tell you the truth. Behold
Daniel11:3 And a mighty king will arise
Daniel11:4 But as soon as he has arisen
Daniel11:5 Then the king of the South will grow strong
Daniel11:6 After some years they will form an alliance
Daniel11:7 But one of the descendants of her line will arise in his place
Daniel11:8 Also their gods with their metal images and their precious vessels of silver and gold he will take into captivity to Egypt
Daniel11:9 Then the latter will enter the realm of the king of the South
Daniel11:10 His sons will mobilize and assemble a multitude of great forces; and one of them will keep on coming and overflow and pass through
Daniel11:11 The king of the South will be enraged and go forth and fight with the king of the North. Then the latter will raise a great multitude
Daniel11:12 When the multitude is carried away
Daniel11:13 For the king of the North will again raise a greater multitude than the former
Daniel11:14 Now in those times many will rise up against the king of the South; the violent ones among your people will also lift themselves up in order to fulfill the vision
Daniel11:15 Then the king of the North will come
Daniel11:16 But he who comes against him will do as he pleases
Daniel11:17 He will set his face to come with the power of his whole kingdom
Daniel11:18 Then he will turn his face to the coastlands and capture many. But a commander will put a stop to his scorn against him; moreover
Daniel11:19 So he will turn his face toward the fortresses of his own land
Daniel11:20 Then in his place one will arise who will send an oppressor through the Jewel of his kingdom; yet within a few days he will be shattered
Daniel11:21 In his place a despicable person will arise
Daniel11:22 The overflowing forces will be flooded away before him and shattered
Daniel11:23 After an alliance is made with him he will practice deception
Daniel11:24 In a time of tranquility he will enter the richest parts of the realm
Daniel11:25 He will stir up his strength and courage against the king of the South with a large army; so the king of the South will mobilize an extremely large and mighty army for war; but he will not stand
Daniel11:26 Those who eat his choice food will destroy him
Daniel11:27 As for both kings
Daniel11:28 Then he will return to his land with much plunder; but his heart will be set against the holy covenant
Daniel11:29 At the appointed time he will return and come into the South
Daniel11:30 For ships of Kittim will come against him; therefore he will be disheartened and will return and become enraged at the holy covenant and take action; so he will come back and show regard for those who forsake the holy covenant.
Daniel11:31 Forces from him will arise
Daniel11:32 By smooth words he will turn to godlessness those who act wickedly toward the covenant
Daniel11:33 Those who have insight among the people will give understanding to the many; yet they will fall by sword and by flame
Daniel11:34 Now when they fall they will be granted a little help
Daniel11:35 Some of those who have insight will fall
Daniel11:36 Then the king will do as he pleases
Daniel11:37 He will show no regard for the gods of his fathers or for the desire of women
Daniel11:38 But instead he will honor a god of fortresses
Daniel11:39 He will take action against the strongest of fortresses with the help of a foreign god; he will give great honor to those who acknowledge him and will cause them to rule over the many
Daniel11:40 At the end time the king of the South will collide with him
Daniel11:41 He will also enter the Beautiful Land
Daniel11:42 Then he will stretch out his hand against other countries
Daniel11:43 But he will gain control over the hidden treasures of gold and silver and over all the precious things of Egypt; and Libyans and Ethiopians will follow at his heels.
Daniel11:44 But rumors from the East and from the North will disturb him
Daniel11:45 He will pitch the tents of his royal pavilion between the seas and the beautiful Holy Mountain; yet he will come to his end

Joel1:18 How the beasts groan! The herds of cattle wander aimlessly Because there is no pasture for them; Even the flocks of sheep suffer.
Joel1:19 To You
Joel1:20 Even the beasts of the field pant for You; For the water brooks are dried up And fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness.
Joel2:1 Blow a trumpet in Zion
Joel2:2 A day of darkness and gloom
Joel2:3 A fire consumes before them And behind them a flame burns. The land is like the garden of Eden before them But a desolate wilderness behind them
Joel2:4 Their appearance is like the appearance of horses; And like war horses
Joel2:5 With a noise as of chariots They leap on the tops of the mountains
Joel2:6 Before them the people are in anguish; All faces turn pale.
Joel2:7 They run like mighty men
Joel2:8 They do not crowd each other
Joel2:9 They rush on the city
Joel2:10 Before them the earth quakes
Joel2:11 The LORD utters His voice before His army; Surely His camp is very great
Joel2:12 Yet even now
Joel2:13 And rend your heart and not your garments. Now return to the LORD your God
Joel2:14 Who knows whether He will not turn and relent And leave a blessing behind Him
Joel2:15 Blow a trumpet in Zion
Joel2:16 Gather the people
Joel2:17 Let the priests
Joel2:18 Then the LORD will be zealous for His land And will have pity on His people.
Joel2:19 The LORD will answer and say to His people
Joel2:20 But I will remove the northern army far from you
Joel2:21 Do not fear
Joel2:22 Do not fear
Joel2:23 So rejoice
Joel2:24 The threshing floors will be full of grain
Joel2:25 Then I will make up to you for the years That the swarming locust has eaten
Joel2:26 You will have plenty to eat and be satisfied And praise the name of the LORD your God
Joel2:27 Thus you will know that I am in the midst of Israel
Joel2:28 It will come about after this That I will pour out My Spirit on all mankind; And your sons and daughters will prophesy
Joel2:29 Even on the male and female servants I will pour out My Spirit in those days.
Joel2:30 I will display wonders in the sky and on the earth
Joel2:31 The sun will be turned into darkness And the moon into blood Before the great and awesome day of the LORD comes.
Joel2:32 And it will come about that whoever calls on the name of the LORD Will be delivered; For on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem There will be those who escape
Joel3:1 For behold
Joel3:2 I will gather all the nations And bring them down to the valley of Jehoshaphat. Then I will enter into judgment with them there On behalf of My people and My inheritance
Joel3:3 They have also cast lots for My people
Joel3:4 Moreover
Joel3:5 Since you have taken My silver and My gold
Joel3:6 and sold the sons of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks in order to remove them far from their territory
Joel3:7 behold
Joel3:8 Also I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the sons of Judah
Joel3:9 Proclaim this among the nations: Prepare a war; rouse the mighty men! Let all the soldiers draw near
Joel3:10 Beat your plowshares into swords And your pruning hooks into spears; Let the weak say
Joel3:11 Hasten and come
Joel3:12 Let the nations be aroused And come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat
Joel3:13 Put in the sickle
Joel3:14 Multitudes
Joel3:15 The sun and moon grow dark And the stars lose their brightness.
Joel3:16 The LORD roars from Zion And utters His voice from Jerusalem
Joel3:17 Then you will know that I am the LORD your God
Joel3:18 And in that day The mountains will drip with sweet wine
Joel3:19 Egypt will become a waste
Joel3:20 But Judah will be inhabited forever And Jerusalem for all generations.
Joel3:21 And I will avenge their blood which I have not avenged

Amos1:2 He said
Amos1:3 Thus says the LORD

Amos1:6 Thus says the LORD

Amos1:8 I will also cut off the inhabitant from Ashdod
Amos1:9 Thus says the LORD
Amos1:10 So I will send fire upon the wall of Tyre And it will consume her citadels.
Amos1:11 Thus says the LORD
Amos1:12 So I will send fire upon Teman And it will consume the citadels of Bozrah.
Amos1:13 Thus says the LORD
Amos1:14 So I will kindle a fire on the wall of Rabbah And it will consume her citadels Amid war cries on the day of battle

Amos2:1 Thus says the LORD
Amos2:2 So I will send fire upon Moab And it will consume the citadels of Kerioth; And Moab will die amid tumult
Amos2:3 I will also cut off the judge from her midst And slay all her princes with him
Amos2:4 Thus says the LORD
Amos2:5 So I will send fire upon Judah And it will consume the citadels of Jerusalem.
Amos2:6 Thus says the LORD
Amos2:7 These who pant after the very dust of the earth on the head of the helpless Also turn aside the way of the humble; And a man and his father resort to the same girl In order to profane My holy name.
Amos2:8 On garments taken as pledges they stretch out beside every altar
Amos2:9 Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorite before them
Amos2:10 It was I who brought you up from the land of Egypt
Amos2:11 Then I raised up some of your sons to be prophets And some of your young men to be Nazirites. Is this not so
Amos2:12 But you made the Nazirites drink wine
Amos2:13 Behold
Amos2:14 Flight will perish from the swift
Amos2:15 He who grasps the bow will not stand his ground
Amos2:16 Even the bravest among the warriors will flee naked in that day
Amos3:1 Hear this word which the LORD has spoken against you
Amos3:2 You only have I chosen among all the families of the earth; Therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.
Amos3:3 Do two men walk together unless they have made an appointment?
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the forest when he has no prey? Does a young lion growl from his den unless he has captured something?
Amos3:5 Does a bird fall into a trap on the ground when there is no bait in it? Does a trap spring up from the earth when it captures nothing at all?
Amos3:6 If a trumpet is blown in a city will not the people tremble? If a calamity occurs in a city has not the LORD done it?
Amos3:7 Surely the Lord GOD does nothing Unless He reveals His secret counsel To His servants the prophets.
Amos3:8 A lion has roared! Who will not fear? The Lord GOD has spoken! Who can but prophesy?
Amos3:9 Proclaim on the citadels in Ashdod and on the citadels in the land of Egypt and say
Amos3:10 But they do not know how to do what is right
Amos3:11 Therefore
Amos3:12 Thus says the LORD
Amos3:13 Hear and testify against the house of Jacob
Amos3:14 For on the day that I punish Israel's transgressions
Amos3:15 I will also smite the winter house together with the summer house; The houses of ivory will also perish And the great houses will come to an end
Amos4:1 Hear this word
Amos4:2 The Lord GOD has sworn by His holiness
Amos4:3 You will go out through breaches in the walls
Amos4:4 Enter Bethel and transgress; In Gilgal multiply transgression! Bring your sacrifices every morning
Amos4:5 Offer a thank offering also from that which is leavened
Amos4:6 But I gave you also cleanness of teeth in all your cities And lack of bread in all your places
Amos4:7 Furthermore
Amos4:8 So two or three cities would stagger to another city to drink water
Amos4:9 I smote you with scorching wind and mildew; And the caterpillar was devouring Your many gardens and vineyards
Amos4:10 I sent a plague among you after the manner of Egypt; I slew your young men by the sword along with your captured horses
Amos4:11 I overthrew you
Amos4:12 Therefore thus I will do to you
Amos4:13 For behold

Amos5:2 She has fallen
Amos5:3 For thus says the Lord GOD
Amos5:4 For thus says the LORD to the house of Israel
Amos5:5 But do not resort to Bethel And do not come to Gilgal
Amos5:6 Seek the LORD that you may live
Amos5:7 For those who turn justice into wormwood And cast righteousness down to the earth.
Amos5:8 He who made the Pleiades and Orion And changes deep darkness into morning
Amos5:9 It is He who flashes forth with destruction upon the strong
Amos5:10 They hate him who reproves in the gate
Amos5:11 Therefore because you impose heavy rent on the poor And exact a tribute of grain from them
Amos5:12 For I know your transgressions are many and your sins are great
Amos5:13 Therefore at such a time the prudent person keeps silent
Amos5:14 Seek good and not evil
Amos5:15 Hate evil
Amos5:16 Therefore thus says the LORD God of hosts
Amos5:17 And in all the vineyards there is wailing
Amos5:18 Alas
Amos5:19 As when a man flees from a lion And a bear meets him
Amos5:20 Will not the day of the LORD be darkness instead of light
Amos5:21 I hate
Amos5:22 Even though you offer up to Me burnt offerings and your grain offerings
Amos5:23 Take away from Me the noise of your songs; I will not even listen to the sound of your harps.
Amos5:24 But let justice roll down like waters And righteousness like an ever-flowing stream.
Amos5:25 Did you present Me with sacrifices and grain offerings in the wilderness for forty years
Amos5:26 You also carried along Sikkuth your king and Kiyyun
Amos5:27 Therefore

Obadiah1:13 Do not enter the gate of My people In the day of their disaster. Yes
Obadiah1:14 Do not stand at the fork of the road To cut down their fugitives; And do not imprison their survivors In the day of their distress.

Obadiah1:16 Because just as you drank on My holy mountain

Jonah1:2 Arise

Jonah1:4 The LORD hurled a great wind on the sea and there was a great storm on the sea so that the ship was about to break up.
Jonah1:5 Then the sailors became afraid and every man cried to his god
Jonah1:6 So the captain approached him and said
Jonah1:7 Each man said to his mate
Jonah1:8 Then they said to him
Jonah1:9 He said to them
Jonah1:10 Then the men became extremely frightened and they said to him
Jonah1:11 So they said to him
Jonah1:12 He said to them
Jonah1:13 However

Jonah1:15 So they picked up Jonah

Jonah2:2 and he said
Jonah2:3 For You had cast me into the deep
Jonah2:4 So I said

Jonah2:6 I descended to the roots of the mountains. The earth with its bars was around me forever
Jonah2:7 While I was fainting away
Jonah2:8 Those who regard vain idols Forsake their faithfulness
Jonah2:9 But I will sacrifice to You With the voice of thanksgiving. That which I have vowed I will pay. Salvation is from the LORD.

Jonah3:4 Then Jonah began to go through the city one day's walk; and he cried out and said
Jonah3:5 Then the people of Nineveh believed in God; and they called a fast and put on sackcloth from the greatest to the least of them.

Jonah3:7 He issued a proclamation and it said
Jonah3:8 But both man and beast must be covered with sackcloth; and let men call on God earnestly that each may turn from his wicked way and from the violence which is in his hands.

Jonah4:6 So the LORD God appointed a plant and it grew up over Jonah to be a shade over his head to deliver him from his discomfort. And Jonah was extremely happy about the plant.
Jonah4:7 But God appointed a worm when dawn came the next day and it attacked the plant and it withered.

Nahum1:1 The oracle of Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
Nahum1:2 A jealous and avenging God is the LORD; The LORD is avenging and wrathful. The LORD takes vengeance on His adversaries
Nahum1:3 The LORD is slow to anger and great in power
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and makes it dry; He dries up all the rivers. Bashan and Carmel wither; The blossoms of Lebanon wither.
Nahum1:5 Mountains quake because of Him And the hills dissolve; Indeed the earth is upheaved by His presence
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before His indignation? Who can endure the burning of His anger? His wrath is poured out like fire And the rocks are broken up by Him.
Nahum1:7 The LORD is good
Nahum1:8 But with an overflowing flood He will make a complete end of its site
Nahum1:9 Whatever you devise against the LORD
Nahum1:10 Like tangled thorns
Nahum1:11 From you has gone forth One who plotted evil against the LORD
Nahum1:12 Thus says the LORD
Nahum1:13 So now
Nahum1:14 The LORD has issued a command concerning you: Your name will no longer be perpetuated. I will cut off idol and image From the house of your gods. I will prepare your grave
Nahum1:15 Behold

Nahum2:2 For the LORD will restore the splendor of Jacob Like the splendor of Israel

Nahum2:4 The chariots race madly in the streets
Nahum2:5 He remembers his nobles; They stumble in their march
Nahum2:6 The gates of the rivers are opened And the palace is dissolved.

Nahum2:8 Though Nineveh was like a pool of water throughout her days
Nahum2:9 Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold! For there is no limit to the treasure--Wealth from every kind of desirable object.

Nahum2:12 The lion tore enough for his cubs

Nahum3:3 Horsemen charging

Nahum3:6 I will throw filth on you And make you vile

Nahum3:8 Are you better than No-amon
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia was her might
Nahum3:10 Yet she became an exile

Nahum3:12 All your fortifications are fig trees with ripe fruit--When shaken
Nahum3:13 Behold

Nahum3:15 There fire will consume you
Nahum3:16 You have increased your traders more than the stars of heaven--The creeping locust strips and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your guardsmen are like the swarming locust. Your marshals are like hordes of grasshoppers Settling in the stone walls on a cold day. The sun rises and they flee
Nahum3:18 Your shepherds are sleeping
Nahum3:19 There is no relief for your breakdown
Habakkuk1:1 The oracle which Habakkuk the prophet saw.
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Why do You make me see iniquity
Habakkuk1:4 Therefore the law is ignored And justice is never upheld. For the wicked surround the righteous; Therefore justice comes out perverted.
Habakkuk1:5 Look among the nations! Observe! Be astonished! Wonder! Because I am doing something in your days--You would not believe if you were told.
Habakkuk1:6 For behold
Habakkuk1:7 They are dreaded and feared; Their justice and authority originate with themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards And keener than wolves in the evening. Their horsemen come galloping
Habakkuk1:9 All of them come for violence. Their horde of faces moves forward. They collect captives like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They mock at kings And rulers are a laughing matter to them. They laugh at every fortress And heap up rubble to capture it.
Habakkuk1:11 Then they will sweep through like the wind and pass on. But they will be held guilty
Habakkuk1:12 Are You not from everlasting
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are too pure to approve evil
Habakkuk1:14 Why have You made men like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 The Chaldeans bring all of them up with a hook
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore they offer a sacrifice to their net And burn incense to their fishing net; Because through these things their catch is large
Habakkuk1:17 Will they therefore empty their net And continually slay nations without sparing?
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand on my guard post And station myself on the rampart; And I will keep watch to see what He will speak to me
Habakkuk2:2 Then the LORD answered me and said
Habakkuk2:3 For the vision is yet for the appointed time; It hastens toward the goal and it will not fail. Though it tarries
Habakkuk2:4 Behold
Habakkuk2:5 Furthermore
Habakkuk2:6 Will not all of these take up a taunt-song against him
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your creditors rise up suddenly
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have looted many nations
Habakkuk2:9 Woe to him who gets evil gain for his house To put his nest on high
Habakkuk2:10 You have devised a shameful thing for your house By cutting off many peoples; So you are sinning against yourself.
Habakkuk2:11 Surely the stone will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 Woe to him who builds a city with bloodshed And founds a town with violence!
Habakkuk2:13 Is it not indeed from the LORD of hosts That peoples toil for fire
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled With the knowledge of the glory of the LORD
Habakkuk2:15 Woe to you who make your neighbors drink
Habakkuk2:16 You will be filled with disgrace rather than honor. Now you yourself drink and expose your own nakedness. The cup in the LORD'S right hand will come around to you
Habakkuk2:17 For the violence done to Lebanon will overwhelm you
Habakkuk2:18 What profit is the idol when its maker has carved it
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to a piece of wood
Habakkuk2:20 But the LORD is in His holy temple. Let all the earth be silent before Him.
Habakkuk3:1 A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet
Habakkuk3:2 LORD
Habakkuk3:3 God comes from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 His radiance is like the sunlight; He has rays flashing from His hand
Habakkuk3:5 Before Him goes pestilence
Habakkuk3:6 He stood and surveyed the earth; He looked and startled the nations. Yes
Habakkuk3:7 I saw the tents of Cushan under distress
Habakkuk3:8 Did the LORD rage against the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 Your bow was made bare
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw You and quaked; The downpour of waters swept by. The deep uttered forth its voice
Habakkuk3:11 Sun and moon stood in their places; They went away at the light of Your arrows
Habakkuk3:12 In indignation You marched through the earth; In anger You trampled the nations.
Habakkuk3:13 You went forth for the salvation of Your people
Habakkuk3:14 You pierced with his own spears The head of his throngs. They stormed in to scatter us; Their exultation was like those Who devour the oppressed in secret.
Habakkuk3:15 You trampled on the sea with Your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I heard and my inward parts trembled
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree should not blossom And there be no fruit on the vines
Habakkuk3:18 Yet I will exult in the LORD
Habakkuk3:19 The Lord GOD is my strength

Zechariah14:19 This will be the punishment of Egypt
Zechariah14:20 In that day there will be inscribed on the bells of the horses
Zechariah14:21 Every cooking pot in Jerusalem and in Judah will be holy to the LORD of hosts; and all who sacrifice will come and take of them and boil in them. And there will no longer be a Canaanite in the house of the LORD of hosts in that day.
Malachi1:1 The oracle of the word of the LORD to Israel through Malachi.
Malachi1:2 I have loved you
Malachi1:3 but I have hated Esau
Malachi1:4 Though Edom says
Malachi1:5 Your eyes will see this and you will say
Malachi1:6 'A son honors his father
Malachi1:7 You are presenting defiled food upon My altar. But you say
Malachi1:8 But when you present the blind for sacrifice
Malachi1:9 But now will you not entreat God's favor
Malachi1:10 Oh that there were one among you who would shut the gates
Malachi1:11 For from the rising of the sun even to its setting
Malachi1:12 But you are profaning it
Malachi1:13 You also say
Malachi1:14 But cursed be the swindler who has a male in his flock and vows it
Malachi2:1 And now this commandment is for you
Malachi2:2 If you do not listen
Malachi2:3 Behold
Malachi2:4 Then you will know that I have sent this commandment to you
Malachi2:5 My covenant with him was one of life and peace
Malachi2:6 True instruction was in his mouth and unrighteousness was not found on his lips; he walked with Me in peace and uprightness
Malachi2:7 For the lips of a priest should preserve knowledge
Malachi2:8 But as for you
Malachi2:9 So I also have made you despised and abased before all the people
Malachi2:10 Do we not all have one father? Has not one God created us? Why do we deal treacherously each against his brother so as to profane the covenant of our fathers?
Malachi2:11 Judah has dealt treacherously
Malachi2:12 As for the man who does this
Malachi2:13 This is another thing you do: you cover the altar of the LORD with tears
Malachi2:14 Yet you say
Malachi2:15 But not one has done so who has a remnant of the Spirit. And what did that one do while he was seeking a godly offspring? Take heed then to your spirit
Malachi2:16 For I hate divorce
Malachi2:17 You have wearied the LORD with your words. Yet you say

Malachi3:2 But who can endure the day of His coming? And who can stand when He appears? For He is like a refiner's fire and like fullers' soap.
Malachi3:3 He will sit as a smelter and purifier of silver
Malachi3:4 Then the offering of Judah and Jerusalem will be pleasing to the LORD as in the days of old and as in former years.
Malachi3:5 Then I will draw near to you for judgment; and I will be a swift witness against the sorcerers and against the adulterers and against those who swear falsely
Malachi3:6 For I
Malachi3:7 From the days of your fathers you have turned aside from My statutes and have not kept them. Return to Me

Malachi3:9 You are cursed with a curse
Malachi3:10 Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse
Malachi3:11 Then I will rebuke the devourer for you
Malachi3:12 All the nations will call you blessed
Malachi3:13 Your words have been arrogant against Me
Malachi3:14 You have said
Malachi3:15 'So now we call the arrogant blessed; not only are the doers of wickedness built up but they also test God and escape.'

Malachi3:17 They will be Mine

Matthew1:1 The record of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac
Matthew1:3 Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar
Matthew1:4 Ram was the father of Amminadab
Matthew1:5 Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab
Matthew1:6 Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon by Bathsheba who had been the wife of Uriah.
Matthew1:7 Solomon was the father of Rehoboam
Matthew1:8 Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat
Matthew1:9 Uzziah was the father of Jotham
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh
Matthew1:11 Josiah became the father of Jeconiah and his brothers
Matthew1:12 After the deportation to Babylon: Jeconiah became the father of Shealtiel
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel was the father of Abihud
Matthew1:14 Azor was the father of Zadok
Matthew1:15 Eliud was the father of Eleazar
Matthew1:16 Jacob was the father of Joseph the husband of Mary
Matthew1:17 So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; from David to the deportation to Babylon
Matthew1:18 Now the birth of Jesus Christ was as follows: when His mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph
Matthew1:19 And Joseph her husband
Matthew1:20 But when he had considered this
Matthew1:21 She will bear a Son; and you shall call His name Jesus
Matthew1:22 Now all this took place to fulfill what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
Matthew1:23 BEHOLD
Matthew1:24 And Joseph awoke from his sleep and did as the angel of the Lord commanded him
Matthew1:25 but kept her a virgin until she gave birth to a Son; and he called His name Jesus.

Matthew2:11 After coming into the house they saw the Child with Mary His mother; and they fell to the ground and worshiped Him. Then
Matthew2:12 And having been warned by God in a dream not to return to Herod
Matthew2:13 Now when they had gone
Matthew2:14 So Joseph got up and took the Child and His mother while it was still night
Matthew2:15 He remained there until the death of Herod. This was to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet: OUT OF EGYPT I CALLED MY SON.
Matthew2:16 Then when Herod saw that he had been tricked by the magi

Matthew2:20 Get up

Matthew2:22 But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod
Matthew2:23 and came and lived in a city called Nazareth. This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophets: He shall be called a Nazarene.

Matthew10:30 But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.
Matthew10:31 So do not fear; you are more valuable than many sparrows.

Matthew10:33 But whoever denies Me before men

Matthew10:38 And he who does not take his cross and follow after Me is not worthy of Me.
Matthew10:39 He who has found his life will lose it
Matthew10:40 He who receives you receives Me
Matthew10:41 He who receives a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet's reward; and he who receives a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man's reward.
Matthew10:42 And whoever in the name of a disciple gives to one of these little ones even a cup of cold water to drink

Matthew24:42 Therefore be on the alert
Matthew24:43 But be sure of this
Matthew24:44 For this reason you also must be ready; for the Son of Man is coming at an hour when you do not think He will.

Matthew24:46 Blessed is that slave whom his master finds so doing when he comes.

Matthew24:48 But if that evil slave says in his heart
Matthew24:49 and begins to beat his fellow slaves and eat and drink with drunkards;
Matthew24:50 the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour which he does not know
Matthew24:51 and will cut him in pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites; in that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

Matthew27:30 They spat on Him

Matthew27:39 And those passing by were hurling abuse at Him
Matthew27:40 and saying

Matthew27:42 He saved others; He cannot save Himself. He is the King of Israel; let Him now come down from the cross
Matthew27:43 HE TRUSTS IN GOD; LET GOD RESCUE Him now
Matthew27:44 The robbers who had been crucified with Him were also insulting Him with the same words.
Matthew27:45 Now from the sixth hour darkness fell upon all the land until the ninth hour.
Matthew27:46 About the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice
Matthew27:47 And some of those who were standing there
Matthew27:48 Immediately one of them ran
Matthew27:49 But the rest of them said
Matthew27:50 And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice
Matthew27:51 And behold

Matthew27:54 Now the centurion
Matthew27:55 Many women were there looking on from a distance
Matthew27:56 Among them was Mary Magdalene
Matthew27:57 When it was evening

Matthew27:60 and laid it in his own new tomb
Matthew27:61 And Mary Magdalene was there
Matthew27:62 Now on the next day
Matthew27:63 and said

Matthew27:65 Pilate said to them
Matthew27:66 And they went and made the grave secure

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: BEHOLD
Mark1:3 THE VOICE OF ONE CRYING IN THE WILDERNESS
Mark1:4 John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
Mark1:5 And all the country of Judea was going out to him
Mark1:6 John was clothed with camel's hair and wore a leather belt around his waist
Mark1:7 And he was preaching
Mark1:8 I baptized you with water; but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
Mark1:9 In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
Mark1:10 Immediately coming up out of the water
Mark1:11 and a voice came out of the heavens: You are My beloved Son
Mark1:12 Immediately the Spirit impelled Him to go out into the wilderness.
Mark1:13 And He was in the wilderness forty days being tempted by Satan; and He was with the wild beasts
Mark1:14 Now after John had been taken into custody
Mark1:15 and saying
Mark1:16 As He was going along by the Sea of Galilee
Mark1:17 And Jesus said to them
Mark1:18 Immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
Mark1:19 Going on a little farther
Mark1:20 Immediately He called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants
Mark1:21 They went into Capernaum; and immediately on the Sabbath He entered the synagogue and began to teach.
Mark1:22 They were amazed at His teaching; for He was teaching them as one having authority
Mark1:23 Just then there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit; and he cried out
Mark1:24 saying
Mark1:25 And Jesus rebuked him
Mark1:26 Throwing him into convulsions
Mark1:27 They were all amazed
Mark1:28 Immediately the news about Him spread everywhere into all the surrounding district of Galilee.
Mark1:29 And immediately after they came out of the synagogue
Mark1:30 Now Simon's mother-in-law was lying sick with a fever; and immediately they spoke to Jesus about her.
Mark1:31 And He came to her and raised her up
Mark1:32 When evening came
Mark1:33 And the whole city had gathered at the door.
Mark1:34 And He healed many who were ill with various diseases
Mark1:35 In the early morning
Mark1:36 Simon and his companions searched for Him;
Mark1:37 they found Him
Mark1:38 He said to them
Mark1:39 And He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee
Mark1:40 And a leper came to Jesus
Mark1:41 Moved with compassion
Mark1:42 Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cleansed.
Mark1:43 And He sternly warned him and immediately sent him away
Mark1:44 and He said to him
Mark1:45 But he went out and began to proclaim it freely and to spread the news around
Mark2:1 When He had come back to Capernaum several days afterward
Mark2:2 And many were gathered together
Mark2:3 And they came
Mark2:4 Being unable to get to Him because of the crowd
Mark2:5 And Jesus seeing their faith said to the paralytic
Mark2:6 But some of the scribes were sitting there and reasoning in their hearts
Mark2:7 Why does this man speak that way? He is blaspheming; who can forgive sins but God alone?

Mark2:9 Which is easier
Mark2:10 But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins--He said to the paralytic
Mark2:11 I say to you
Mark2:12 And he got up and immediately picked up the pallet and went out in the sight of everyone
Mark2:13 And He went out again by the seashore; and all the people were coming to Him
Mark2:14 As He passed by
Mark2:15 And it happened that He was reclining at the table in his house
Mark2:16 When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that He was eating with the sinners and tax collectors
Mark2:17 And hearing this
Mark2:18 John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting; and they came and said to Him
Mark2:19 And Jesus said to them
Mark2:20 But the days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them
Mark2:21 No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; otherwise the patch pulls away from it
Mark2:22 No one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the wine will burst the skins
Mark2:23 And it happened that He was passing through the grainfields on the Sabbath
Mark2:24 The Pharisees were saying to Him
Mark2:25 And He said to them
Mark2:26 how he entered the house of God in the time of Abiathar the high priest
Mark2:27 Jesus said to them
Mark2:28 So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.

Mark4:1 He began to teach again by the sea. And such a very large crowd gathered to Him that He got into a boat in the sea and sat down; and the whole crowd was by the sea on the land.

Mark4:3 Listen to this! Behold
Mark4:4 as he was sowing
Mark4:5 Other seed fell on the rocky ground where it did not have much soil; and immediately it sprang up because it had no depth of soil.
Mark4:6 And after the sun had risen
Mark4:7 Other seed fell among the thorns
Mark4:8 Other seeds fell into the good soil

Mark4:10 As soon as He was alone
Mark4:11 And He was saying to them

John5:35 He was the lamp that was burning and was shining and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
John5:36 But the testimony which I have is greater than the testimony of John; for the works which the Father has given Me to accomplish--the very works that I do--testify about Me
John5:37 And the Father who sent Me
John5:38 You do not have His word abiding in you
John5:39 You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; it is these that testify about Me;
John5:40 and you are unwilling to come to Me so that you may have life.

Acts27:1 When it was decided that we would sail for Italy
Acts27:2 And embarking in an Adramyttian ship
Acts27:3 The next day we put in at Sidon; and Julius treated Paul with consideration and allowed him to go to his friends and receive care.
Acts27:4 From there we put out to sea and sailed under the shelter of Cyprus because the winds were contrary.
Acts27:5 When we had sailed through the sea along the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia
Acts27:6 There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship sailing for Italy
Acts27:7 When we had sailed slowly for a good many days
Acts27:8 and with difficulty sailing past it we came to a place called Fair Havens
Acts27:9 When considerable time had passed and the voyage was now dangerous
Acts27:10 and said to them
Acts27:11 But the centurion was more persuaded by the pilot and the captain of the ship than by what was being said by Paul.
Acts27:12 Because the harbor was not suitable for wintering
Acts27:13 When a moderate south wind came up
Acts27:14 But before very long there rushed down from the land a violent wind
Acts27:15 and when the ship was caught in it and could not face the wind
Acts27:16 Running under the shelter of a small island called Clauda
Acts27:17 After they had hoisted it up
Acts27:18 The next day as we were being violently storm-tossed
Acts27:19 and on the third day they threw the ship's tackle overboard with their own hands.
Acts27:20 Since neither sun nor stars appeared for many days
Acts27:21 When they had gone a long time without food
Acts27:22 Yet now I urge you to keep up your courage
Acts27:23 For this very night an angel of the God to whom I belong and whom I serve stood before me
Acts27:24 saying
Acts27:25 Therefore
Acts27:26 But we must run aground on a certain island.
Acts27:27 But when the fourteenth night came
Acts27:28 They took soundings and found it to be twenty fathoms; and a little farther on they took another sounding and found it to be fifteen fathoms.
Acts27:29 Fearing that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks
Acts27:30 But as the sailors were trying to escape from the ship and had let down the ship's boat into the sea

Acts27:32 Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the ship's boat and let it fall away.
Acts27:33 Until the day was about to dawn
Acts27:34 Therefore I encourage you to take some food
Acts27:35 Having said this
Acts27:36 All of them were encouraged and they themselves also took food.
Acts27:37 All of us in the ship were two hundred and seventy-six persons.
Acts27:38 When they had eaten enough
Acts27:39 When day came
Acts27:40 And casting off the anchors
Acts27:41 But striking a reef where two seas met
Acts27:42 The soldiers' plan was to kill the prisoners
Acts27:43 but the centurion

Acts28:1 When they had been brought safely through
Acts28:2 The natives showed us extraordinary kindness; for because of the rain that had set in and because of the cold
Acts28:3 But when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks and laid them on the fire
Acts28:4 When the natives saw the creature hanging from his hand
Acts28:5 However he shook the creature off into the fire and suffered no harm.
Acts28:6 But they were expecting that he was about to swell up or suddenly fall down dead. But after they had waited a long time and had seen nothing unusual happen to him
Acts28:7 Now in the neighborhood of that place were lands belonging to the leading man of the island
Acts28:8 And it happened that the father of Publius was lying in bed afflicted with recurrent fever and dysentery; and Paul went in to see him and after he had prayed
Acts28:9 After this had happened
Acts28:10 They also honored us with many marks of respect; and when we were setting sail
Acts28:11 At the end of three months we set sail on an Alexandrian ship which had wintered at the island
Acts28:12 After we put in at Syracuse
Acts28:13 From there we sailed around and arrived at Rhegium
Acts28:14 There we found some brethren
Acts28:15 And the brethren

Acts28:18 And when they had examined me
Acts28:19 But when the Jews objected
Acts28:20 For this reason
Acts28:21 They said to him

Acts28:23 When they had set a day for Paul
Acts28:24 Some were being persuaded by the things spoken
Acts28:25 And when they did not agree with one another
Acts28:26 saying
Acts28:27 FOR THE HEART OF THIS PEOPLE HAS BECOME DULL
Acts28:28 Therefore let it be known to you that this salvation of God has been sent to the Gentiles; they will also listen.
