Acts28:31 preaching the kingdom of God and teaching concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all openness
Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 which He promised beforehand through His prophets in the holy Scriptures
Romans1:3 concerning His Son
Romans1:4 who was declared the Son of God with power by the resurrection from the dead
Romans1:5 through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith among all the Gentiles for His name's sake
Romans1:6 among whom you also are the called of Jesus Christ;
Romans1:7 to all who are beloved of God in Rome
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 For God
Romans1:10 always in my prayers making request
Romans1:11 For I long to see you so that I may impart some spiritual gift to you
Romans1:12 that is
Romans1:13 I do not want you to be unaware
Romans1:14 I am under obligation both to Greeks and to barbarians
Romans1:15 So
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel
Romans1:17 For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith to faith; as it is written
Romans1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men who suppress the truth in unrighteousness
Romans1:19 because that which is known about God is evident within them; for God made it evident to them.
Romans1:20 For since the creation of the world His invisible attributes
Romans1:21 For even though they knew God
Romans1:22 Professing to be wise
Romans1:23 and exchanged the glory of the incorruptible God for an image in the form of corruptible man and of birds and four-footed animals and crawling creatures.
Romans1:24 Therefore God gave them over in the lusts of their hearts to impurity
Romans1:25 For they exchanged the truth of God for a lie
Romans1:26 For this reason God gave them over to degrading passions; for their women exchanged the natural function for that which is unnatural
Romans1:27 and in the same way also the men abandoned the natural function of the woman and burned in their desire toward one another
Romans1:28 And just as they did not see fit to acknowledge God any longer
Romans1:29 being filled with all unrighteousness
Romans1:30 slanderers
Romans1:31 without understanding
Romans1:32 and although they know the ordinance of God
Romans2:1 Therefore you have no excuse
Romans2:2 And we know that the judgment of God rightly falls upon those who practice such things.
Romans2:3 But do you suppose this
Romans2:4 Or do you think lightly of the riches of His kindness and tolerance and patience
Romans2:5 But because of your stubbornness and unrepentant heart you are storing up wrath for yourself in the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God
Romans2:6 who WILL RENDER TO EACH PERSON ACCORDING TO HIS DEEDS:
Romans2:7 to those who by perseverance in doing good seek for glory and honor and immortality
Romans2:8 but to those who are selfishly ambitious and do not obey the truth
Romans2:9 There will be tribulation and distress for every soul of man who does evil
Romans2:10 but glory and honor and peace to everyone who does good
Romans2:11 For there is no partiality with God.
Romans2:12 For all who have sinned without the Law will also perish without the Law
Romans2:13 for it is not the hearers of the Law who are just before God
Romans2:14 For when Gentiles who do not have the Law do instinctively the things of the Law
Romans2:15 in that they show the work of the Law written in their hearts
Romans2:16 on the day when
Romans2:17 But if you bear the name Jew and rely upon the Law and boast in God
Romans2:18 and know His will and approve the things that are essential
Romans2:19 and are confident that you yourself are a guide to the blind
Romans2:20 a corrector of the foolish
Romans2:21 you
Romans2:22 You who say that one should not commit adultery
Romans2:23 You who boast in the Law
Romans2:24 For THE NAME OF GOD IS BLASPHEMED AMONG THE GENTILES BECAUSE OF YOU
Romans2:25 For indeed circumcision is of value if you practice the Law; but if you are a transgressor of the Law
Romans2:26 So if the uncircumcised man keeps the requirements of the Law
Romans2:27 And he who is physically uncircumcised
Romans2:28 For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly
Romans2:29 But he is a Jew who is one inwardly; and circumcision is that which is of the heart
Romans3:1 Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the benefit of circumcision?
Romans3:2 Great in every respect. First of all
Romans3:3 What then? If some did not believe
Romans3:4 May it never be! Rather
Romans3:5 But if our unrighteousness demonstrates the righteousness of God
Romans3:6 May it never be! For otherwise
Romans3:7 But if through my lie the truth of God abounded to His glory
Romans3:8 And why not say (as we are slanderously reported and as some claim that we say)
Romans3:9 What then? Are we better than they? Not at all; for we have already charged that both Jews and Greeks are all under sin;
Romans3:10 as it is written
Romans3:11 THERE IS NONE WHO UNDERSTANDS
Romans3:12 ALL HAVE TURNED ASIDE
Romans3:13 THEIR THROAT IS AN OPEN GRAVE
Romans3:14 WHOSE MOUTH IS FULL OF CURSING AND BITTERNESS;
Romans3:15 THEIR FEET ARE SWIFT TO SHED BLOOD
Romans3:16 DESTRUCTION AND MISERY ARE IN THEIR PATHS
Romans3:17 AND THE PATH OF PEACE THEY HAVE NOT KNOWN.
Romans3:18 THERE IS NO FEAR OF GOD BEFORE THEIR EYES.
Romans3:19 Now we know that whatever the Law says
Romans3:20 because by the works of the Law no flesh will be justified in His sight; for through the Law comes the knowledge of sin.
Romans3:21 But now apart from the Law the righteousness of God has been manifested
Romans3:22 even the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all those who believe; for there is no distinction;
Romans3:23 for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God
Romans3:24 being justified as a gift by His grace through the redemption which is in Christ Jesus;
Romans3:25 whom God displayed publicly as a propitiation in His blood through faith. This was to demonstrate His righteousness
Romans3:26 for the demonstration
Romans3:27 Where then is boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? Of works? No
Romans3:28 For we maintain that a man is justified by faith apart from works of the Law.
Romans3:29 Or is God the God of Jews only? Is He not the God of Gentiles also? Yes
Romans3:30 since indeed God who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith is one.
Romans3:31 Do we then nullify the Law through faith? May it never be! On the contrary
Romans4:1 What then shall we say that Abraham
Romans4:2 For if Abraham was justified by works
Romans4:3 For what does the Scripture say? ABRAHAM BELIEVED GOD
Romans4:4 Now to the one who works
Romans4:5 But to the one who does not work
Romans4:6 just as David also speaks of the blessing on the man to whom God credits righteousness apart from works:
Romans4:7 BLESSED ARE THOSE WHOSE LAWLESS DEEDS HAVE BEEN FORGIVEN
Romans4:8 BLESSED IS THE MAN WHOSE SIN THE LORD WILL NOT TAKE INTO ACCOUNT.
Romans4:9 Is this blessing then on the circumcised
Romans4:10 How then was it credited? While he was circumcised
Romans4:11 and he received the sign of circumcision
Romans4:12 and the father of circumcision to those who not only are of the circumcision
Romans4:13 For the promise to Abraham or to his descendants that he would be heir of the world was not through the Law
Romans4:14 For if those who are of the Law are heirs
Romans4:15 for the Law brings about wrath
Romans4:16 For this reason it is by faith
Romans4:17 (as it is written
Romans4:18 In hope against hope he believed
Romans4:19 Without becoming weak in faith he contemplated his own body
Romans4:20 yet
Romans4:21 and being fully assured that what God had promised
Romans4:22 Therefore IT WAS ALSO CREDITED TO HIM AS RIGHTEOUSNESS.
Romans4:23 Now not for his sake only was it written that it was credited to him
Romans4:24 but for our sake also
Romans4:25 He who was delivered over because of our transgressions
Romans5:1 Therefore
Romans5:2 through whom also we have obtained our introduction by faith into this grace in which we stand; and we exult in hope of the glory of God.
Romans5:3 And not only this
Romans5:4 and perseverance
Romans5:5 and hope does not disappoint
Romans5:6 For while we were still helpless
Romans5:7 For one will hardly die for a righteous man; though perhaps for the good man someone would dare even to die.
Romans5:8 But God demonstrates His own love toward us
Romans5:9 Much more then
Romans5:10 For if while we were enemies we were reconciled to God through the death of His Son
Romans5:11 And not only this
Romans5:12 Therefore
Romans5:13 for until the Law sin was in the world
Romans5:14 Nevertheless death reigned from Adam until Moses
Romans5:15 But the free gift is not like the transgression. For if by the transgression of the one the many died
Romans5:16 The gift is not like that which came through the one who sinned; for on the one hand the judgment arose from one transgression resulting in condemnation
Romans5:17 For if by the transgression of the one
Romans5:18 So then as through one transgression there resulted condemnation to all men
Romans5:19 For as through the one man's disobedience the many were made sinners
Romans5:20 The Law came in so that the transgression would increase; but where sin increased
Romans5:21 so that
Romans6:1 What shall we say then? Are we to continue in sin so that grace may increase?
Romans6:2 May it never be! How shall we who died to sin still live in it?
Romans6:3 Or do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus have been baptized into His death?
Romans6:4 Therefore we have been buried with Him through baptism into death
Romans6:5 For if we have become united with Him in the likeness of His death
Romans6:6 knowing this
Romans6:7 for he who has died is freed from sin.
Romans6:8 Now if we have died with Christ
Romans6:9 knowing that Christ
Romans6:10 For the death that He died
Romans6:11 Even so consider yourselves to be dead to sin
Romans6:12 Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body so that you obey its lusts
Romans6:13 and do not go on presenting the members of your body to sin as instruments of unrighteousness; but present yourselves to God as those alive from the dead
Romans6:14 For sin shall not be master over you
Romans6:15 What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? May it never be!
Romans6:16 Do you not know that when you present yourselves to someone as slaves for obedience
Romans6:17 But thanks be to God that though you were slaves of sin
Romans6:18 and having been freed from sin
Romans6:19 I am speaking in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh. For just as you presented your members as slaves to impurity and to lawlessness
Romans6:20 For when you were slaves of sin
Romans6:21 Therefore what benefit were you then deriving from the things of which you are now ashamed? For the outcome of those things is death.
Romans6:22 But now having been freed from sin and enslaved to God
Romans6:23 For the wages of sin is death
Romans7:1 Or do you not know
Romans7:2 For the married woman is bound by law to her husband while he is living; but if her husband dies
Romans7:3 So then
Romans7:4 Therefore
Romans7:5 For while we were in the flesh
Romans7:6 But now we have been released from the Law
Romans7:7 What shall we say then? Is the Law sin? May it never be! On the contrary
Romans7:8 But sin
Romans7:9 I was once alive apart from the Law; but when the commandment came
Romans7:10 and this commandment
Romans7:11 for sin
Romans7:12 So then
Romans7:13 Therefore did that which is good become a cause of death for me? May it never be! Rather it was sin
Romans7:14 For we know that the Law is spiritual
Romans7:15 For what I am doing
Romans7:16 But if I do the very thing I do not want to do
Romans7:17 So now
Romans7:18 For I know that nothing good dwells in me
Romans7:19 For the good that I want
Romans7:20 But if I am doing the very thing I do not want
Romans7:21 I find then the principle that evil is present in me
Romans7:22 For I joyfully concur with the law of God in the inner man
Romans7:23 but I see a different law in the members of my body
Romans7:24 Wretched man that I am! Who will set me free from the body of this death?
Romans7:25 Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then
Romans8:1 Therefore there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
Romans8:2 For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and of death.
Romans8:3 For what the Law could not do
Romans8:4 so that the requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us
Romans8:5 For those who are according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh
Romans8:6 For the mind set on the flesh is death

1Corinthians9:10 Or is He speaking altogether for our sake? Yes
1Corinthians9:11 If we sowed spiritual things in you
1Corinthians9:12 If others share the right over you
1Corinthians9:13 Do you not know that those who perform sacred services eat the food of the temple
1Corinthians9:14 So also the Lord directed those who proclaim the gospel to get their living from the gospel.
1Corinthians9:15 But I have used none of these things. And I am not writing these things so that it will be done so in my case; for it would be better for me to die than have any man make my boast an empty one.
1Corinthians9:16 For if I preach the gospel
1Corinthians9:17 For if I do this voluntarily
1Corinthians9:18 What then is my reward? That
1Corinthians9:19 For though I am free from all men
1Corinthians9:20 To the Jews I became as a Jew
1Corinthians9:21 to those who are without law
1Corinthians9:22 To the weak I became weak
1Corinthians9:23 I do all things for the sake of the gospel
1Corinthians9:24 Do you not know that those who run in a race all run
1Corinthians9:25 Everyone who competes in the games exercises self-control in all things. They then do it to receive a perishable wreath
1Corinthians9:26 Therefore I run in such a way
1Corinthians9:27 but I discipline my body and make it my slave
1Corinthians10:1 For I do not want you to be unaware
1Corinthians10:2 and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
1Corinthians10:3 and all ate the same spiritual food;
1Corinthians10:4 and all drank the same spiritual drink
1Corinthians10:5 Nevertheless
1Corinthians10:6 Now these things happened as examples for us
1Corinthians10:7 Do not be idolaters
1Corinthians10:8 Nor let us act immorally
1Corinthians10:9 Nor let us try the Lord
1Corinthians10:10 Nor grumble
1Corinthians10:11 Now these things happened to them as an example
1Corinthians10:12 Therefore let him who thinks he stands take heed that he does not fall.
1Corinthians10:13 No temptation has overtaken you but such as is common to man; and God is faithful
1Corinthians10:14 Therefore
1Corinthians10:15 I speak as to wise men; you judge what I say.
1Corinthians10:16 Is not the cup of blessing which we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Is not the bread which we break a sharing in the body of Christ?
1Corinthians10:17 Since there is one bread
1Corinthians10:18 Look at the nation Israel; are not those who eat the sacrifices sharers in the altar?
1Corinthians10:19 What do I mean then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything
1Corinthians10:20 No
1Corinthians10:21 You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
1Corinthians10:22 Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? We are not stronger than He
1Corinthians10:23 All things are lawful
1Corinthians10:24 Let no one seek his own good
1Corinthians10:25 Eat anything that is sold in the meat market without asking questions for conscience' sake;
1Corinthians10:26 FOR THE EARTH IS THE LORD'S
1Corinthians10:27 If one of the unbelievers invites you and you want to go
1Corinthians10:28 But if anyone says to you
1Corinthians10:29 I mean not your own conscience
1Corinthians10:30 If I partake with thankfulness
1Corinthians10:31 Whether
1Corinthians10:32 Give no offense either to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God;
1Corinthians10:33 just as I also please all men in all things
1Corinthians11:1 Be imitators of me
1Corinthians11:2 Now I praise you because you remember me in everything and hold firmly to the traditions
1Corinthians11:3 But I want you to understand that Christ is the head of every man
1Corinthians11:4 Every man who has something on his head while praying or prophesying disgraces his head.
1Corinthians11:5 But every woman who has her head uncovered while praying or prophesying disgraces her head
1Corinthians11:6 For if a woman does not cover her head
1Corinthians11:7 For a man ought not to have his head covered
1Corinthians11:8 For man does not originate from woman
1Corinthians11:9 for indeed man was not created for the woman's sake
1Corinthians11:10 Therefore the woman ought to have a symbol of authority on her head
1Corinthians11:11 However
1Corinthians11:12 For as the woman originates from the man
1Corinthians11:13 Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?
1Corinthians11:14 Does not even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair
1Corinthians11:15 but if a woman has long hair
1Corinthians11:16 But if one is inclined to be contentious
1Corinthians11:17 But in giving this instruction
1Corinthians11:18 For
1Corinthians11:19 For there must also be factions among you
1Corinthians11:20 Therefore when you meet together
1Corinthians11:21 for in your eating each one takes his own supper first; and one is hungry and another is drunk.
1Corinthians11:22 What! Do you not have houses in which to eat and drink? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this I will not praise you.
1Corinthians11:23 For I received from the Lord that which I also delivered to you
1Corinthians11:24 and when He had given thanks
1Corinthians11:25 In the same way He took the cup also after supper
1Corinthians11:26 For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup
1Corinthians11:27 Therefore whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner
1Corinthians11:28 But a man must examine himself
1Corinthians11:29 For he who eats and drinks
1Corinthians11:30 For this reason many among you are weak and sick
1Corinthians11:31 But if we judged ourselves rightly
1Corinthians11:32 But when we are judged
1Corinthians11:33 So then
1Corinthians11:34 If anyone is hungry
1Corinthians12:1 Now concerning spiritual gifts
1Corinthians12:2 You know that when you were pagans
1Corinthians12:3 Therefore I make known to you that no one speaking by the Spirit of God says
1Corinthians12:4 Now there are varieties of gifts
1Corinthians12:5 And there are varieties of ministries
1Corinthians12:6 There are varieties of effects
1Corinthians12:7 But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
1Corinthians12:8 For to one is given the word of wisdom through the Spirit
1Corinthians12:9 to another faith by the same Spirit
1Corinthians12:10 and to another the effecting of miracles
1Corinthians12:11 But one and the same Spirit works all these things
1Corinthians12:12 For even as the body is one and yet has many members
1Corinthians12:13 For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body
1Corinthians12:14 For the body is not one member
1Corinthians12:15 If the foot says
1Corinthians12:16 And if the ear says
1Corinthians12:17 If the whole body were an eye
1Corinthians12:18 But now God has placed the members
1Corinthians12:19 If they were all one member
1Corinthians12:20 But now there are many members
1Corinthians12:21 And the eye cannot say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 On the contrary
1Corinthians12:23 and those members of the body which we deem less honorable
1Corinthians12:24 whereas our more presentable members have no need of it. But God has so composed the body
1Corinthians12:25 so that there may be no division in the body
1Corinthians12:26 And if one member suffers
1Corinthians12:27 Now you are Christ's body
1Corinthians12:28 And God has appointed in the church
1Corinthians12:29 All are not apostles
1Corinthians12:30 All do not have gifts of healings
1Corinthians12:31 But earnestly desire the greater gifts. And I show you a still more excellent way.

1Corinthians13:3 And if I give all my possessions to feed the poor

1Corinthians13:5 does not act unbecomingly; it does not seek its own
1Corinthians13:6 does not rejoice in unrighteousness
1Corinthians13:7 bears all things

1Corinthians13:10 but when the perfect comes

1Corinthians13:12 For now we see in a mirror dimly
1Corinthians13:13 But now faith
1Corinthians14:1 Pursue love
1Corinthians14:2 For one who speaks in a tongue does not speak to men but to God; for no one understands
1Corinthians14:3 But one who prophesies speaks to men for edification and exhortation and consolation.
1Corinthians14:4 One who speaks in a tongue edifies himself; but one who prophesies edifies the church.
1Corinthians14:5 Now I wish that you all spoke in tongues
1Corinthians14:6 But now

1Corinthians14:9 So also you
1Corinthians14:10 There are
1Corinthians14:11 If then I do not know the meaning of the language
1Corinthians14:12 So also you
1Corinthians14:13 Therefore let one who speaks in a tongue pray that he may interpret.
1Corinthians14:14 For if I pray in a tongue
1Corinthians14:15 What is the outcome then? I will pray with the spirit and I will pray with the mind also; I will sing with the spirit and I will sing with the mind also.
1Corinthians14:16 Otherwise if you bless in the spirit only
1Corinthians14:17 For you are giving thanks well enough
1Corinthians14:18 I thank God
1Corinthians14:19 however
1Corinthians14:20 Brethren

1Corinthians14:22 So then tongues are for a sign
1Corinthians14:23 Therefore if the whole church assembles together and all speak in tongues
1Corinthians14:24 But if all prophesy
1Corinthians14:25 the secrets of his heart are disclosed; and so he will fall on his face and worship God
1Corinthians14:26 What is the outcome then
1Corinthians14:27 If anyone speaks in a tongue
1Corinthians14:28 but if there is no interpreter
1Corinthians14:29 Let two or three prophets speak
1Corinthians14:30 But if a revelation is made to another who is seated
1Corinthians14:31 For you can all prophesy one by one
1Corinthians14:32 and the spirits of prophets are subject to prophets;
1Corinthians14:33 for God is not a God of confusion but of peace
1Corinthians14:34 The women are to keep silent in the churches; for they are not permitted to speak
1Corinthians14:35 If they desire to learn anything
1Corinthians14:36 Was it from you that the word of God first went forth? Or has it come to you only?

1Corinthians14:38 But if anyone does not recognize this
1Corinthians14:39 Therefore
1Corinthians14:40 But all things must be done properly and in an orderly manner.

1Corinthians15:2 by which also you are saved
1Corinthians15:3 For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received

1Corinthians15:5 and that He appeared to Cephas

1Corinthians15:7 then He appeared to James
1Corinthians15:8 and last of all
1Corinthians15:9 For I am the least of the apostles
1Corinthians15:10 But by the grace of God I am what I am
1Corinthians15:11 Whether then it was I or they
1Corinthians15:12 Now if Christ is preached
1Corinthians15:13 But if there is no resurrection of the dead
1Corinthians15:14 and if Christ has not been raised
1Corinthians15:15 Moreover we are even found to be false witnesses of God
1Corinthians15:16 For if the dead are not raised
1Corinthians15:17 and if Christ has not been raised
1Corinthians15:18 Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
1Corinthians15:19 If we have hoped in Christ in this life only
1Corinthians15:20 But now Christ has been raised from the dead
1Corinthians15:21 For since by a man came death
1Corinthians15:22 For as in Adam all die
1Corinthians15:23 But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits
1Corinthians15:24 then comes the end
1Corinthians15:25 For He must reign until He has put all His enemies under His feet.
1Corinthians15:26 The last enemy that will be abolished is death.
1Corinthians15:27 For HE HAS PUT ALL THINGS IN SUBJECTION UNDER HIS FEET. But when He says
1Corinthians15:28 When all things are subjected to Him
1Corinthians15:29 Otherwise
1Corinthians15:30 Why are we also in danger every hour?
1Corinthians15:31 I affirm
1Corinthians15:32 If from human motives I fought with wild beasts at Ephesus
1Corinthians15:33 Do not be deceived: Bad company corrupts good morals.
1Corinthians15:34 Become sober-minded as you ought
1Corinthians15:35 But someone will say
1Corinthians15:36 You fool! That which you sow does not come to life unless it dies;
1Corinthians15:37 and that which you sow
1Corinthians15:38 But God gives it a body just as He wished
1Corinthians15:39 All flesh is not the same flesh
1Corinthians15:40 There are also heavenly bodies and earthly bodies
1Corinthians15:41 There is one glory of the sun
1Corinthians15:42 So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown a perishable body
1Corinthians15:43 it is sown in dishonor
1Corinthians15:44 it is sown a natural body
1Corinthians15:45 So also it is written
1Corinthians15:46 However
1Corinthians15:47 The first man is from the earth
1Corinthians15:48 As is the earthy
1Corinthians15:49 Just as we have borne the image of the earthy
1Corinthians15:50 Now I say this
1Corinthians15:51 Behold
1Corinthians15:52 in a moment
1Corinthians15:53 For this perishable must put on the imperishable
1Corinthians15:54 But when this perishable will have put on the imperishable
1Corinthians15:55 O DEATH
1Corinthians15:56 The sting of death is sin
1Corinthians15:57 but thanks be to God
1Corinthians15:58 Therefore
1Corinthians16:1 Now concerning the collection for the saints
1Corinthians16:2 On the first day of every week each one of you is to put aside and save
1Corinthians16:3 When I arrive
1Corinthians16:4 and if it is fitting for me to go also
1Corinthians16:5 But I will come to you after I go through Macedonia
1Corinthians16:6 and perhaps I will stay with you
1Corinthians16:7 For I do not wish to see you now just in passing; for I hope to remain with you for some time
1Corinthians16:8 But I will remain in Ephesus until Pentecost;
1Corinthians16:9 for a wide door for effective service has opened to me
1Corinthians16:10 Now if Timothy comes
1Corinthians16:11 So let no one despise him. But send him on his way in peace
1Corinthians16:12 But concerning Apollos our brother
1Corinthians16:13 Be on the alert
1Corinthians16:14 Let all that you do be done in love.
1Corinthians16:15 Now I urge you
1Corinthians16:16 that you also be in subjection to such men and to everyone who helps in the work and labors.
1Corinthians16:17 I rejoice over the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus
1Corinthians16:18 For they have refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore acknowledge such men.
1Corinthians16:19 The churches of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you heartily in the Lord
1Corinthians16:20 All the brethren greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
1Corinthians16:21 The greeting is in my own hand--Paul.
1Corinthians16:22 If anyone does not love the Lord
1Corinthians16:23 The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
1Corinthians16:24 My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

Philippians2:22 But you know of his proven worth
Philippians2:23 Therefore I hope to send him immediately
Philippians2:24 and I trust in the Lord that I myself also will be coming shortly.
Philippians2:25 But I thought it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus
Philippians2:26 because he was longing for you all and was distressed because you had heard that he was sick.
Philippians2:27 For indeed he was sick to the point of death
Philippians2:28 Therefore I have sent him all the more eagerly so that when you see him again you may rejoice and I may be less concerned about you.
Philippians2:29 Receive him then in the Lord with all joy
Philippians2:30 because he came close to death for the work of Christ

Philippians3:5 circumcised the eighth day
Philippians3:6 as to zeal
Philippians3:7 But whatever things were gain to me
Philippians3:8 More than that

Philippians3:11 in order that I may attain to the resurrection from the dead.
Philippians3:12 Not that I have already obtained it or have already become perfect

Philippians3:16 however

1Thessalonians1:6 You also became imitators of us and of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:7 so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.

1Thessalonians2:6 nor did we seek glory from men

1Thessalonians2:10 You are witnesses

1Thessalonians2:12 so that you would walk in a manner worthy of the God who calls you into His own kingdom and glory.

1Timothy3:3 not addicted to wine or pugnacious

1Timothy3:9 but holding to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.

1Timothy3:12 Deacons must be husbands of only one wife

1Timothy6:3 If anyone advocates a different doctrine and does not agree with sound words
1Timothy6:4 he is conceited and understands nothing; but he has a morbid interest in controversial questions and disputes about words
1Timothy6:5 and constant friction between men of depraved mind and deprived of the truth
1Timothy6:6 But godliness actually is a means of great gain when accompanied by contentment.
1Timothy6:7 For we have brought nothing into the world
1Timothy6:8 If we have food and covering
1Timothy6:9 But those who want to get rich fall into temptation and a snare and many foolish and harmful desires which plunge men into ruin and destruction.
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is a root of all sorts of evil
1Timothy6:11 But flee from these things
1Timothy6:12 Fight the good fight of faith; take hold of the eternal life to which you were called
1Timothy6:13 I charge you in the presence of God
1Timothy6:14 that you keep the commandment without stain or reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ
1Timothy6:15 which He will bring about at the proper time--He who is the blessed and only Sovereign
1Timothy6:16 who alone possesses immortality and dwells in unapproachable light
1Timothy6:17 Instruct those who are rich in this present world not to be conceited or to fix their hope on the uncertainty of riches
1Timothy6:18 Instruct them to do good
1Timothy6:19 storing up for themselves the treasure of a good foundation for the future
1Timothy6:20 O Timothy
1Timothy6:21 which some have professed and thus gone astray from the faith. Grace be with you.

2Timothy1:6 For this reason I remind you to kindle afresh the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.

2Timothy1:8 Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord or of me His prisoner
2Timothy1:9 who has saved us and called us with a holy calling
2Timothy1:10 but now has been revealed by the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus
2Timothy1:11 for which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle and a teacher.
2Timothy1:12 For this reason I also suffer these things
2Timothy1:13 Retain the standard of sound words which you have heard from me
2Timothy1:14 Guard
2Timothy1:15 You are aware of the fact that all who are in Asia turned away from me
2Timothy1:16 The Lord grant mercy to the house of Onesiphorus
2Timothy1:17 but when he was in Rome
2Timothy1:18 the Lord grant to him to find mercy from the Lord on that day--and you know very well what services he rendered at Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 You therefore
2Timothy2:2 The things which you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses
2Timothy2:3 Suffer hardship with me
2Timothy2:4 No soldier in active service entangles himself in the affairs of everyday life
2Timothy2:5 Also if anyone competes as an athlete
2Timothy2:6 The hard-working farmer ought to be the first to receive his share of the crops.
2Timothy2:7 Consider what I say
2Timothy2:8 Remember Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:9 for which I suffer hardship even to imprisonment as a criminal; but the word of God is not imprisoned.
2Timothy2:10 For this reason I endure all things for the sake of those who are chosen
2Timothy2:11 It is a trustworthy statement: For if we died with Him
2Timothy2:12 If we endure
2Timothy2:13 If we are faithless
2Timothy2:14 Remind them of these things
2Timothy2:15 Be diligent to present yourself approved to God as a workman who does not need to be ashamed
2Timothy2:16 But avoid worldly and empty chatter
2Timothy2:17 and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus
2Timothy2:18 men who have gone astray from the truth saying that the resurrection has already taken place
2Timothy2:19 Nevertheless
2Timothy2:20 Now in a large house there are not only gold and silver vessels
2Timothy2:21 Therefore
2Timothy2:22 Now flee from youthful lusts and pursue righteousness
2Timothy2:23 But refuse foolish and ignorant speculations
2Timothy2:24 The Lord's bond-servant must not be quarrelsome
2Timothy2:25 with gentleness correcting those who are in opposition
2Timothy2:26 and they may come to their senses and escape from the snare of the devil
2Timothy3:1 But realize this
2Timothy3:2 For men will be lovers of self
2Timothy3:3 unloving
2Timothy3:4 treacherous
2Timothy3:5 holding to a form of godliness
2Timothy3:6 For among them are those who enter into households and captivate weak women weighed down with sins
2Timothy3:7 always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
2Timothy3:8 Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses
2Timothy3:9 But they will not make further progress; for their folly will be obvious to all
2Timothy3:10 Now you followed my teaching
2Timothy3:11 persecutions
2Timothy3:12 Indeed
2Timothy3:13 But evil men and impostors will proceed from bad to worse
2Timothy3:14 You
2Timothy3:15 and that from childhood you have known the sacred writings which are able to give you the wisdom that leads to salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
2Timothy3:16 All Scripture is inspired by God and profitable for teaching
2Timothy3:17 so that the man of God may be adequate
2Timothy4:1 I solemnly charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus
2Timothy4:2 preach the word; be ready in season and out of season; reprove
2Timothy4:3 For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but wanting to have their ears tickled
2Timothy4:4 and will turn away their ears from the truth and will turn aside to myths.
2Timothy4:5 But you
2Timothy4:6 For I am already being poured out as a drink offering
2Timothy4:7 I have fought the good fight
2Timothy4:8 in the future there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness
2Timothy4:9 Make every effort to come to me soon;
2Timothy4:10 for Demas
2Timothy4:11 Only Luke is with me. Pick up Mark and bring him with you
2Timothy4:12 But Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
2Timothy4:13 When you come bring the cloak which I left at Troas with Carpus
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the coppersmith did me much harm; the Lord will repay him according to his deeds.
2Timothy4:15 Be on guard against him yourself
2Timothy4:16 At my first defense no one supported me
2Timothy4:17 But the Lord stood with me and strengthened me
2Timothy4:18 The Lord will rescue me from every evil deed
2Timothy4:19 Greet Prisca and Aquila
2Timothy4:20 Erastus remained at Corinth
2Timothy4:21 Make every effort to come before winter. Eubulus greets you
2Timothy4:22 The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.
Titus1:1 Paul
Titus1:2 in the hope of eternal life
Titus1:3 but at the proper time manifested
Titus1:4 To Titus
Titus1:5 For this reason I left you in Crete
Titus1:6 namely
Titus1:7 For the overseer must be above reproach as God's steward
Titus1:8 but hospitable
Titus1:9 holding fast the faithful word which is in accordance with the teaching
Titus1:10 For there are many rebellious men
Titus1:11 who must be silenced because they are upsetting whole families
Titus1:12 One of themselves
Titus1:13 This testimony is true. For this reason reprove them severely so that they may be sound in the faith
Titus1:14 not paying attention to Jewish myths and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.
Titus1:15 To the pure
Titus1:16 They profess to know God
Titus2:1 But as for you
Titus2:2 Older men are to be temperate
Titus2:3 Older women likewise are to be reverent in their behavior
Titus2:4 so that they may encourage the young women to love their husbands
Titus2:5 to be sensible
Titus2:6 Likewise urge the young men to be sensible;
Titus2:7 in all things show yourself to be an example of good deeds
Titus2:8 sound in speech which is beyond reproach
Titus2:9 Urge bondslaves to be subject to their own masters in everything
Titus2:10 not pilfering
Titus2:11 For the grace of God has appeared
Titus2:12 instructing us to deny ungodliness and worldly desires and to live sensibly
Titus2:13 looking for the blessed hope and the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior
Titus2:14 who gave Himself for us to redeem us from every lawless deed
Titus2:15 These things speak and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one disregard you.
Titus3:1 Remind them to be subject to rulers
Titus3:2 to malign no one
Titus3:3 For we also once were foolish ourselves
Titus3:4 But when the kindness of God our Savior and His love for mankind appeared
Titus3:5 He saved us
Titus3:6 whom He poured out upon us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior
Titus3:7 so that being justified by His grace we would be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
Titus3:8 This is a trustworthy statement; and concerning these things I want you to speak confidently
Titus3:9 But avoid foolish controversies and genealogies and strife and disputes about the Law
Titus3:10 Reject a factious man after a first and second warning
Titus3:11 knowing that such a man is perverted and is sinning
Titus3:12 When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you
Titus3:13 Diligently help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way so that nothing is lacking for them.
Titus3:14 Our people must also learn to engage in good deeds to meet pressing needs
Titus3:15 All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.
Philemon1:1 Paul
Philemon1:2 and to Apphia our sister
Philemon1:3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philemon1:4 I thank my God always
Philemon1:5 because I hear of your love and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and toward all the saints;
Philemon1:6 and I pray that the fellowship of your faith may become effective through the knowledge of every good thing which is in you for Christ's sake.
Philemon1:7 For I have come to have much joy and comfort in your love
Philemon1:8 Therefore
Philemon1:9 yet for love's sake I rather appeal to you--since I am such a person as Paul
Philemon1:10 I appeal to you for my child Onesimus
Philemon1:11 who formerly was useless to you
Philemon1:12 I have sent him back to you in person
Philemon1:13 whom I wished to keep with me
Philemon1:14 but without your consent I did not want to do anything
Philemon1:15 For perhaps he was for this reason separated from you for a while
Philemon1:16 no longer as a slave
Philemon1:17 If then you regard me a partner
Philemon1:18 But if he has wronged you in any way or owes you anything
Philemon1:19 I
Philemon1:20 Yes
Philemon1:21 Having confidence in your obedience
Philemon1:22 At the same time also prepare me a lodging
Philemon1:23 Epaphras
Philemon1:24 as do Mark
Philemon1:25 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
Hebrews1:1 God
Hebrews1:2 in these last days has spoken to us in His Son
Hebrews1:3 And He is the radiance of His glory and the exact representation of His nature
Hebrews1:4 having become as much better than the angels
Hebrews1:5 For to which of the angels did He ever say
Hebrews1:6 And when He again brings the firstborn into the world
Hebrews1:7 And of the angels He says
Hebrews1:8 But of the Son He says
Hebrews1:9 YOU HAVE LOVED RIGHTEOUSNESS AND HATED LAWLESSNESS; THEREFORE GOD
Hebrews1:10 And
Hebrews1:11 THEY WILL PERISH
Hebrews1:12 AND LIKE A MANTLE YOU WILL ROLL THEM UP; LIKE A GARMENT THEY WILL ALSO BE CHANGED. BUT YOU ARE THE SAME
Hebrews1:13 But to which of the angels has He ever said
Hebrews1:14 Are they not all ministering spirits
Hebrews2:1 For this reason we must pay much closer attention to what we have heard
Hebrews2:2 For if the word spoken through angels proved unalterable
Hebrews2:3 how will we escape if we neglect so great a salvation? After it was at the first spoken through the Lord
Hebrews2:4 God also testifying with them
Hebrews2:5 For He did not subject to angels the world to come
Hebrews2:6 But one has testified somewhere
Hebrews2:7 YOU HAVE MADE HIM FOR A LITTLE WHILE LOWER THAN THE ANGELS; YOU HAVE CROWNED HIM WITH GLORY AND HONOR
Hebrews2:8 YOU HAVE PUT ALL THINGS IN SUBJECTION UNDER HIS FEET. For in subjecting all things to him
Hebrews2:9 But we do see Him who was made for a little while lower than the angels
Hebrews2:10 For it was fitting for Him
Hebrews2:11 For both He who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are all from one Father; for which reason He is not ashamed to call them brethren
Hebrews2:12 saying
Hebrews2:13 And again
Hebrews2:14 Therefore
Hebrews2:15 and might free those who through fear of death were subject to slavery all their lives.
Hebrews2:16 For assuredly He does not give help to angels
Hebrews2:17 Therefore
Hebrews2:18 For since He Himself was tempted in that which He has suffered
Hebrews3:1 Therefore
Hebrews3:2 He was faithful to Him who appointed Him
Hebrews3:3 For He has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses
Hebrews3:4 For every house is built by someone
Hebrews3:5 Now Moses was faithful in all His house as a servant
Hebrews3:6 but Christ was faithful as a Son over His house--whose house we are
Hebrews3:7 Therefore
Hebrews3:8 DO NOT HARDEN YOUR HEARTS AS WHEN THEY PROVOKED ME
Hebrews3:9 WHERE YOUR FATHERS TRIED Me BY TESTING Me
Hebrews3:10 THEREFORE I WAS ANGRY WITH THIS GENERATION
Hebrews3:11 AS I SWORE IN MY WRATH
Hebrews3:12 Take care
Hebrews3:13 But encourage one another day after day
Hebrews3:14 For we have become partakers of Christ
Hebrews3:15 while it is said
Hebrews3:16 For who provoked Him when they had heard? Indeed
Hebrews3:17 And with whom was He angry for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned
Hebrews3:18 And to whom did He swear that they would not enter His rest
Hebrews3:19 So we see that they were not able to enter because of unbelief.
Hebrews4:1 Therefore
Hebrews4:2 For indeed we have had good news preached to us
Hebrews4:3 For we who have believed enter that rest
Hebrews4:4 For He has said somewhere concerning the seventh day: AND GOD RESTED ON THE SEVENTH DAY FROM ALL HIS WORKS;
Hebrews4:5 and again in this passage
Hebrews4:6 Therefore
Hebrews4:7 He again fixes a certain day
Hebrews4:8 For if Joshua had given them rest
Hebrews4:9 So there remains a Sabbath rest for the people of God.
Hebrews4:10 For the one who has entered His rest has himself also rested from his works
Hebrews4:11 Therefore let us be diligent to enter that rest
Hebrews4:12 For the word of God is living and active and sharper than any two-edged sword
Hebrews4:13 And there is no creature hidden from His sight
Hebrews4:14 Therefore
Hebrews4:15 For we do not have a high priest who cannot sympathize with our weaknesses
Hebrews4:16 Therefore let us draw near with confidence to the throne of grace
Hebrews5:1 For every high priest taken from among men is appointed on behalf of men in things pertaining to God
Hebrews5:2 he can deal gently with the ignorant and misguided
Hebrews5:3 and because of it he is obligated to offer sacrifices for sins
Hebrews5:4 And no one takes the honor to himself
Hebrews5:5 So also Christ did not glorify Himself so as to become a high priest
Hebrews5:6 just as He says also in another passage
Hebrews5:7 In the days of His flesh
Hebrews5:8 Although He was a Son
Hebrews5:9 And having been made perfect
Hebrews5:10 being designated by God as a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
Hebrews5:11 Concerning him we have much to say
Hebrews5:12 For though by this time you ought to be teachers
Hebrews5:13 For everyone who partakes only of milk is not accustomed to the word of righteousness
Hebrews5:14 But solid food is for the mature
Hebrews6:1 Therefore leaving the elementary teaching about the Christ
Hebrews6:2 of instruction about washings and laying on of hands
Hebrews6:3 And this we will do
Hebrews6:4 For in the case of those who have once been enlightened and have tasted of the heavenly gift and have been made partakers of the Holy Spirit
Hebrews6:5 and have tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come
Hebrews6:6 and then have fallen away
Hebrews6:7 For ground that drinks the rain which often falls on it and brings forth vegetation useful to those for whose sake it is also tilled
Hebrews6:8 but if it yields thorns and thistles
Hebrews6:9 But
Hebrews6:10 For God is not unjust so as to forget your work and the love which you have shown toward His name
Hebrews6:11 And we desire that each one of you show the same diligence so as to realize the full assurance of hope until the end
Hebrews6:12 so that you will not be sluggish
Hebrews6:13 For when God made the promise to Abraham
Hebrews6:14 saying
Hebrews6:15 And so
Hebrews6:16 For men swear by one greater than themselves
Hebrews6:17 In the same way God
Hebrews6:18 so that by two unchangeable things in which it is impossible for God to lie
Hebrews6:19 This hope we have as an anchor of the soul
Hebrews6:20 where Jesus has entered as a forerunner for us
Hebrews7:1 For this Melchizedek
Hebrews7:2 to whom also Abraham apportioned a tenth part of all the spoils
Hebrews7:3 Without father
Hebrews7:4 Now observe how great this man was to whom Abraham
Hebrews7:5 And those indeed of the sons of Levi who receive the priest's office have commandment in the Law to collect a tenth from the people
Hebrews7:6 But the one whose genealogy is not traced from them collected a tenth from Abraham and blessed the one who had the promises.
Hebrews7:7 But without any dispute the lesser is blessed by the greater.
Hebrews7:8 In this case mortal men receive tithes
Hebrews7:9 And
Hebrews7:10 for he was still in the loins of his father when Melchizedek met him.
Hebrews7:11 Now if perfection was through the Levitical priesthood (for on the basis of it the people received the Law)
Hebrews7:12 For when the priesthood is changed
Hebrews7:13 For the one concerning whom these things are spoken belongs to another tribe
Hebrews7:14 For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah
Hebrews7:15 And this is clearer still
Hebrews7:16 who has become such not on the basis of a law of physical requirement
Hebrews7:17 For it is attested of Him
Hebrews7:18 For
Hebrews7:19 (for the Law made nothing perfect)
Hebrews7:20 And inasmuch as it was not without an oath
Hebrews7:21 (for they indeed became priests without an oath
Hebrews7:22 so much the more also Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
Hebrews7:23 The former priests
Hebrews7:24 but Jesus
Hebrews7:25 Therefore He is able also to save forever those who draw near to God through Him
Hebrews7:26 For it was fitting for us to have such a high priest
Hebrews7:27 who does not need daily
Hebrews7:28 For the Law appoints men as high priests who are weak
Hebrews8:1 Now the main point in what has been said is this: we have such a high priest
Hebrews8:2 a minister in the sanctuary and in the true tabernacle
Hebrews8:3 For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices; so it is necessary that this high priest also have something to offer.
Hebrews8:4 Now if He were on earth
Hebrews8:5 who serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things
Hebrews8:6 But now He has obtained a more excellent ministry
Hebrews8:7 For if that first covenant had been faultless
Hebrews8:8 For finding fault with them
Hebrews8:9 NOT LIKE THE COVENANT WHICH I MADE WITH THEIR FATHERS ON THE DAY WHEN I TOOK THEM BY THE HAND TO LEAD THEM OUT OF THE LAND OF EGYPT; FOR THEY DID NOT CONTINUE IN MY COVENANT
Hebrews8:10 FOR THIS IS THE COVENANT THAT I WILL MAKE WITH THE HOUSE OF ISRAEL AFTER THOSE DAYS
Hebrews8:11 AND THEY SHALL NOT TEACH EVERYONE HIS FELLOW CITIZEN
Hebrews8:12 FOR I WILL BE MERCIFUL TO THEIR INIQUITIES
Hebrews8:13 When He said
Hebrews9:1 Now even the first covenant had regulations of divine worship and the earthly sanctuary.
Hebrews9:2 For there was a tabernacle prepared
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second veil there was a tabernacle which is called the Holy of Holies
Hebrews9:4 having a golden altar of incense and the ark of the covenant covered on all sides with gold
Hebrews9:5 and above it were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat; but of these things we cannot now speak in detail.
Hebrews9:6 Now when these things have been so prepared
Hebrews9:7 but into the second
Hebrews9:8 The Holy Spirit is signifying this
Hebrews9:9 which is a symbol for the present time. Accordingly both gifts and sacrifices are offered which cannot make the worshiper perfect in conscience
Hebrews9:10 since they relate only to food and drink and various washings
Hebrews9:11 But when Christ appeared as a high priest of the good things to come
Hebrews9:12 and not through the blood of goats and calves
Hebrews9:13 For if the blood of goats and bulls and the ashes of a heifer sprinkling those who have been defiled sanctify for the cleansing of the flesh
Hebrews9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 For this reason He is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 For where a covenant is
Hebrews9:17 For a covenant is valid only when men are dead
Hebrews9:18 Therefore even the first covenant was not inaugurated without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when every commandment had been spoken by Moses to all the people according to the Law
Hebrews9:20 saying
Hebrews9:21 And in the same way he sprinkled both the tabernacle and all the vessels of the ministry with the blood.
Hebrews9:22 And according to the Law
Hebrews9:23 Therefore it was necessary for the copies of the things in the heavens to be cleansed with these
Hebrews9:24 For Christ did not enter a holy place made with hands
Hebrews9:25 nor was it that He would offer Himself often
Hebrews9:26 Otherwise
Hebrews9:27 And inasmuch as it is appointed for men to die once and after this comes judgment
Hebrews9:28 so Christ also
Hebrews10:1 For the Law
Hebrews10:2 Otherwise
Hebrews10:3 But in those sacrifices there is a reminder of sins year by year.
Hebrews10:4 For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
Hebrews10:5 Therefore
Hebrews10:6 IN WHOLE BURNT OFFERINGS AND sacrifices FOR SIN YOU HAVE TAKEN NO PLEASURE.
Hebrews10:7 THEN I SAID
Hebrews10:8 After saying above
Hebrews10:9 then He said
Hebrews10:10 By this will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
Hebrews10:11 Every priest stands daily ministering and offering time after time the same sacrifices
Hebrews10:12 but He
Hebrews10:13 waiting from that time onward UNTIL HIS ENEMIES BE MADE A FOOTSTOOL FOR HIS FEET.
Hebrews10:14 For by one offering He has perfected for all time those who are sanctified.
Hebrews10:15 And the Holy Spirit also testifies to us; for after saying
Hebrews10:16 THIS IS THE COVENANT THAT I WILL MAKE WITH THEM AFTER THOSE DAYS
Hebrews10:17 AND THEIR SINS AND THEIR LAWLESS DEEDS I WILL REMEMBER NO MORE.
Hebrews10:18 Now where there is forgiveness of these things
Hebrews10:19 Therefore
Hebrews10:20 by a new and living way which He inaugurated for us through the veil
Hebrews10:21 and since we have a great priest over the house of God
Hebrews10:22 let us draw near with a sincere heart in full assurance of faith
Hebrews10:23 Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering
Hebrews10:24 and let us consider how to stimulate one another to love and good deeds
Hebrews10:25 not forsaking our own assembling together
Hebrews10:26 For if we go on sinning willfully after receiving the knowledge of the truth
Hebrews10:27 but a terrifying expectation of judgment and THE FURY OF A FIRE WHICH WILL CONSUME THE ADVERSARIES.
Hebrews10:28 Anyone who has set aside the Law of Moses dies without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
Hebrews10:29 How much severer punishment do you think he will deserve who has trampled under foot the Son of God
Hebrews10:30 For we know Him who said
Hebrews10:31 It is a terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
Hebrews10:32 But remember the former days
Hebrews10:33 partly by being made a public spectacle through reproaches and tribulations
Hebrews10:34 For you showed sympathy to the prisoners and accepted joyfully the seizure of your property
Hebrews10:35 Therefore
Hebrews10:36 For you have need of endurance
Hebrews10:37 FOR YET IN A VERY LITTLE WHILE
Hebrews10:38 BUT MY RIGHTEOUS ONE SHALL LIVE BY FAITH; AND IF HE SHRINKS BACK
Hebrews10:39 But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction
Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 For by it the men of old gained approval.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the worlds were prepared by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered to God a better sacrifice than Cain
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken up so that he would not see death; AND HE WAS NOT FOUND BECAUSE GOD TOOK HIM UP; for he obtained the witness that before his being taken up he was pleasing to God.
Hebrews11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please Him
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:9 By faith he lived as an alien in the land of promise
Hebrews11:10 for he was looking for the city which has foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith even Sarah herself received ability to conceive
Hebrews11:12 Therefore there was born even of one man
Hebrews11:13 All these died in faith
Hebrews11:14 For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a country of their own.
Hebrews11:15 And indeed if they had been thinking of that country from which they went out
Hebrews11:16 But as it is
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 it was he to whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 He considered that God is able to raise people even from the dead
Hebrews11:20 By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to endure ill-treatment with the people of God than to enjoy the passing pleasures of sin
Hebrews11:26 considering the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he was looking to the reward.
Hebrews11:27 By faith he left Egypt
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of the blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith they passed through the Red Sea as though they were passing through dry land; and the Egyptians
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they had been encircled for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith Rahab the harlot did not perish along with those who were disobedient
Hebrews11:32 And what more shall I say? For time will fail me if I tell of Gideon
Hebrews11:33 who by faith conquered kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 quenched the power of fire
Hebrews11:35 Women received back their dead by resurrection; and others were tortured
Hebrews11:36 and others experienced mockings and scourgings
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 (men of whom the world was not worthy)
Hebrews11:39 And all these
Hebrews11:40 because God had provided something better for us
Hebrews12:1 Therefore
Hebrews12:2 fixing our eyes on Jesus
Hebrews12:3 For consider Him who has endured such hostility by sinners against Himself
Hebrews12:4 You have not yet resisted to the point of shedding blood in your striving against sin;
Hebrews12:5 and you have forgotten the exhortation which is addressed to you as sons
Hebrews12:6 FOR THOSE WHOM THE LORD LOVES HE DISCIPLINES
Hebrews12:7 It is for discipline that you endure; God deals with you as with sons; for what son is there whom his father does not discipline?
Hebrews12:8 But if you are without discipline
Hebrews12:9 Furthermore
Hebrews12:10 For they disciplined us for a short time as seemed best to them
Hebrews12:11 All discipline for the moment seems not to be joyful
Hebrews12:12 Therefore
Hebrews12:13 and make straight paths for your feet
Hebrews12:14 Pursue peace with all men
Hebrews12:15 See to it that no one comes short of the grace of God; that no root of bitterness springing up causes trouble
Hebrews12:16 that there be no immoral or godless person like Esau
Hebrews12:17 For you know that even afterwards
Hebrews12:18 For you have not come to a mountain that can be touched and to a blazing fire
Hebrews12:19 and to the blast of a trumpet and the sound of words which sound was such that those who heard begged that no further word be spoken to them.
Hebrews12:20 For they could not bear the command
Hebrews12:21 And so terrible was the sight
Hebrews12:22 But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God
Hebrews12:23 to the general assembly and church of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven
Hebrews12:24 and to Jesus
Hebrews12:25 See to it that you do not refuse Him who is speaking. For if those did not escape when they refused him who warned them on earth
Hebrews12:26 And His voice shook the earth then
Hebrews12:27 This expression
Hebrews12:28 Therefore
Hebrews12:29 for our God is a consuming fire.
Hebrews13:1 Let love of the brethren continue.
Hebrews13:2 Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers
Hebrews13:3 Remember the prisoners
Hebrews13:4 Marriage is to be held in honor among all
Hebrews13:5 Make sure that your character is free from the love of money
Hebrews13:6 so that we confidently say
Hebrews13:7 Remember those who led you

Hebrews13:9 Do not be carried away by varied and strange teachings; for it is good for the heart to be strengthened by grace
